Summary: Generating code for a model that contains a Bus Selector block operating on a nonvirtual bus containing just one bus element can result in an error
Description: Generating code for a model when both  of these conditions exist may throw an error: The model has a Bus Selector block. The input bus to this Bus Selector is composed of just one bus element.   The reported error may be of the form:  Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_append.cpp line 461: CG::StructType::isa(CG::Type::cast(getInputType(0))) ". Please report this to The MathWorks if you can cause it to recur.
Workaround: Use one of these workarounds:Use the single bus element/signal by itself, without packing it in a bus. Add another signal (for example, dummySignal) to the same bus, so that this bus is composed of more than one element/signal.

Summary: Disabling the link to a library Stateflow chart causes an error under certain circumstances

Summary: Creating a protected model fails for Lookup Table (n-D) blocks
Description: When you use Simulink.ModelReference.protect for a model that has a Lookup Table (n-D) block, you can get an error. The error might look something like this: ??? Error using ==> tlc_c at 138Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: .\lookup\cg_lookupnd.cpp line58: fMultiplePrevIndices
Products: Simulink Coder, Target Support Package, Embedded IDE Link, Stateflow Coder

Summary: MSVC target build uses the Microsoft Visual Studio 2008 compiler when the selected default MEX compiler is Microsoft Visual Studio 2003
Description: Model builds use the incorrect version of the Microsoft Visual Studio compiler if both of the following conditions exist: The model is configured to generate code for an MSVC target. For example, you selected system target file grt.tlc  with template makefile grt_msvc.tmf . You selected Microsoft Visual Studio 2003 as the default MEX compiler using the command mex -setup .  Under these conditions, the build uses Microsoft Visual Studio 2008 instead of Microsoft Visual Studio 2003, and displays messages including the following:  Setting environment for using Microsoft Visual Studio 2008
Workaround: To work around this bug, download the attached .zip file and extract its contents to your MATLAB installation tree, using the following steps:On your system, log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation root folder by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the .zip file attached to this Bug Report and place the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Examine the contents of the .zip file and, for each file, rename or make a backup copy of the equivalent file in your MATLAB installation tree. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/attachment_686241_9b_win32_2010-11-09.zipExtract the files from the .zip file. Windows systems:Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the .zip file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):cd <MATLAB>unzip attachment_686241_9b_win32_2010-11-09.zipDelete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Complicated single-line statements may crash on 64-bit platforms.
Description: Complicated statements using complex arithmetic operations (e.g. power) on a single line can cause MATLAB to crash on 64-bit systems. One example of such a statement is shown in the example below:   function v = test(x,y)     v = [x^2; x^2; x^2; x^2; y(1)*y(1)^0.5];   >> test(1,1)
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds.1. Break up complicated expressions into simpler ones on separate lines.  The example above can be rewritten as,function v = test(x,y)  a = x^2;  b = x^2;  c = x^2;  d = x^2;  e = y(1)*y(1)^0.5;  v = [a; b; c; d; e];2. Disable the JIT using "feature jit off".

Summary: "C280x Peripheral Interrupt Vector Values" table in the "C280x/C2802x/C2803x/C28x3x Hardware Interrupt" help topic contains errors.
Description: "C280x Peripheral Interrupt Vector Values" table in the "C280x/C2802x/C2803x/C28x3x Hardware Interrupt" help topic contains errors. It maps the following peripherals to the wrong PIE interrupts. I2CINT1A (I2C-A) uses INT8.y and INTx.2 I2CINT2A (I2C-A) uses INT8.y and INTx.1  Furthermore, the information in this table is not valid for other processors.
Workaround: Table 6-4. 280x, 2801x PIE MUXed Peripheral Interrupt Vector Table, located in TI Literature number SPRU712, gives the following correct mappings:I2CINT1A (I2C-A) uses INT8.y and INTx.1I2CINT2A (I2C-A) uses INT8.y and INTx.2For other processors, visit http://www.ti.com and search for the literature numbers provided below:For 280x and 28044 processors, see the TI literature number SPRU712For C2833x processors, see the TI literature number SPRUFB0 and SPRS439For C2802x processors, see the TI literature number SPRUFN3For C2803x processors, see the TI literature number SPRUGL8

Summary: MATLAB crashes leaving an empty log file while using serial ports on 64-bit Linux
Description: MATLAB may crash when the following is performed on 64-bit Linux s=serial('/dev/ttyUSB0')fopen(s)  This issue is reproducible on Linux systems that have come out since R2009a was released.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks that use variable-size arrays with Data Type Override mode may cause errors
Description: Running Update Diagram may fail on a Simulink model that: Contains an Embedded MATLAB block that uses variable-size arrays Uses Data Type Override mode set to "Double" or "Single" in the Fixed-Point Tool  The bug causes either an assertion failure with the message "MClass of a CG_Node may not change", or a compilation error indicating that "Computed maximum size is not bounded".
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Change Data Type Override mode to "Use local settings" or "Off". To change these settings, select Tools > Fixed-Point > Fixed-Point Tool in the model window.Set correct types for inputs and parameters in the Embedded MATLAB Function block that actually carry double, single, or integer values (as opposed to fixed-point values). To change data types, select Tools > Edit Data/Ports in the Embedded MATLAB editor.

Summary: MDL file fails to open if missing a trailing new-line character
Description: Simulink creates MDL files with a new-line character at the end.  If this character is removed, Simulink cannot load the model properly. Some text editors or revision control systems may remove this trailing character and cause this problem. You do not see an error or warning, but the model does not display, and is "partially loaded" in memory.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, follow these steps: If the model is already partially loaded, close it using "close_system".Then open the MDL file in a text editor (such as the MATLAB Editor) and add an extra line-break at the end of the file.After this you can load the model in Simulink.

Summary: Starting MATLAB -nodesktop may occasionally crash on Linux systems.
Description: On rare occasions, starting MATLAB using the -nodesktop or -nojvm command line flag on Linux will crash with a stack trace including XOpenDisplay  and the system call getenv .

Summary: When using the C6747EVM ADC and DAC blocks, the audio stops after a while.
Description: When C6747EVM ADC/DAC blocks are used outside of a function call subsystem that is connected to a C6000 DSP/BIOS Task block, the audio may stop after a while. The duration until audio stops depends on the Sampling rate  parameter. The audio stoppage is a result of drift between the the audio clock and the CPU Timer period that triggers the base rate task.
Workaround: To install the patch:Download the attached patch installer, patch_installer.m, and the ZIP archive, patch_623216.zip.Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt: patch_installer('patch_623216.zip', 'install')Complete the installation by following the instructions on the MATLAB command line.Restart MATLAB.The attached patch modifies the following files in your MATLAB installation:<matlabroot>\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\blks\lct\src\c6747evm_aic31.tci<matlabroot>\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\blks\lct\src\c6747evm_aic31.c<matlabroot>\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\blks\masks\c6747evm_aic31_sampleRate.m<matlabroot>\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\messages\blocks\en\xlate

Summary: Print Details option may display error for subsystems with unconnected lines
Description: In the Simulink Editor, when you select File -> Print Details , Simulink might display an error message. If the model includes a subsystem with an an unconnected signal line, you get an error message similar to the following: In component System Snapshot : Attempt to reference field of non-structure array. In component Title Page : Attempt to reference field of non-structure array. In component Model Loop : Attempt to reference field of non-structure array. "ignore" contains no content and will not be inserted into document
Workaround: Close MATLAB.Make a backup copy of MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@csl_sys_snap/gr_getFileName.p, where MATLABROOT is the root of the MATLAB installation directory on your system.Download a copy of the version of gr_getFileName.p attached to this report.Replace MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@csl_sys_snap/gr_getFileName.p with the downloaded copy of gr_getFileName.p.

Summary: MATLAB can crash if you assign a listener object to an array that has unassigned elements.
Description: MATLAB can crash if you assign a listener object to an array that has any unassigned elements. For example, given the following class definition: classdef exampleclass < handle  events    e1  endend  This code can cause MATLAB to crash: c = exampleclass;listeners(2) = addlistener(c, 'e1', @(src, evnt) disp('listener')); Notice that listeners(1)  has not been assigned.
Workaround: Do not assign listener objects to an array position that leaves an unassigned postion in the array.

Summary: IPEXTRAFFIC demo hangs on Mac OS X 10.5 Systems
Description: When executing the IPEXTRAFFIC demo on a Mac OS X 10.5 system with MATLAB R2009a or later,  MATLAB becomes unresponsive.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation of model reference variants
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model under the following conditions: The model has a subsystem which contains more than one Model Variants block. The Model Variants blocks have a different number of inports or a different number of outports. For example, one Model Variants block has 4 inports and another has 5 inports.
Workaround: Encapsulate each Model Variants block within a virtual subsystem. For example, you can select each Model Variants block and place it inside a virtual subsystem with the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+G.

Summary: Signal with a long name or no label, when connected to an Outport block, can cause a crash
Description: A crash can occur when both  these situations occur: The Outport block has the Icon display  parameter set to either Signal name  or Port number and signal name . The signal entering the Outport block has a name longer than 66 characters or the signal has no label. (For example, you connect a Bus Selector signal to an Outport block and the signal has a long name (more than 66 characters) or no label.)
Workaround: You can prevent this issue by doing one of the following actions:Change the Icon display parameter of the Outport block to be Port number.Use a name with less than 66 characters for the signal entering the Outport block.

Summary: The block output display feature has issues in R2009b that compromise accuracy and may cause a crash on Linux
Description: When you are running a simulation, the block output display feature samples the simulation values every second (to reduce flicker). The displayed values are accurate. However, when you pause or stop the simulation, the displayed values shown are not for the most recent simulation time step. The displayed values correspond poorly with scopes or other means of debugging your Simulink model output. Also, if you pause or stop a simulation without having toggled a port, and then you enable block output display for a block, you see either xx.xx  or a stale simulation value. On Linux platforms, when you toggle on the block output display feature for the first time and get a signal reuse warning dialog, Simulink might crash after pressing OK.
Workaround: On Linux, to avoid crashing Simulink, before you toggle on any blocks in the model, minimize and then restore the model window.

Summary: emlc and emlmex generate incorrect MEX files with the Watcom compiler
Description: This bug occurs when you use the mex -setup  command to select the Watcom compiler. The bug is caused by an incompatibility between the Watcom and Microsoft Visual C/C++ compilers. MEX files generated by emlc  and emlmex  use a dynamic library called libemlrt.dll , which was compiled with Microsoft Visual C/C++. This incompatibility causes certain functions in libemlrt.dll  to compute wrong answers when called from a MEX file generated with Watcom.
Workaround: Replace the file emlrt.h, located in the subfolder extern\include of the MATLAB distribution, with the new version in the attachment to this bug report. The new version includes pragmas that fix the incompatibility between Watcom and Microsoft Visual C/C++ compilers:#ifdef __WATCOMC__#pragma aux emlrtIntegerCheckR2009b value [8087];#pragma aux emlrtNotNanCheckR2009b value [8087];#pragma aux emlrtNonNegativeCheckR2009b value [8087];#endif

Summary: Simulink HDL Coder fails to emit saturation logic for Sum block under certain conditions
Description: The default HDL implementation for the Sum block may not produce the correct output under certain conditions. If both the accumulator type and the output type are specified on the Sum block, and conversion from the accumulator type to the output type requires saturation, then Simulink HDL Coder may not produce correct HDL saturation code.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, change the output data type specification to "Inherit: Inherit via internal rule". Then, place a Data Type Conversion block after the output of the Sum block that converts to the desired type, using the same rounding and saturation settings as the Sum block.An alternative approach is to specify a different HDL implementation for the affected Sum block via a control file. This method does not require modifying the design, but does require the creation or modification of a control file. With the control file, specify the use of the 'hdldefaults.SumLinearHDLEmission' implementation. An example of such a specification is:c.forEach('model/DUT/Sum2',...    'built-in/Sum', {}, ...    'hdldefaults.SumLinearHDLEmission');

Summary: Failure when linking code generated for model reference variants
Description: When generating code for a model hierarchy that utilizes model reference variants with preprocessor conditionals, the link of the top-level application might fail if there is a submodel referenced from multiple variants. The link error states that functions corresponding to that submodel are missing.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Real-Time Workshop might generate invalid code for Selector block operating on n-dimensional signal
Description: Real-Time Workshop might generate invalid code for a model under the following conditions: A Selector block indexes into a n-dimensional signal The source of the n-dimensional signal is a virtual Bus Selector block The virtual bus signal source is an input port of an Enable and Triggered Subsystem block One or more bus signal elements connect directly to an output port of the Enabled and Triggered Subsystem block  The Inport block becomes a nonvirtual operation on the bus signal, if one of its elements connects directly to an Outport block. In the generated code, the bus signal is copied into an array for this nonvirtual operation. The offset into this array is miscalculated if a Selector block is operating on n-dimensional bus signal elements. Avoid connecting a signal directly from an Inport block to an Outport block in triggered subsystems. The nonvirtual copy operation might be expensive when only a subset of the bus signal connects to the Outport block. To avoid this issue, insert a Signal Conversion block in front of the Outport block.
Workaround: The attached MATLAB script finds any occurrence of a Selector block that generates invalid code and returns an array of block handles for those Selector blocks. The input argument is the model name. After running the script, use the hilite_system command to highlight the Selector block in the model diagram. The input argument to hilite_system is a block handle. See the following example:>> blkHdls = findIvalidSelectorBlocks('<model_name>')blkHdls =16.0010 17.0010>> hilite_system(blkHdls(1))If a model is not affected by this issue, this script returns an empty array. A workaround to this problem is to insert a Signal Conversion block at the first input port of the offending Selector block.

Summary: Environment Checker requirements incorrect for DSP/BIOS and EDMA3 Low Level Driver (LLD) for Texas Instruments C674x processors.
Description: The Environment Checker utility does not include the EDMA3 Low Level Driver (LLD) as a requirement for the TI C674x processors. If you enter the following MATLAB command: checkEnvSetup('ccs', 'c674x', 'list') The report does not  include the following text: 6. EDMA3 LLD (EDMA3 Low Level Driver) Your version :  Required version: 1.06.00 or later Required by : Target Support Package 4.1 Required for : Device drivers EDMA3LLD_BIOS5_INSTALLDIR="" Later, if EDMA3 LLD is missing or the environment variables are not configured correctly, building the target application returns the following error message: The call to ccslink_ert_make_rtw_hook, during the exit hook generated the following error:Error while creating the project.Caused by: Code generation cannot proceed because the directory token, $(EDMA3LLD_BIOS5_INSTALLDIR), in the following path string could not be replaced with a valid path: "$(EDMA3LLD_BIOS5_INSTALLDIR)\packages\ti\sdo\edma3\rm\lib\c6747\Release\edma3_rm_bios.lib". Create an environment variable called "EDMA3LLD_BIOS5_INSTALLDIR" that stores the desired path name.The build process will terminate as a result. The DSP/BIOS version required for C674x processors is 5.33.05. The environment checker incorrectly reports that the required DSP/BIOS version is 5.32.04.
Workaround: In Windows Environment Variables, create a System Variable for EDMA3LLD_BIOS5_INSTALLDIR.- For Variable name, enter EDMA3LLD_BIOS5_INSTALLDIR.- For Variable value, enter the path of the EDMA3 LLD folder. For example, if you used the default installation directory, set Variable value to C:\Program Files\Texas Instruments\edma3_lld_01_06_00_01.Verify that MATLAB can see the environment variable. Enter getenv('EDMA3LLD_BIOS5_INSTALLDIR') at the MATLAB prompt. MATLAB should reply with the correct Variable value.Make sure that you are using DSP/BIOS version 5.33.05 or higher.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Reusable subsystem with mask and mask parameters?
Description: Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code for a model under all of the following conditions: There are two atomic subsystems and one subsystem includes the other. For both subsystems, on the Subsystem block parameter dialog box, Real-Time Workshop system code  is set to Auto  or Reusable function . Both subsystems have a mask. Both subsystem masks specify a mask parameter of the same name. The lower subsystem uses a mask variable of the upper subsystem as a mask parameter input in an expression.  In the generated code, a local variable computing the mask parameter expression might use the same symbol as the input argument corresponding to the mask parameter.
Workaround: Use different mask parameter names for these two subsystems.

Summary: Report Generator may lose unsaved Microsoft Word documents
Description: When using Report Generator to generate a Word document, open instances of Microsoft Word (including those launched outside of the MATLAB environment) may close silently and lose any unsaved work.
Workaround: To correct this issue, apply the attached patch:1. Close MATLAB.2. From the MATLAB installation directory, navigate to toolbox\rptgen\rptgen.3. Rename docview.m to docview.m.bak.4. Copy the attached docview.m into the directory indicated above.5. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Folder removal errors might occur during diagram update or build that triggers automatic parallel model reference build
Description: In a parallel computing environment, initiating a diagram update or build for your model might produce temporary-folder removal errors if both of the following conditions exist: Your model contains a model reference hierarchy. Conditions allow referenced models to be built in parallel.  The parallel build displays error messages similar to the following: C:\Temp\tp46914731_cfd2_4d11_a9d7_be95f4f7e087\model\slprj\sim could not be removed.C:\Temp\tp46914731_cfd2_4d11_a9d7_be95f4f7e087\model\slprj could not be removed.C:\Temp\tp46914731_cfd2_4d11_a9d7_be95f4f7e087\model could not be removed.C:\Temp\tp46914731_cfd2_4d11_a9d7_be95f4f7e087 could not be removed.

Summary: PolySpace Excel macro crashes with Excel 2007: "Run-time error '-2147024809 (80070057)': This object has no title."?
Description: During creation of the Excel statistics summary, Excel stops and displays the following message:"Run-time error '-2147024809 (80070057)': This object has no title. " In Debug mode, Excel highlights a problem with the macro in the function AllCheckRepartitionGraph . The problem occurs specifically on the ChartTitle  property. This appears to be an Excel bug. The HasTitle  property is set to true , but the chart fails to accept the title. A workaround is to set the HasTitle  field of the chart to false , and then back to true  before changing the title. ?
Workaround: Note that following workaround is only available for release R2009a and R2009bTo workaround this issue under Linux: 1. Create a backup copy of the following file:$POLYSPACE_C/lib/PolySpace_Macros.xls for a Linux or Solaris machine 2. Download the attached file attachment_611152_client_R200<XX>_linux_2010-02-10.zip. 3. Browse to the Polyspace installation folder (/usr/local/Polyspace by default under Linux) 4. Unzip the file in this installation folder and confirm the replacementswhere <XX> is either 9a or 9bTo workaround this issue under Windows: 1. Create a backup copy of the following file:POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\lib\PolySpace_Macros.xls for a Windows machine 2. Download the attached file attachment_611152_client_R200<XX>_win_2010-02-10.zip. 3. Browse to the Polyspace installation folder (C:\Polyspace by default under Windows) 4. Unzip the file in this installation folder and confirm the replacementswhere <XX> is either 9a or 9b

Summary: Incorrect data saved when using cvsave after measuring coverage from different parts of the model.
Description: When you run two or more simulations that collect model coverage and you change the target system for coverage collection, you might encounter a problem saving coverage data: more coverage data is saved to file than is specified in the call to cvsave() . This problem can occur when you explicitly indicate the coverage data that should be saved using the syntax: CVSAVE(FILENAME, DATA1, DATA2, ...) Save the specified dataobjects, the tests that created them and the referenced model(s)structure in the text file FILENAME.CVT.  When this problem occurs and the data file is opened using cvload() , there will be one or more data objects loaded than the arguments passed to cvsave() . These objects are associated with a target system for coverage collection that is not related to any of the arguments of cvsave() .
Workaround: There is no workaround for this problem. You can avoid the problem by limiting coverage collection to a single system and only changing the target system for coverage collection after closing and reopening the model.

Summary: Incorrect function hint can be shown for some methods
Description: Function Hints displayed for methods of classes defined in MATLAB can be incorrect. Specifically, the Function Hint for a method can display the signature for the object constructor.

Summary: Lookup Table (n-D) block gives inaccurate interpolation result in some mixed floating-point cases
Description: The Lookup Table (n-D) block gives an inaccurate output result in R2009a for the following case: The Interpolation method  is set to Linear .The input and the fraction data type are double .The table type, the output type, and the intermediate type are all single .  Under these conditions, the fraction was cast to single .  The single  type was used for the product operation, which led to an inaccurate interpolation result. In R2010a, the double  type is used for the product operation, and the interpolation result is then cast to single .

Summary: linkage fails with large arrays on 64-bit platforms
Description: When running on a 64-bit operating system, if you pass a large array to the linkage  function, MATLAB(R) might crash. The resulting stack trace points to a failure in the linkagemex  function. This crash occurs if linkage  attempts to create too large an array. The limiting size is the largest array MATLAB supports on 32-bit operating systems.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier fails to generate results if the Inport or Outport blocks of the model specify bus objects and their name includes a space character.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier fails to generate results if the Inport or Outport blocks of the model specify bus objects and their name includes a space character.
Workaround: Remove the space characters from the names of the Inport or Outport blocks that specify bus objects.

Summary: Simulink can crash if the [Min, Max] range of a Data Store block doesn't match the [Min, Max] range of an associated signal object.
Description: When a Data Store block has an associated signal object, and the block and the object both specify a [Min, Max] range, the [Min, Max] ranges must be identical. If they are not, Simulink should report the error when you perform Update Diagram , but may crash instead.
Workaround: Correct the mismatch between the block and object [Min, Max] ranges in one of these ways:Make the ranges identical on the Data Store block and the associated signal object.Specifiy the desired [Min, Max] range only on the block, or only on the signal object.The correction is necessary in any case, and also avoids the bug that can cause the crash.

Summary: Simulink may stop abruptly when you update model containing Merge block in a loop that drives Outport block of nonvirtual subsystem
Description: Simulink may stop abruptly when you update a model under the following conditions: The model contains a Merge block inside a nonvirtual subsystem, for example, an Atomic Subsystem or a conditionally executed subsystem. The output of the Merge block directly drives an Outport block of the nonvirtual subsystem. The Merge block is connected in a loop. Typically this condition leads to an error. But when the above conditions are met, Simulink may exit abruptly before the error is thrown.
Workaround: You may be able to prevent the abrupt exit by inserting a Signal Conversion block between the output port of the Merge block and the input port of the Outport block. However, connecting a Merge block in a loop causes an error. Consult the documentation for the proper use of the Merge block.

Summary: Map frame and graticule do not match map limits at very large map scales
Description: Map frames and graticule lines are visibly inconsistent with each other and with the map limits on large scale maps. The limits of both the frame and graticule are too tight, but by different amounts. (The frame is pulled inward by 10^-3 degrees; the graticule is pulled inward by 10^-4 degrees.)
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the framem.m and gridm.m function files in your current installation with the updated versions in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following files:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/framem.mmatlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/gridm.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing framem.m file, adding "old" to the file extension: framem.m.oldCopy the versions of framem.m and gridm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Segmentation fault during code generation of a Selector block with two dimensional row vectors
Description: Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder might cause a segmentation fault during code generation of a model containing a Selector block where the data input and indexing vector are both two dimensional row vectors.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB might produce wrong answers when embedded hardware setting specifies integer size different from that of MATLAB host or test hardware.
Description: Embedded MATLAB might produce wrong answers during simulation and RTW code generation when calling library functions whose implementations rely on the C function frexp  in the following situations: In simulation, the integer size specified for the embedded hardware is different from the integer size of the MATLAB host. In RTW code generation without emulation hardware, the integer size specified for embedded hardware is different from the integer size of the MATLAB host. In RTW code generation with emulation hardware, the embedded hardware and emulation hardware settings specify different integer sizes.  The following library functions might be affected: betainc , colon , eps , expint , expm , filter , gemmainc , hist , ismember , intersect , issorted , log2 , log10 , median , nchoosek , quadgk , quad2d , rank , sort , sortrows , unique , randperm , setdiff , setxor , and union .

Summary: xPC Target file scopes configured to dynamically create file names might skip overwriting one of the dynamically created files
Description: xPC Target file scopes configured to dynamically create file names might skip overwriting one of the dynamically created files. When using this feature, you configure the file scope such that the amount of files required to hold the data is not enough. This configuration is intended to cause the file scopes to cycle through and overwrite existing file contents sequentially. With the currently described issue, the file scope might skip overwriting the contents of one of the dynamically created files. For example, if the files to be overwritten are sequential files from file_1.dat  to file_9.dat , this issue might skip overwriting the file file_2.dat  as the file scope cyles through and overwrites the other dynamically created files.  ?
Workaround: As a workaround, increase the number of dynamically created files so that there are enough files to hold the data without the need to cycle through and overwrite existing files.

Summary: Simulink may incorrectly throw an error if a Switch block has scalar nonvirtual bus input signals
Description: If a Switch block has a scalar nonvirtual bus as its input data signals, Simulink may incorrectly throw the following error: "When one of the inputs to the block '<Model Name>/<Switch block Name>' is a nonvirtual bus, all data port inputs must have the same dimensions."

Summary: analyze method of commscope.eyediagram may throw an error while calculating eye rise and fall times
Description: The analyze method of commscope.eyediagram object performs several eye diagram measurements, including the eye rise and fall times. If the analyze method cannot determine the eye levels, then the eye rise and fall time measurement throws the following error:  ??? Attempted to access crossingPdf(1,:); index out of bounds because size(crossingPdf)=[0,128]. Error in ==> calcEyeRiseFallTime>calcRiseFallTimes at 38crossTimesHigh = findCrossingTimes(crossingPdf(1,:), numCrossingPoints,... Error in ==> calcEyeRiseFallTime at 20[eyeRiseTime(1,1) eyeFallTime(1,1)] = ... Error in ==> commscope.eyemeasurements.analyze at 125calcEyeRiseFallTime(this, horHistRFRe, horHistRFIm, symbolsPerTrace, Fs); Error in ==> commscope.eyediagram.analyze at 85 this.Measurements.analyze(this);

Summary: When computing definite integrals such as int(x*cos(x), x, 0, pi/2), Symbolic Math Toolbox can return incorrect results.
Description: Symbolic Math Toolbox returns the following incorrect result:  syms x;int(x*cos(x), x, 0, pi/2)ans =pi/2 The correct result is pi/2-1.
Workaround: First, compute the indefinite integral:syms x;g = int(x*cos(x), x)g =cos(x) + x*sin(x)Verify that g is a continuous function. If g is a continuous function, compute the definite integral as follows:subs(g, x, sym(pi)/2) - subs(g, x, sym(0))

Summary: Unexpected termination or wrong sample time reported by rate transition diagnostic
Description: The rate transition diagnostic reports an incorrect sample time if the destination block of the rate transition is a subsystem. If, in addition, the following conditions apply, then Simulink terminates unexpectedly. The source block has multiple outputs. The output port number for the source block is larger than the number of destination output ports.
Workaround: To avoid an unexpected termination of Simulink, disable the diagnostic on the Solvers pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box by either:Turning on "Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer".Changing the "Tasking mode for periodic sample times" to "SingleTasking" and setting the "Single task rate transition" to "none".?

Summary: On the Windows? 7 platform, the From Audio Device and To Audio Device blocks throw a warning when running a Simulink model for certain audio devices?
Description: The From Audio Device and To Audio Device blocks throws a warning when a custom device is selected. The block switches selection to the default device, and continues running. The warning message received is as follows: Could not find the specified device (Line 1/2 (Windows DirectSound)) in the list of devices. Switching to the Default device. ?
Workaround: For R2009b on Windows? platforms, follow these steps to correct this issue:1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Download and replace the following files with those contained in the attached zip file:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\dspblks\dspblks\@dspdialog\@ToAudioDevice\ToAudioDevice.p$MATLABROOT\toolbox\dspblks\dspblks\@dspdialog\@FromAudioDevice\FromAudioDevice.p3. Restart MATLAB?.

Summary: Missing parts of fitted curve when using "Print to Figure" from Curve Fitting Tool (CFTOOL)
Description: When using "Print to Figure" from Curve Fitting Tool (CFTOOL), the plot of the fitted curve may be missing some points in the new figure. This typically occurs when the fitted curve has an asymptote and the fitted curve is complex (non-zero imaginary part) or NaN valued beyond the asymptote.
Workaround: In the directory MATLABROOT\toolbox\curvefit\cftoolgui\private, rename the file cfduplicatefigure.m to cfduplicatefigure.old.m.Download the file attachment_605391_20100111 that is listed in the Attachments section.Unzip the contents of this file, cfduplicatefigure.m. Replace your installed version of the file MATLABROOT\toolbox\curvefit\cftoolgui\private\cfduplicatefigure.m.Restart MATLAB.This workaround has been tested in the following releases: R2007a, R2007b, R2008a, R2008b, R2009a, R2009b.

Summary: Data type mismatch error related to S-functions and linked blocks
Description: An error can occur if all the following conditions exist: Data type definitions exist in the base workspace You use library blocks that rely on the definitions in a subsystem of a model Blocks are not resolved from the library before you perform a task requiring an update diagram action   If you perform the task a second time, that is, multiple update diagrams occur, you do not get the error  because the first update diagram action resolved the blocks.
Workaround: Before performing the task  (simulate, update diagram, generate code, and so on), which initiated the error, a second time, enter the following command, where model is the name of the model specified in the failed operation:find_system(model, 'FollowLinks', 'on', 'LookUnderMasks', 'all');?

Summary: Using MATLAB on 64 bit Windows 7 as an automation server might cause a crash.
Description: I have a model which contains a 'Model Reference' block. When I simulate the model via the external C interface while using MATLAB as an automation server, MATLAB crashes. Only happens on 64 bit Windows 7.

Summary: Possible link errors for model build that generates C++ code for RSim target with Save to workspace options selected
Description: Building a model might generate link errors if all of the following conditions exist: The System target file  selected for the model is rsim.tlc . The Language  selected for the model is C++ . You selected Save to workspace  options on the Data Import/Export  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.  Under these conditions, the build process might produce link errors similar to the following: model.h:166: error: previous declaration of 'DataMapInfo* rt_dataMapInfoPtr' with 'C++' linkagemodel.cpp:30: error: conflicts with new declaration with 'C' linkage
Workaround: To work around this bug, take one of the following actions:Change the Language value to C on the Real-Time Workshop pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.Clear all Save to workspace options on the Data Import/Export pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Assertion when generating code for exported runnables with outports that specify an initial condition
Description: The software may produce an assertion when you generate code for exported runnables and a function call subsystem inside the wrapper subsystem has an outport which: Is non-scalar Specifies an initial condition  The assertion may be similar to the following:   CGIR assertion in base_core/expr/ExprTools.cpp(662): 'false'  [0] libmwsimulink.so:assertion_func(void*, char const*, int, char const*)() + 67 bytes  [1] libmwcg_ir.so:CG::ExprTools::takeAddress(CG::NodeFactory*, CG::TypeFactory*, CG::Expr*, bool)() + 474 bytes  [2] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGTransformForAutosar::apply()() + 2298 bytes  [3] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWTransformForAutosar(RTWCG::RTWFcnConst*, RTWCG::RTWContext*, PtrSet*)() + 255 bytes  [4] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWFcnConst::OptimizeAndEmitCode(int, RTWCG::TLCOutPortInfo*, RTWCG::RTWCGVector&)() + 4370 bytes  [5] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGModule::OptimizeAndEmitCode(int, RTWCG::TLCOutPortInfo*)() + 133 bytes  [6] libmwsimulink.so:RTWCGCodeGeneration::optimizeAndEmitCode()() + 686 bytes  [7] libmwsimulink.so:createRTWCGCodeGenMap(slBlockDiagram_tag*)() + 1561 bytes  [8] libmwsimulink.so:DoRTWGen(RTWInfo*, mxArray_tag*, int, mxArray_tag**)() + 2323 bytes
Workaround: Specify the initial condition of the non-scalar, function call subsystem outports to be empty, that is, "[]".

Summary: FFT Analysis Tool does not display negative phases of computed harmonics when the Display style parameter is set to List
Description: The FFT Analysis Tool of Powergui displays zeros in place of the negative values of phase harmonics when the Display style  parameter is set to List .

Summary: The target PC reboots when you use the Open Watcom 1.7 compiler for xPC Target models that contain IRQ Source blocks
Description: The target PC reboots when you use the Open Watcom 1.7 compiler for xPC Target models that contain IRQ Source blocks. An incorrect compiler optimization level causes the target PC to reboot when the IRQ Source block services the first interrupt. This issue occurs most often for models that contain composite serial port blocks.
Workaround: As a workaround,Close MATLAB.Download the attached files xpc_watc.tmf and xpcert_wartc.tmf and place them in matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpcRestart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: The Deployment Tool fails to launch on systems that have the locale set to Turkish.
Description: When attempting to launch the Deployment Tool with the locale set to Turkish, the command appears to have no effect or an error is displayed.
Workaround: In MATLAB, run the matlabroot command to determine the location of your MATLAB installation.Download the attached .zip file in the location indicated by matlabroot.Run the command cd matlabroot.In MATLAB, run the command: unzip deploytool-turkish-9b.zipRestart MATLAB.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during 'Software Safety Analysis Level 3' with the message: '@FFT: variable <variable name> is already declared'.?
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on Ada code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: ***** Software Safety Analysis Level 3 - 1.1 (Interprocedural static verification) ...  ---------------------------------------------------------------------- @FFT: variable <variable name> is already declared. |An internal consistency problem has been detected. |In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information, |the computation has been halted. ...---------------------------------------------------------------------- FATAL ERROR while processing my_package.dfa exception Failure(Failure) raised.   Note that <variable name>  has nothing to do with cause of the stop.
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder: %POLYSPACE_ADA%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32Create backup copies of all files named: rte-fe1*.x86-mingw32.exe  rte-fef*.x86-mingw32.exeDownload the attached files:  602194_Windows_1.zip  602194_Windows_2.zipExtract the .exe files from zip archives and copy them to: %POLYSPACE_ADA%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder: $POLYSPACE_ADA/Verifier/bin/x86-linuxCreate backup copies of all files with the following prefixes: rte-fe1*.x86-linux  rte-fef*.x86-linuxDownload the attached files: 602194_Linux_1.zip 602194_Linux_2.zipExtract the files from the zip archives and copy them to: $POLYSPACE_ADA/Verifier/bin/x86-linux

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: data consistency issue for malloc-based targets with multiple model instances
Description: Generated code for malloc-based targets potentially can corrupt global pointer variables if you create two or more instances of a model in the same executable. Code is generated that might corrupt data if all of the following conditions are true: You generate code for a malloc-based target, either grt_malloc.tlc , or tornado.tlc  with Tornado Target  pane parameter Code Format  set to RealTimeMalloc .  You customized your code to create two or more instances of a model in the same executable.  You customized your code to preemptively schedule those model instances on a single processor or concurrently schedule the instances across multiple cores.  A parent subsystem in the model explicitly sets the subsystem parameter Real-Time Workshop system code  to Reusable function .  That parent subsystem has at least one child subsystem that cannot be parented by a reusable subsystem for one of the following reasons: The child subsystem is a function-call subsystem and is called by a wide (nonscalar) function call signal. The child subsystem is a function-call subsystem and is called by a noninlined S-function. The child subsystem contains a noninlined S-function.    In this modeling scenario, the build process generates global pointer variables that potentially can be corrupted when an instance of a model is preemptively or concurrently scheduled with other instances of the same model. Note:  The following modeling approaches are not affected   by this issue: Preemptively schedule rates within the same model instance (multitasking solver mode with rate monotonic scheduling). Use Model blocks to combine multiple instances of the same model into one model hierarchy and one generated executable. Use the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder target option Generate reusable code  and customize the target to create multiple instances of the same model.
Workaround: To work around this bug, open the parent subsystem in which the parameter Real-Time Workshop system code is explicitly set to Reusable function and change the setting to Auto.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB functions may incorrectly create shared utilities, resulting in uncompilable code.
Description: If you enable shared utilities and use parameters in Embedded MATLAB code in Simulink or Stateflow, your model might generate uncompilable code. When this bug occurs, the C compiler reports an error about undeclared identifiers.

Summary: Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block: Table as row matrix and column selection asserts for Accelerator modes and Real-Time Workshop code generation
Description: A model asserts during Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulation and Real-Time Workshop code generation when a Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block has the following parameter settings: Table  is a row matrix.Inputs select this object from table  is set to Column .
Workaround: Because Table is a row matrix, changing Inputs select this object from table to Element gives the same result and does not assert for simulation or code generation.
