Description: Compilation time for the following function in MATLAB R2009b:  function simplifiedA = [-1, -2,-2;1 2 2];b= [0;72];x0 = [10; 10; 10];    % Starting guess at the solution[x,fval] = fmincon(@myfun,x0,A,b)    function f = myfun(x)        f = -x(1) * x(2) * x(3);    endend is slower than in release R2009a.  The verbose compilation output will have the following: Processing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\shared\optimlib\mcc.encProcessing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\optim\mcc.encProcessing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\distcomp\mcc.encProcessing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\control\mcc.encProcessing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\shared\controllib\mcc.encProcessing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\signal\mcc.encProcessing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\ident\mcc.encProcessing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\mpc\mcc.encmcc.enc file not found for licensed toolbox file C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\sldo\sldo\slparamestim.mAll files in directory C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\sldo\sldo will be excluded.mcc.enc file not found for licensed toolbox file C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\rtw\targets\mpc555dk\rt\blockset\mfiles\getuniquesignalname.pAll files in directory C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\rtw\targets\mpc555dk\rt\blockset\mfiles will be excluded.Processing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\map\mcc.encProcessing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\shared\maputils\mcc.encProcessing C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\shared\mapgeodesy\mcc.enc
Workaround: Remove PCT from the compilation search path in the following manner: 1. If using deploytool, click Actions > Settings > Toolboxes on Path and uncheck Parallel Computing Toolbox.2. If using mcc, use the -N and -p options to include the toolboxes required.

Summary: Angular velocity of Screw Joint sensed with Joint Sensor always reported in rad/s regardless of units selected in Joint Sensor dialog box
Description: When you sense the angular velocity of a Screw Joint with a Joint Sensor, the Sensor reports the angular velocity in radians/second, irrespective of the units that you select in the Joint Sensor dialog box.
Workaround: Sense the angular velocity in rad/s and convert the output signal to degress/second using a Simulink Gain block, with the gain set to 180/pi.

Summary: Invalid code generated for model with reusable subsystem connected to root model inport
Description: The code generated for a model may be invalid and fail to compile if the following conditions are true: A root model inport is configured to implement an AUTOSAR Require  port with data access mode ImplicitReceive . This root model inport is connected to the inport of a subsystem that is configured to generate a reusable function.
Workaround: The following is a workaround:Place a Signal Conversion block between the root model inport and the subsystem configured to generate a reusable subsystem.Select the Signal Conversion block's option "Exclude this block from the 'Block reduction' optimization".

Summary: Cannot communicate with instruments using the VISA RSIB interface or use VISA SPOLL.
Description: The Instrument Control Toolbox will throw an error if you try to establish communication with an instrument using the VISA RSIB interface. >> v=visa('ni', 'RSIB::172.31.146.222::INSTR')  VISA-RSIB Object Using NI Adaptor : VISA-RSIB-172.31.146.222  Communication Address  RemoteHost: 172.31.146.222  Communication State  Status: closed  RecordStatus: off  Read/Write State   TransferStatus: idle  BytesAvailable: 0  ValuesReceived: 0  ValuesSent: 0  >> fopen(v)  ??? Error using ==> icinterface.fopen   ?
Workaround: To fix this issue in versions R2009a and R2009bBackup the following files if shipped with your platform:<MATLAB>/toolbox/instrument/instrumentadaptors/win32/*<MATLAB>/toolbox/instrument/instrumentadaptors/win64/*Save the attached file in a known location such as c:\tmp\G600790.zip In the MATLAB Command Window, type:>> cd(matlabroot) >> unzip('c:/tmp/G600790.zip')>> rehash path>> rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Incorrect angular velocity reported for planet gear in Planetary Gear
Description: If you select the Show planet connector port  check box in the Planetary Gear block and sense the planet gear velocity, the reported velocity is the same as that of the sun gear for all ratios of the ring to sun radii. To see an example, open and run the demo model drive_planetary_pic. If you sense the planet and sun gear angular velocities in this model, they are reported as the same for all ratios of the ring to sun gear radii. The visualization of the gear rotations also shows that the gear teeth intersect each other during the simulation, because the planet gear velocity is incorrect.
Workaround: Close all SimDriveline models.Make a backup copy of the SimDriveline library file ($MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/driveline.mdl).$MATLABROOT is the directory returned by the command matlabrootOpen the SimDriveline library by typing the command driveline at the MATLAB command line.Unlock the library for editing by selecting Unlock Library from the Edit menu.Open the Gears sublibrary.Right-click on the Planetary Gear block and select the Look Under Mask option. The implementation of the Planetary Gear becomes visible.Double-click on the Planet-Planet block and enter the following expression for the gear ratio: (Ratio-1)/2Double-click on the Ring-Planet block and enter the following expression for the gear ratio: 2*Ratio/(Ratio-1)Close the Planetary Gear block dialog boxes, the subsystem diagram, and the Gears sublibrary.Save the SimDriveline library driveline.mdl.Reopen and proceed with your SimDriveline models.

Summary: MAKEMID does not include certain child interface methods and properties
Description: When you install the AgilentRFSigGen driver version 1.3.3.0 and create a wrapper using MAKEMID, the generated mdd wrapper fails to include the Utility interface's methods and properties.
Workaround: For versions before R2010a, provide an explicit interface name when using you use MAKEMID. For example:makemid('AgilentRfSigGen.AgilentRfSigGen','ASigGenTest1.mdd','ivi-com','IAgilentRFSigGen')Or use a class compliant interface:makemid('AgilentRfSigGen.AgilentRfSigGen','ASigGenTest2.mdd','ivi-com','IIviRfSigGen')

Summary: A(p) = B may incorrectly assign arbitrary elements when A is sparse and p is an extremely large sparse logical matrix.
Description: A(p) = B  may incorrectly assign arbitrary elements into A  (instead of erroring, as was the case in previous versions of MATLAB?) when all of the following conditions are met: p  is a large sparse logical matrix, prod(size(p)) >= maxSize , where maxSize = 2^32  on a 32-bit machine and maxSize = 2^64  on 64-bit machine, nnz(p(maxSize:end)) > 0 .  An example of this behavior on a 32-bit machine is given by A = speye(10);p = sparse(2^26+1, 2^6, true);A(p) = 2 ?
Workaround: As a workaround for this bug, consider replacing A(p) = B with A(find(p)) = B.?

Summary: Generated code from Import Wizard gives wrong result for Excel files
Description: The Import Wizard generates incorrect code when importing Excel files under the following conditions:The imported worksheet contains at least two columns of data and header rows, like the following table.             text1 text2                  data1 data2                  1 2                  2 4                  3 6                  4 8        In the Import Wizard, you select "Create vectors from each column using column names" and "Generate MATLAB code".
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file by appending .old to the file name:   $MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\codetools\private\makeimportcode.m$MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root folder on your machine, as returned by entering   matlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.Download the attached file and place it in the folder mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: binornd gives incomplete results for some inputs of type single
Description: The binornd function can produce incomplete results when all of the following conditions are true: The N parameter is a scalar. Either the N parameter or the P parameter, or both, are of type single rather than double. The output is a non-scalar array. This will be true if P is a non-scalar array or the 3rd and following inputs specify a non-scalar size.  In this case the result will have a random binomial value in its first element and zeros in the remaining elements.
Workaround: Specify N as a non-scalar array. For example, instead ofp = single(.5);N = 10;x = binornd(N,p,[2 3])expand N to the size of the desired output:p = single(.5);N = repmat(10,[2 3]);x = binornd(N,p,[2 3])

Summary: Printing 2-D graphics from the Graphics window in MuPAD leads to an empty page.
Description: When you print a MuPAD 2-D plot from the Graphics window, the printed page is empty. 3-D graphics are printed correctly. This issue affects the separate MuPAD Graphics window only. Printing a 2-D plot from a MuPAD notebook itself does not print empty pages.

Summary: Macintosh? platform: Attempt to print from the MATLAB? Editor or Command Window fails and returns a Java NullPointerException
Description: Some Macintosh users report that when they attempt to print from the MATLAB Editor or the Command Window, no printout is sent to the printer. Instead, an Exception like the following appears in the Command Window: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException at com.mathworks.widgets.text.print.PrintSettings.getLastPrintRequestAttributes(PrintSettings.java:280) at com.mathworks.widgets.SyntaxTextPaneBase.printDocument(SyntaxTextPaneBase.java:2031) at com.mathworks.widgets.SyntaxTextPaneMultiView.printDocument(SyntaxTextPaneMultiView.java:750) at com.mathworks.mde.editor.EditorView.doPrint(EditorView.java:1284) at com.mathworks.mde.editor.EditorView.access$2300(EditorView.java:92) at com.mathworks.mde.editor.EditorView$8.update(EditorView.java:775) at com.mathworks.mde.editor.EditorAction$ObservableAction.actionPerformed(EditorAction.java:52) at javax.swing.AbstractButton.fireActionPerformed(AbstractButton.java:2028) at javax.swing.AbstractButton$Handler.actionPerformed(AbstractButton.java:2351) at javax.swing.DefaultButtonModel.fireActionPerformed(DefaultButtonModel.java:387) at javax.swing.DefaultButtonModel.setPressed(DefaultButtonModel.java:242) at javax.swing.AbstractButton.doClick(AbstractButton.java:389) at javax.swing.plaf.basic.BasicMenuItemUI.doClick(BasicMenuItemUI.java:1225) at com.apple.laf.AquaMenuItemUI.doClick(AquaMenuItemUI.java:120)
Workaround: For both MATLAB Version 7.8 (R2009a) and MATLAB Version 7.9 (R2009b) apply the attached patch, PrintSettings.class. This patch enables you to print, although the paper margins may be slightly larger than usual.To apply the patch:Determine your MATLAB root folder by typing matlabroot in the Command Window.Create patch/com/mathworks/widgets/text/print subfolders in /MATLABROOT/java.For instance, if the MATLAB root folder is /Applications/MATLAB_R2009a.app, type the following command in the MATLAB Command window:  mkdir /Applications/MATLAB_R2009a.app/java/patch/com/mathworks/widgets/text/printDownload the attached zip file and extract the enclosed PrintSettings.class file to the /print folder.For instance, set your current folder in MATLAB to the location where you extract PrintSettings.class, and then type the following command in the MATLAB Command window: copyfile('PrintSettings.class', '/Applications/MATLAB_R2009a.app/java/patch/com/mathworks/widgets/text/print')Restart MATLAB.

Summary: The C2808i2ctest and c28335i2ctest models in the "Using the I2C Bus to Access a Connected EEPROM" demo do not work.
Description: Completing the steps in the demo does not update the EEPROM_DATA values because interrupts are not enabled in the target preferences block.
Workaround: Enable the interrupts to read back the EEPROM_DATA data:In the demo model, open the F2808 eZdsp or F28335 eZdsp target preferences block.Select the Peripherals tab.Select the I2C peripheral.Select the following checkboxes:- Enable system interrupt- Enable SCD interrupt- Enable ARDY interruptClick OK and save the demo model.

Summary: The Bit Packing and Bit Unpacking blocks do not work properly with bit positions greater than 1023
Description: The Bit Packing and Bit Unpacking Bit patterns  parameter selects bits from either the input stream (pack) or the output stream (unpack). If you enter a bit pattern value greater than 1023, the block uses the wrong bit.
Workaround: As a workaround, use the attached xpcbitpacking.c file. This file contains an update for the Bit Packing and Unpacking blocks.Close MATLAB.Download the attached file xpcbitpacking.c and save it to:matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\Change folder to this directory.Compile and link the replacement S-function with the mex command: mex xpcbitpacking.cRestart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Cannot export Function-Call Subsystem or Triggered Subsystem from a model that uses simplified initialization mode
Description: If the parameter 'UnderspecifiedInitializationDetection' is set to 'Simplified', the following error message appears when you export a Function-Call Subsystem from the model: "The parameter 'Source of initial output value:' of Outport block 'rtwdemo_subsystem/__ExpFcn__1/Out1' must be set to 'Input signal' because a function-call trigger signal from output port 1 of block 'rtwdemo_subsystem/__ExpFcn__1/expFcn' is passing through the Outport block." Additionally, in R2009b, the following error message appears when you export a Triggered Subsystem from the model: "The parameter 'Source of initial output value:' of Outport block 'Subsystem_expfcn/Subsystem/__ConvTrig2FcnCallFcnCall__2/sys/Out1' must be set to 'Input signal' because a function-call trigger signal from output port 1 of block 'Subsystem_expfcn/Subsystem/__ConvTrig2FcnCallFcnCall__2/sys/expFcn' is passing through the Outport block."
Workaround: Locate the folder where the MATLAB software is installed using the command matlabroot.Replace the file matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/private/slbus.p with the attached patch file associated with your version of MATLAB.For R2009b, also replace the file matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/private/slPrepMdlForExportFcn.p with the attached patch file.

Summary: When MacOS 10.6 (Snow Leopard) is booted into 64-bit kernel mode, MuPAD does not start.
Description: When you run MacOS 10.6 (Snow Leopard) in 64-bit kernel mode, MuPAD does not start. Note that Apple does not recommend booting the kernel into 64-bit mode.
Workaround: If you use Symbolic Math Toolbox version 5.4 (release 2009b), follow these steps:Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report. Save the file in the <MATLAB>/matlab/toolbox/symbolic/mupad/share/bin folder.Open the <MATLAB>/matlab/toolbox/symbolic/mupad/share/bin folder and double-click the .zip file. This will replace the file muparch.Restart MATLAB.Type mupad at the MATLAB command prompt.There is no workaround for the version 5.3 (release 2009a) or earlier versions.

Summary: Running Report Generator from PolySpace Viewer can cause the following error message: "File not found: <path>\.rte\?extern.ci"
Description: When you select "Run Report...:" to generate a report from PolySpace Viewer, you may receive the following error message:File not found: <pathTo>\.rte\?extern.ci ' where <pathTo>  corresponds to your user account profile location. This occurs only with verifications that contain extern variables. The error has no impact on verification results.
Workaround: Click "OK" to continue report generation.Note: This error message appears only once during each Viewer session.

Summary: Custom C code that you specify to emlc for inclusion in generated source or header files is truncated at 1024 characters.
Description: Custom C code that you specify to emlc  in the CustomSourceCode  or CustomHeaderCode  fields of the MEXConfig  or RTWConfig  objects for inclusion in generated source or header files is truncated at 1024 characters.
Workaround: Instead of specifying the custom code directly in configuration objects, put the custom C code in a separate header file and use an include statement that names that header file as the custom code.

Summary: Simulation of Stateflow truth tables can result in the editor refusing to maximize
Description: The Stateflow Editor fails to open correctly if the following conditions are satisfied: Add two or more Stateflow truth tables to a model Close the Stateflow Editor Simulate the model (for example, from Simulink) Open the Stateflow Editor  The Stateflow Editor opens entirely off the screen on Windows. On Linux, it opens minimized in the top-right corner of the screen.
Workaround: Save your model, close it, then re-open it. Now when you open the Stateflow Editor, it appears on screen but minimized. So you need to resize it.

Summary: Code generation error for S-function TLC code, which calls both Outputs and BlockOutputSignal
Description: Real-Time Workshop software might call both TLC functions, Outputs  and BlockOutputSignal , of your S-function under the following conditions: You configured your S-function to allow generating an expression On the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, Eliminate superfluous local variables (Expressions folding)  is selected Code generated from your S-function is inlined into a Stateflow function  The code generation error might occur if your S-function TLC code has a global TLC variable shared between Outputs  and BlockOutputSignal . This behavior is inconsistent with the documented behavior.

Summary: Normal mode simulation results differ from SIL simulation results for model with AUTOSAR target
Description: If a model specifies the AUTOSAR target, and has a non-scalar root-level inport that is configured to be an ImplicitReceive  port, then SIL simulation results and normal mode simulation results may differ.

Summary: Incorrect results when using north-to-south columns in colorm, filterm, los2, ltln2val, mapprofile, and viewshed
Description: In R2009b (Mapping Toolbox Version 3.0) several functions that previously supported only data grids referenced to latitude-longitude with 1-by-3 referencing vectors gained the capability to work also with 3-by-2 referencing matrices. In addition to supporting columns that run south-to-north, referencing matrices allow you to specify columns that run north-to-south (common for spatially-referenced images), as well as rows that run east-to-west rather than west-to-east (uncommon, but possible with GeoTIFF or worldfile input). However, the update to function ltln2val  was not sufficiently general, and in R2009b ltln2val  and client functions colorm , filterm , los2 , mapprofile , and viewshed  will output incorrect numerical values when both of the following are true (a) columns run north-to-south (and/or rows run east-to-west) and (b) the interpolation method is 'bilinear'  or 'bicubic' . This happens even though no warnings or errors are issued. To clarify, there is no problem with the 'nearest'  interpolation method, and because north-to-south columns (and east-to-west rows) are not possible with referencing vectors, older code that used them should be unaffected. However, the problem is serious and users are encouraged to apply the following workaround (or the more general workaround provided in Bug Report 581492 ) as quickly as possible.
Workaround: The best way to work around this problem is to apply the multi-file workaround provided in Bug Report 581492. That workaround includes an updated version of ltln2val.m that works properly with north-to-south columns (and east-to-west rows) and is also somewhat more efficient than the version provided with R2009b.Or, to be slightly more expedient, you can replace the ltln2val.mfunction file in your current installation with the version in the attachment for this bug report, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/ltln2val.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: ltln2val.m.oldCopy the version of ltln2val.m from the workaround into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.The version of ltln2val.m provided with this Bug Report and the one provided with Bug Report 581492 are exactly the same, and either one will completely resolve the numerical problems described in this report. The difference is that the other workaround requires you to replace additional files in your toolbox folders, but it also substantially improves performance, especially for viewshed.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Model reference with bus at root outport might cause incorrect simulation and code generation result
Description: Referenced models might produce incorrect simulation and code generation results under the following conditions: The Outport block of a conditional subsystem or function call subsystem connects to a input of a Bus Creator block The Bus Creator block connects to the model's root Outport block, which has a bus object  ? See the attached example model, mRootOutLocalBug.mdl , which references a model that meets the above conditions. The Real-Time Workshop generated code does not produce the same result as simulation.
Workaround: In the parent model, mark the model block's output port as a testpoint.You can fix the the attached example model, mRootOutLocalBug.mdl, using this workaround. See mFix.mdl, for details.

Summary: Code generated for an integer parameter whose CSC's DataInit property is set to Macro may cause arithmatic overflow
Description: When a parameter of a fixed-point or built-in integer data type uses a Custom Storage Class whose DataInit  property is set to Macro , C compiler may treat the value of the parameter as int or unsigned int , which may not match the data type specified in the model. Such data type mismatch can cause arithmatic operations to yield incorrect results. For example, unsigned short  in a model may become unsigned int  in C code. The data type promotion that should convert unsigned short  to signed int  then does not occur, causing signed int  values to be wrongly treated as unsigned . Consequently, integer overflow can occur when a negative int  value is wrongly interpreted as a large unsigned int  value.
Workaround: If you have your own TLC file for the CSC whose DataInit property is set to Macro, generate a data type cast to explicitly convert integers of mismatched type to the correct integer data type used in model. Perform the cast either in a #define statement or in the expression where the variable is referenced.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For models that pass reusable subsystem outputs as individual arguments
Description: Real-Time Workshop software might generate invalid code for a model containing reusable subsystems under all of the following conditions: On the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Individual arguments . The reusable Subsystem blocks connect to a Rate Transition block.  ?
Workaround: On the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the parameter Pass reusable subsystem outputs as to Structure reference, which is the default value.

Summary: Flash Loader/Flash Programmer utility for C2000 processors does not work
Description: This bug consists of the following four issues: The enableQuestion_Flash.fig  file is corrupt, preventing the flash warning message dialog from opening. This failure halts code generation with the flash loader and stops the entire flash programming procedure. The C2808pmsmfocflash  and c28335pmsmfocflash  demos give annoying popup warnings blocking the code generation process. After launching code generation on the demo, the pop up warning window appears multiple times stating "Unconfigured FLASH memory section ". The Execute  button on the Flash_loader pane under the Peripherals tab of a C2000 target preferences block doesn't work. Regardless of how you set Enable Flash Programmer  parameter, clicking Execute  produces the following error message: Undefined variable "prop" or class "prop.Enable_Flash.ui" . Flash API browse button does not work. After you click "Apply", the API goes back to the default one, unless before clicking "Apply", you manually edit the input area, e.g., type an extra space to the end or cut and paste the entire content. F28027 flash programmer does not work.  ?
Workaround: The patch is created for R2009b release.To install the patch, download the attached files, patch_installer.m and patch_flashprogrammer.zip.Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt:patch_installer('patch_flashprogrammer.zip', 'install')Complete the installation by following the instructions on the MATLAB command line.Remove the following file:toolbox\idelink\foundation\pjtgenerator\tgtpref2\+targetpref\@Controller\Controller.pReboot MATLAB.The attached patch files modify the following files in your MATLAB installation:toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+targetprefext\@Peripheral\Flash_loaderAPIBrowse_UICallback.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+FlashProgrammer\checkFlashValidation.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\utils\enableQuestion_Flash.figtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+targetprefext\@Peripheral\Flash_loaderExecute_UICallback.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\messages\codegen\en\xlatetoolbox\idelink\foundation\pjtgenerator\tgtpref2\@targetprefu\@View\getPeripheralWidgetFor.mtoolbox\idelink\foundation\pjtgenerator\tgtpref2\+targetpref\@Controller\Controller.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+FlashProgrammer\flashDeviceMemMap.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+FlashProgrammer\runPC.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+FlashProgrammer\getHardCode.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+FlashProgrammer\getEntrypoint.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+FlashProgrammer\getAPILocation.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+FlashProgrammer\eraseValidation.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+FlashProgrammer\checkSupportedChip.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\+tic2000\+FlashProgrammer\checkFlashAPI.mtoolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic2000\blks\c2000tgtpreflib.mdl?

Summary: deploytool might fail to build a project if mcc throws a first-chance exception
Description: When deploytool  builds a project, it invokes mcc  to build. If for some reason mcc  throws a first-chance exception, deploytool  assumes the build is finished and terminates the mcc  process, which results in an unfinished build. The problem often manifests itself with the following symptoms: The build fails because deploytool  failed to copy files from the project's intermediate  folder to the output  folder, no other build errors occured before this. There are no other files generated by mcc  other than mccExcludedFiles.log  in the project's intermediate  folder.
Workaround: Build the project with the following mcc command:mcc -F project_nameThe location of the project file can be found in deploytool's Settings> General tab.Note: The output will be placed in the intermediate directory and not the output directory.

Summary: Incorrect CPU clock speed setting in ADSP-BF537 EZ-KIT Lite Target Preferences block causes timing errors
Description: The CPU Clock parameter in the ADSP-BF537 EZ-KIT Lite target preferences block is set to 600 MHz. When you reset the processor, its CPU clock rate defaults to 250 MHz. This mismatch causes timing errors in your generated code.
Workaround: To get the correct timing, perform one of the following tasks:In your model, configure the CPU Clock parameter in the ADSP-BF537 EZ-KIT Lite target preferences block to match the frequency of the processor (250 MHz).After resetting the processor, change the CPU clock rate of the processor to match the CPU Clock parameter in the target preferences block.

Summary: From Multimedia File block can error out while generating code, or while running in accelerator mode.?
Description: While running a Simulink model containing the From Multimedia File block in accelerator mode, the following error message was generated: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR:.\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_prop.cpp line 1489: propType.isPointer()". Please report this to The MathWorks if you can cause it to recur. ? ?
Workaround: For R2009b on Windows or 64-bit Linux, follow these steps to correct this issue:1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Replace the S-Function in the following folder with the one in the attached zip file: $MATLABROOT/toolbox/dspblks/dspmex/3. Restart MATLAB?.If you are using a different platform or a different version of MATLAB, please contact The MathWorks to obtain a new S-Function.?

Summary: PolySpace C++ verification stops during 'compilation checking' with the message: 'Error: integer overflow in internal computation due to size or complexity'
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on C++ code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: Verifying C++ sources ...Verifying MyClass.cppError: integer overflow in internal computation due to size or complexity of "MyClass"  This is an internal error related to the size of a variable member or to a virtual inheritence of 'MyClass '.
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32Create backup copies of the following three files: edgcpfe.gnu.x86-mingw32.exeedgcpfe.microsoft.x86-mingw32.exeedgcpfe.x86-mingw32.exeDownload the three attached Windows files:596968_Windows_1.zip596968_Windows_2.zip596968_Windows_3.zipExtract the .exe files from the three zip files and copy them to:%POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder:$POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/x86-linuxCreate backup copies of the following three files:edgcpfe.gnu.x86-linuxedgcpfe.microsoft.x86-linuxedgcpfe.x86-linuxDownload the three attached Linux files:596968_Linux_1.zip596968_Linux_2.zip596968_Linux_3.zipExtract the edgcpfe.*x86-linux files from the three zip files, and copy them to$POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/x86-linuxTo workaround this issue on a Solaris system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/sparc-solarisCreate backup copies of the following three files: edgcpfe.gnu.sparc-solarisedgcpfe.microsoft.sparc-solarisedgcpfe.sparc-solarisDownload the attached file: 596968_Solaris.zipExtract the three edgcpfe.*sparc-solaris files from the zip file and copy them to:$POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/sparc-solaris

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Answer: Under certain circumstances, code generated from Stateflow charts containing Simulink functions might be wrong
Description: Under the following conditions, incorrect simulation and Real-Time Workshop code generation for a Stateflow chart might occur: The chart resides in a library model and is being used inside another model for which code is being generated. The chart contains a Simulink function-call subsystem. The chart has input events.
Workaround: NOTE: The following workaround requires access to the MATLAB installation area.Find out the MATLAB version you are using. You can find this out using the following command on the MATLAB prompt:versionDepending on the version of MATLAB you are using, download one of the attached files: autobuild_driver_8b.m, autobuild_driver_9a.m, autobuild_driver_9b.mRename this file to autobuild_driver.mOn the MATLAB prompt, type:which private/autobuild_driver.mReplace this file with the attached file.Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Real-Time Workshop generated C code for Unary Minus or Switch block might not compile
Description: Real-Time Workshop C code generation of the Unary Minus or Switch block might produce uncompilable code. The generated code might have an empty condition and an uncompilable assignment statement, similar to the following code pattern: if (rtb_Input) {} else {= rtb_Input;}  If the Unary Minus or Switch block connects to an S-Function block, the probability of this error increases.
Workaround: Do either of the following:Turn off expression folding for the model by clearing the Eliminate superfluous temporary variables (Expression folding) parameter on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.Place a Signal Conversion block at the outport of the Unary Minus or Switch block and select the Signal Conversion block parameter Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: LMS Filter block generates invalid code when Products & quotient data types have different fraction lengths
Description: The LMS Filter block generates invalid code under the following conditions: The block input is fixed point. The Products and quotient  data type is set to Binary point scaling  or Slope and bias scaling . The fraction lengths of W'u , mu*e  and Q*e  are different.
Workaround: There are two ways to workaround this issue in R2009b:If the Products and quotient data type is set to Binary point scaling or Slope and bias scaling, ensure the fraction lengths of W'u, mu*e and Q*e are identical.If you do not need to specify fraction lengths for W'u, mu*e and Q*e, set the Products and quotient data type to Same as first input.

Summary: Error when interacting with Simulink models and missing paths after invoking 'restoredefaultpath'.
Description: Invoking the command 'restoredefaultpath' on a system that has Embedded IDE Link installed results in the following behavior: If you open a Simulink model and click on the word 'Tools' in the menu bar, the MATLAB software throws the error:    ??? Undefined function or method 'util_isMWSoftwareInstalled' for   input arguments of type 'char'.  Some Embedded IDE Link functionalities do not work properly because of missing paths. The missing paths are:    [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\idelink\foundation\pjtgenerator   [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\idelink\foundation\pjtgenerator\hookpoints   [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\idelink\foundation\autointerface\util   [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\ccslinkblks
Workaround: Download and unzip the attached file.Copy the file, g596680_9b_patch.exe, to your MATLAB root.Double-click the file.In the dialog that opens, click Unzip. You should get the message "2 file(s) unzipped successully."

Summary: Toggling block reduction setting changes numeric behavior of model with Saturation block
Description: The Saturation block gives the wrong numerical results for simulation and code generation when the following conditions are true: Block reduction is on. The upper limit of the Saturation block is greater than the maximum value that the input data type can represent. The lower limit of the Saturation block is less than the minimum value that the input data type can represent. The input and output of the Saturation block have different data types.

Summary: ncfpdf gives the wrong answer at X=0 for degrees of freedom NU1=2
Description: The result from ncfpdf  is incorrect when the input X  has the value 0, and NU1  has the value 2. It is correct for other inputs. For example, the first output from the following command is incorrect:  ncfpdf([0 .1 .2 .3],2,10,1)ans =    3.0327    0.5708    0.5366    0.5039
Workaround: This specific combination of inputs is a special case that is computed differently from other values, but it is not computed correctly. Typically we would want the value of the density at zero to be the limit of the density as it approaches zero from positive values. One workaround is to compute the density at a value slightly larger than zero:delta = 1;ncfpdf(eps,2,10,delta)ans =    0.6065An alternative is to compute the value directly as a function of the noncentrality parameter:exp(-delta/2)ans =    0.6065

Summary: xPC, MPC5xx, C166, and C2000 targets: MATLAB session might crash during external mode simulation when uploading signals from Scope blocks with triggered sample time
Description: During an external mode simulation, your MATLAB session might crash if both of the following conditions are true: Your target application runs on an xPC, Freescale MPC5xx, Infineon C166, or Texas Instruments C2000 target. Your target application contains a Scope block with triggered sample time from which signals are uploaded to the Simulink host system.
Workaround: To work around this bug, when running in external mode, avoid uploading signals from a Scope block with triggered sample time. You can do this in either of the following ways:Relocate the Scope block in the model so that it does not have a triggered sample time.Go to the External Mode Control Panel, open the External Signal & Triggering dialog box, and clear the Scope block with triggered sample time from the list of signals to be collected from the target system.

Summary: Loss of precision when manipulating  '&&' patterns in 'if' statements
Description: When C code contains more than one '&&' pattern in an 'if' statement, verification may not be precise on NIV checks.
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows or Linux system, please refer to 'Workaround' section of EBR 569781. This bug report contains binaries that resolve this issue.

Summary: Indexing a sliced cell array with constant and loop variables for parfor output generates an error
Description: If you attempt to return results from a parfor  block using a sliced cell array in which the initial set of indices are constant and the subsequent index is the loop variable, parfor  throws an error. Running the following code shows the problem: k = 1;parfor i = 1:10 results{k, i} = 1;end ? ?

Summary: Software component with MODE-DECLARATION-GROUP-PROTOTYPE causes importer to fail
Description: A software component with a MODE-DECLARATION-GROUP-PROTOTYPE  causes the importer to fail with the following message: ??? Error using ==> i_extract_component_info/n_extract_interface_description at 286Cannot find a valid DataElement in SenderReceiverInterface "/interfaces/mode" referenced by Port "myPort" (inAtomicSoftwareComponent "mySWC"). Error in ==> C:\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\rtw\targets\autosar\autosar\@arxml\@importer\private\p_extract_all_info.p>i_extract_component_infoat 153 Error in ==> C:\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\rtw\targets\autosar\autosar\@arxml\@importer\private\p_extract_all_info.p>p_extract_all_info at 10 Error in ==> C:\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\rtw\targets\autosar\autosar\@arxml\@importer\private\p_create_slcomponent.p>p_create_slcomponent at31 Error in ==> arxml.importer.createComponentAsModel at 59[modelH, success] = p_create_slcomponent(this, argParser, true);
Workaround: Remove the MODE-GROUPS from the SENDER-RECEIVER-INTERFACE, which is as follows:<SENDER-RECEIVER-INTERFACE> <SHORT-NAME>myInterface</SHORT-NAME> <IS-SERVICE>false</IS-SERVICE> <DATA-ELEMENTS/> <MODE-GROUPS> <MODE-DECLARATION-GROUP-PROTOTYPE> <SHORT-NAME>myModeDeclarationGroup</SHORT-NAME> <TYPE-TREF DEST="MODE-DECLARATION-GROUP">/pkg/modeDeclarationGroup</TYPE-TREF> </MODE-DECLARATION-GROUP-PROTOTYPE> </MODE-GROUPS> </SENDER-RECEIVER-INTERFACE>??

Description: The following error appears in the SystemTest Run Status pane when you run a Simulink model in the Accelerator mode using the Simulink element:   Index exceeds matrix dimensions.   MathWorks strongly recommends that you upgrade to a newer release.
Workaround: As a workaround for R2009b, please perform the following steps:1.  Quit MATLAB?.2.  Navigate to MATLABROOT\toolbox\systemtest\systemtest\@systemtest\@JSimulink. Download the attached file attachment_595156_20100707.zip to a temporary directory. 5. Extract the file OPENMODEL.P from the zip file downloaded in step 4 and copy OPENMODEL.P to the directory in step 2.6.  Navigate to MATLABROOT\toolbox\systemtest\systemtest\private.  7. Extract the file SIMULINKGETOUTPORTINFO.P from the zip file downloaded in step 4 and copy SIMULINKGETOUTPORTINFO.P to the directory in step 6.9. Start MATLAB. 10. Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:>> rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Help does not work for methods that use ~ to denote an unused input.
Description: Help does not find methods that use ~ in the function definition line to denote an unused input. Instead of producing help, Help reports, "No help found for <methodName>."
Workaround: If you encounter this problem, replace the ~ operator with a variable name that is (and will remain) unused.Doing this causes MLint to display the following message:   Input argument '' might be unused. If this    is OK, consider replacing it by ~.You can either ignore this warning, or disable the warning for this line by right-clicking the unused variable name, and selecting:   Suppress this Instance of "Input argument     might be unused..."Selecting this option places the text %#ok at the end of the line and tells MLint not to display the warning shown above.

Summary: AFFYREAD function returns an error indicating the input file is not a valid GIN format.
Description: Some GIN-formatted files contain information for database sources with more fields than expected.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\private\affxginread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\private\affxginread.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarrayRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Stateflow chart or Embedded MATLAB Function block parameters in a library model are evaluated even when the chart or block is not used in the main model
Description: In a library model, if a Stateflow chart or Embedded MATLAB Function block is referred to in the main model, other unused Stateflow charts or Embedded MATLAB blocks in the library have their parameters unnecessarily evaluated when the main model is simulated.

Summary: Communication between the xPC Target host PC and target PC is slow (on the order of 3 to 4 Mbps)
Description: Communication between the xPC Target host PC and target PC is slow (on the order of 3 to 4 Mbps). You will likely experience slow communication only when no real-time model is running on the target PC.
Workaround: As a workaround:Ignore the issue. You will likely experience slow communication only when no real-time model is running on the target PC.Contact MathWorks Technical Support for software patches for xPC Target Version 4.1 (R2009a) or 4.2 (R2009b).

Summary: When using sftool while MATLAB (R) is in debug mode, no fitting or plotting occurs, or fitting and plotting use the wrong data.
Description: When using sftool  while MATLAB is in debug mode, you see function workspace variables in the data selection drop downs (X input, Y input, Z output and Weights). However, if you select any of those variables, either no fitting or plotting occurs, or if there are variables in the base workspace with the same name, fitting and plotting use the variables in the base workspace (not the function workspace).
Workaround: For R2009a, you must explicitly replicate variables in the MATLAB base workspace, and then call sftool:assignin('base', 'x', x);assignin('base', 'y', y);assignin('base', 'z', z);sftool(x, y, z)For R2009b, call sftool by explicitly specifying the working datasets to be used:sftool(x, y, z)

Summary: Simulink terminates if a block RuntimeObject handle is deleted while the model is simulating
Description: If any of the following run-time data is deleted using the delete  command while the model is simulating, Simulink unexpectedly terminates execution at the end of the simulation. Block run-time object handle obtained through the get_param  command (e.g., rto = get_param(blk_path, 'RuntimeObject') ) Block run-time data handle obtained from the block run-time object (e.g., rt_out = rto.OutputPort(1) )
Workaround: As a workaround, do not delete the block run-time object handle or the block run-time data handle while the model is simulating. Simulink removes the object handles at the end of the simulation. To clean up workspace variables, use the clear command after the simulation is complete.

Summary: Assertion error is raised during HDL code generation for some cases of division operator
Description: When generating HDL code for a Simulink model that contains a division operator, the coder may raise an internal assertion error, terminating code generation. This error is encountered under the following conditions: The division occurs in either a Stateflow chart or an Embedded MATLAB block. The division result is an operand of a relational operator. The relational operator may be any of (< <= > >= == ~=).  When both these conditions are met, the coder fails to generate HDL code and displays an Assertion Error with the text isa(aType) . Also, a stack trace is displayed on the MATLAB console. In an Embedded MATLAB Block, the code causing the error will be structured as in the following example: if (a / b) > 4% If statement bodyend  In a Stateflow chart, the code causing the error will be a Condition on a chart transition structured as in the following example: [(a / b) <= 12]
Workaround: To avoid this problem, move the division out from the relational operator expression by using a temporary variable. For the previous Embedded MATLAB block example, a suitable workaround is:div_temp = a / b;if div_temp > 4% If statement bodyendFor the previous Stateflow chart example the workaround is similar. The quantity (a/b) must be computed and stored in a local chart variable. This local variable is then used in the Transition Condition in place of the inline division operator.

Summary: nbinrnd can run out of memory for some inputs
Description: The nbinrnd  function uses two methods to generate negative binomial random values. One requires more memory but is faster for small problems. The other is slower but does not require as much memory. For some inputs, when you use nbinrnd  to generate a lot of values, the function may run out of memory:    r = nbinrnd(50,0.5,1e6,1);
Workaround: If the function runs out of memory, you can allocate space for the result and generate values in a loop, either one at a time or in smaller blocks. For example:   r = zeros(1e6,1);   for j=1:10      r((j-1)*1e5 + (1:1e5)) = nbinrnd(50,0.5,1e5,1);   end

Summary: VITDEC function error message indicates CONT mode when it should indicate TRUNC or TERM mode
Description: VITDEC function has three modes: continuous (CONT), terminated (TERM), and truncated (TRUNC). In TERM and TRUNC modes, the traceback depth should be less than or equal to the number of input symbols. If this condition is violated, then the following error is thrown: ??? Error using ==> vitdec at 334For the 'cont' mode, traceback depth must be a positive integer scalar not larger than the number of input symbols in CODE. The error message incorrectly indicates the 'CONT' mode, while it should be 'TERM and TRUNC' modes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In rare situations, charts containing local events might generate incorrect code
Description: In rare situations, charts containing local events and a specific pattern of state hierarchy might generate incorrect Real-Time Workshop code. See the attached diagram in g59447.jpg. The necessary conditions for this bug are: The chart must contain at least one local event. There must be at least three levels of state hierarchy (for example, B containing C, which contains C1, C2, and so on). A parent containing multiple substates (for example, B) where there should be at least one substate that contains an exit action somewhere in its child states (in this case, it is D). There should be at least one substate that does not contain an exit action anywhere in its child states and itself (in this case, it is C). The state containing no exit actions anywhere should have an entry action (in this case, C).  When the chart satisfies all of these conditions, after exiting the superstate B once and reentering, the entry action for C is not executed.
Workaround: In R2009a and R2009b, this bug can be avoided by executing the following command once per sesson before generating code:sf('feature','Optimized Exit Fcns',0);You can add this command to your startup.m file so that it executes in every session. You can also add this to the file toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/private/sfinit.m so that it executes every time you load a chart. This will help you avoid this bug in the generated code.The only other effect of this command is to turn off a minor readability feature that minimizes switch statements in state exit functions. No observable effect on the code efficiency exists when you turn off this optimization.

Summary: sort with complex array input may return an incorrect result on Windows platforms.
Description: When X  is a complex array (not a vector), sort(X)  may return an incorrect result when size(X,dim) > 10 , where dim  is the dimension being sorted along. This bug affects only Windows platforms (both 32- and 64-bit).
Workaround: Since sort works properly with complex vectors, a workaround for this bug is to call sort in a for loop. For example, if X is a complex matrix, Y = sort(X) can be done correctly with the following code:Y = X;for k = 1:size(X,2)    Y(:,k) = sort(X(:,k));endA more general solution for complex N-D arrays sorting over arbitrary dimensions (i.e. Y = sort(X, dim)) looks like:dimvec = [dim, 1:dim-1, dim+1:ndims(X)];Y = permute(X, dimvec);sortLen = size(Y, 1);for k = 1:sortLen:numel(Y)    Y(k:k+sortLen-1) = sort(Y(k:k+sortLen-1), 1);endY = ipermute(Y, dimvec);

Summary: A chain of function or object method calls within a script file may cause a SEGV in MATLAB?.
Description: The following example script has been observed to result in a SEGV: f = myClass;f.myMethod(); f.myMethod(); f.myMethod();     % More calls to f.myMethod()classdef myClass  properties    sample = otherClass;  end  methods    function obj = myMethod(obj)      obj.sample.fd = rand;      otherFunction();      % otherFunction may call more functions in depth.    end  endend
Workaround: To work around this problem, you can either add the following statement to the beginning of your script file:   feature scopedAccelEnablement offor enter the following command at the MATLAB? Command Window before running the script:   feature accel off

Summary: Invalid icdevice objects created when using MDD files generated from IVI drivers
Description: In Instrument Control Toolbox Version 2.8, creating an icdevice  object using an MDD file generated from an IVI driver should throw an error when the second argument to the icdevice  function is an interface object. For example, when you execute this code: >> makemid('Agilent546XX.Agilent546XX');>>interfaceObj = visa('agilent','TCPIP0:: xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx::inst0::INSTR');>>deviceObj = icdevice('Agilent546XX.mdd',interfaceObj); the icdevice  function should throw an error. Using this invalid device object may result in an error. For example, connecting to the device object causes the following error: >>connect(deviceObj) ??? Error using ==> icdevice.connect at 117Could not open VISA object. Use INSTRHWINFO for a list of available configurations. If this error is not an instrument error, use MIDEDIT to inspect the driver.
Workaround: This issue occurs because an invalid icdevice object is created when:Using the icdevice function with MATLAB MDD filesThe MDD file is generated from an IVI driverThe second argument to icdevice is an interface objectTo fix this issue install the attached patch:Save the icdeviceFix.zip file to a temporary location such as:c:\tmp\icdeviceFix.zipIn the MATLAB Command Window, type:>> cd(matlabroot) >> unzip('c:/tmp/icdeviceFix.zip') >> rehash path >> rehash toolboxcacheTo verify the correct behavior of the icdevice command, type :>> makemid('Agilent546XX.Agilent546XX')>>interfaceObj = visa('agilent','TCPIP0:: xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx::inst0::INSTR');>>deviceObj = icdevice('Agilent546XX.mdd',interfaceObj);The icdevice function should throw the following error:??? Error using ==> icdevice.icdevice at 226 The driver must be a MATLAB interface instrument driver.For the valid syntax of the icdevice function, type help icdevice  at the MATLAB Command prompt.

Summary: The bloomberg.fetch documentation is incorrect for override inputs.
Description: The bloomberg.fetch documentation is incorrect for override inputs. The documentation shows the inputs ordered as data = fetch(Connect, 'Security', 'GETDATA', 'Fields', 'Override', 'Ident', 'Values')  'Ident' and 'Values' are swapped. It should read data = fetch(Connect, 'Security', 'GETDATA', 'Fields', 'Override', 'Values','Ident')  ?

Summary: wmsinfo issues a bad subscript error message for empty Web map server errors
Description: wmsinfo  does not properly handle empty error messages from the Web map server; instead, it issues the following eror: "Attempted to access nonSpaces(1); index out of bounds because numel(nonSpaces)=0. "
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the version of wmsinfo.m in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/geoweb/geoweb/wmsinfo.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: wmsinfo.m.oldCopy the version of wmsinfo.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Japanese Issue: The Bus Creator and Bus Selector blocks change signal names of "Zero" and "One" to numeric values.
Description: On a Japanese PC, when you set bus signal names to "Zero" or "One", the Bus Creator and Bus Selector blocks change the names to "0" or "1".
Workaround: - Download the attached trZeroOneFixes.p file.- Start MATLAB.- In the MATLAB Command Window, enter:trZeroOneFixes- Restart MATLAB

Summary: Check for systems that mix primitive blocks and subsystems incorrectly warns of mix of primitive blocks and subsystems
Description: If your model contains subsystems and Action Port blocks at the same level, but no other blocks, running Check for systems that mix primitive blocks and subsystems  results in a warning. The warning indicates that the model contains a mix of primitive blocks and subsystems. However, the check should pass because Action Port blocks are not considered primitive blocks in this instance and therefore should be ignored.
Workaround: Replace the matlabroot\toolbox\slvnv\styleguide\private\styleguide_db_0143.p file as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Determine your MATLAB release number by entering the version('-release') command at the MATLAB command line.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\toolbox\slvnv\styleguide\private\styleguide_db_0143.p to matlabroot\toolbox\slvnv\styleguide\private\styleguide_db_0143.p.old.Download the attached file that matches your MATLAB release number, and place it in matlabroot\toolbox\slvnv\styleguide\private\.Rename the downloaded file from styleguide_db_0143_release.p to styleguide_db_0143.p.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: In Rapid Accelerator mode, Simulink ignores settings for Simulation Custom Code
Description: In Rapid Accelerator simulation mode, Simulink ignores the settings specified in the model for the Simulation Custom Code parameter.
Workaround: The work around is to use the following command before you simulate in Rapid Accelerator mode with Custom Simulation Code:set_param(gcs,'RTWUseSimCustomCode','on')

Summary: fmincon reports that it stopped because the norm of the step is smaller than TolX, while the report show the norm is larger than TolX.
Description: The fmincon interior-point  algorithm can report that it stopped because the norm of the step is smaller than the TolX  tolerance, while the reported numerical values show that the norm of the step is larger than TolX .
Workaround: Disregard the reported numerical value for the norm of the step. It does not match what is used internally in the algorithm.

Summary: Incorrect error message running Code Generation Verification and cgv.CGV properties are set to: 'ComponentType', 'modelblock' and 'LogMode','SignalLogging'
Description: An incorrect error message occurs when using cgv.CGV  and your model contains all of the following: A scalar signal (not a bus) connects to an outport cgv.CGV  property 'ComponentType'  is set to 'modelblock'  cgv.CGV  property 'LogMode'  is set to 'SignalLogging'  (this is the default)  The incorrect error message pertains to the outport as follows:Error using ==> Config.checkTopModel at 92 For modelblock type execution in CGV, Buses at the outport may require an active Bus Object. The following outports do not have an active bus object: 'ModelName/OutportName'
Workaround: If no buses connect to any outports, run cgv.CGV with property 'LogMode' set to 'SaveOutput'.An alternative workaround is to disable signal logging at the non-bus outports and run the model twice:Run the model with property 'LogMode' set to 'SignalLogging' to capture the outputs from the buses.Run the model with property 'LogMode' set to 'SaveOutput' to capture the outputs from the non-bus signals.?

Summary: AUTOSAR model with multirunnables does not build when there is an instance of a reusable subsystem in different runnables
Description: An AUTOSAR multirunnable model with instances of a reusable subsystem in different runnables will generate the following build error: -----------------------------------------------------### Initial pass through model to cache user defined code.### Caching model source code......Error: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\syslib.tlc Line: 1353 Column: 34The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifierError: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 183 Column: 17The == and != operators can only be used to compare values of the same typeError: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 185 Column: 21The == and != operators can only be used to compare values of the same typeError: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 188 Column: 21The == and != operators can only be used to compare values of the same type### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'model' aborted due to an error.??? Subsystem build failed
Workaround: Do one of the following:Inline the reusable subsystem.Use the attached .tlc file to replace your current .tlc file.

Summary: sort(X,dim)may give an incorrect result when dim > 1 and dim < ndims(X).
Description: For an N-D array X , sort(X,dim)  does not sort the array correctly when dim > 1  and dim < ndims(X) . sort  works properly when size(X,dim)  is sufficiently small (less than 100) regardless of the value of dim . Note that because of the restriction on dim , this bug does not affect sort  on 2-D arrays.
Workaround: A workaround is to permute the array prior to sorting and permute the result back. Consider replacing Y = sort(X, dim)with the following code:dimvec = 1:ndims(X);dimvec(dim) = [];Y = sort(permute(X,[dim dimvec]),1);Y = ipermute(Y, [dim dimvec]);?

Summary: Build of multitasking model for host-based shared library target might fail with unresolved external symbol link error
Description: Building a model might fail with a link error if both of the following conditions are true: You configured the model to use the host-based shared library target (System target file  is set to ert_shrlib.tlc ). You configured the model for multitasking execution (Tasking mode for periodic sample times  is set to MultiTasking ).  When the failure occurs, the build displays the error message error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol model _step1  where model   is the name of your model.
Workaround: To work around this bug, reconfigure the model for single-tasking execution. On the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the parameter Tasking mode for periodic sample times to SingleTasking.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop S-function build fails with undeclared identifier errors for variable RT_MEMORY_ALLOCATION_ERROR
Description: Generating a Real-Time Workshop S-function might fail with undeclared identifier  errors for the variable RT_MEMORY_ALLOCATION_ERROR . The failure indicates that generated code tried to use the variable RT_MEMORY_ALLOCATION_ERROR  before it was declared. For example, the errors can occur during a right-click > Real-Time Workshop > Generate S-Function  build for a subsystem containing SimMechanics blocks.

Summary: PolySpace verification reports extra orange NIV checks on a structure initialized by aliasing
Description: PolySpace results display extra orange NIV checks on some structure fields even if the complete structure has been initialized in another function. This happens most frequently when a structure is given by reference to a function and initialized inside it.
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows system on a R2009b release:Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\binCreate backup copies of the following two files:x86_64-mingw32\iabc-il.cx86_64-mingw32.exex86-mingw32\iabc-il.cx86-mingw32.exeDownload the file 592257_Windows.zip that is attached to this bug report.Extract the files x86_64-mingw32\iabc-il.cx86_64-mingw32.exe and x86-mingw32\iabc-il.cx86-mingw32.exe from the zip file.Copy these two files to the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin.To workaround this issue on a Linux system on a R2009b release:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin.Create backup copies of the following two files:bin/x86_64-linux/iabc-il.cx86_64-linuxbin/x86-linux/iabc-il.cx86-linuxDownload the file 592257_Linux.zip that is attached to this bug report.Extract the files x86_64-linux/iabc-il.cx86_64-linux and x86-linux/iabc-il.cx86-linux from the zip file.Copy these two files to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin.To workaround this issue on a Solaris system on a R2009b release:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin.Create a backup copy of the following file: sparc-solaris/iabc-il.csparc-solarisDownload the file 592257_Solaris.zip that is attached to this bug report.Extract the file sparc-solaris/iabc-il.csparc-solaris from the zip file.Copy this file to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during "C to intermediate language translation" with the message: "unhandled exception: SysErr: The system cannot find the file specified. [noent]"
Description: While verifying a C code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: **** C to intermediate language translation - 15 (P_PT) took 4.5real, 4.5u + 0s (0.1gc)exception SysErr(OS.SysErr(name="Unknown error", syserror=notempty)) raised.unhandled exception: SysErr: The system cannot find the file specified. [noent]  After the end of this phase, Polyspace writes some temporary files inside 'temp ' sub-folder. Anti-virus stops the writting of these temporary files leading to this error. To ascertain the diagnostic, stopping anti virus solves the problem.
Workaround: Note that following workaround is only available for release R2009b. To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\binCreate backup copy of the following file x86-mingw32\iabc-c2il.x86-mingw32.exeDownload the attached file 2010-07-09-attachment-592256-client-server-9b-win32.zip.Extract *.exe file from the zip archive and copy it into previous sub-folder

Summary: When a model has a Signal Builder block with a signal group that has no links to requirements, an error occurs when creating the requirements report.
Description: User gets the following error when trying to generate a requirements report: ??? Error using ==> locGenerateReport at 146Attempt to reference field of non-structure array. Error in ==> D:\archive\R2009b\ship\win32\dist\toolbox\matlab\iofun\@rptgen\report.p>report at 51 Error in ==> report at 47varargout = rptgen.report(varargin{:}); ?
Workaround: You can patch this problem by updating one of the files in the MATLAB installation:1) cd <matlabroot>\R2009b\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\@RptgenRMI\@CReqSigbGroupLoop where <matlabroot> is the installation directory.2) Back up the file loop_getLoopObjects.p to a new directory that is not on the MATLAB path.3) Copy the attached file into the directory.4) Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Failure creating digital I/O objects for a Measurement Computing PCI-INT32
Description: When you create a digital I/O object for a Measurement Computing PCI-INT32 MATLAB throws the following segmentation violation: dio = digitalio('mcc', 1)  Segmentation violation detected at Wed Oct 28 08:36:01 2009...
Workaround: This is due to an error in the initialization file entry for the MCC PCI-INT32. To work around this issue modify the entry for this device in the mwmcc.ini file.In the MATLAB Command Window type:matlabrootand record the location of the MATLAB root.Exit MATLAB.In any text editor open the following file:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwmcc.iniFind the section for the PCI-INT32. Delete the existing section and replace it with the following lines:;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;[PCI-INT32]ID = 0x002CBus = PCIDIOConfigurable = 1DPORTS = 6DPORT0LINES = 8DPORT0TYPE = 10DPORT0CONFIG = 2DPORT1LINES = 8DPORT1TYPE = 11DPORT1CONFIG = 2DPORT2LINES = 4DPORT2TYPE = 12DPORT2CONFIG = 2DPORT3LINES = 8DPORT3TYPE = 14DPORT3CONFIG = 2DPORT4LINES = 8DPORT4TYPE = 15DPORT4CONFIG = 2DPORT5LINES = 4DPORT5TYPE = 16DPORT5CONFIG = 2Save mwmcc.ini and restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Setting source workspace to 'current' inside parfor results in transparency error and possibly a Segv
Description: Using the sim command inside a parfor loop with 'SrcWorkspace' set to 'current' results in transparency errors. In addition, MATLAB might hang or cause a segmentation violation. These transparency errors occur because Simulink attempts to evaluate variables in the source workspace, which in this case is the parfor workspace. For more information, see the Simulink documentation on transparency issues in "Running Parallel Simulations".
Workaround: You can avoid the transparency errors by using either the model or the base workspace for the model's referenced workspace parameters. For example, replace the following erroneous code with one of the two workarounds shown:Errorneous Code:parfor idx = 1:4   K = 0.001*idx;   m = 0.045*idx;    output{idx} = sim(model, 'SrcWorkSpace', 'current');endWorkaround 1: (Using Model Workspace)parfor idx = 1:4   load_system(model);   hws = get_param(model, 'modelWorkspace');   hws.assignin('K', 0.001*idx);   hws.assignin('m', 0.045*idx);   output{idx} = sim(model, 'ReturnWorkspaceOutputs', 'on');endWorkaround 2: (Using Base Workspace)parfor idx = 1:4   assignin('base', 'K', 0.001*idx);   assignin('base', 'm', 0.045*idx);    output{idx} = sim(model, 'ReturnWorkspaceOutputs', 'on');end?

Summary: Warning message: "WARNING: QApplication was not created in the main() thread."
Description: You may see the warning message: "WARNING: QApplication was not created in the main() thread." On Linux and Mac operating systems, this message is output from stderr, which by default is the console window. On Windows operating systems, this is output from an attached debugging console window if you run MATLAB from a source code debugger. This message is benign, it will not cause any problems. You can safely ignore it.

Summary: For some symbolic expressions, subs returns symbolic results instead of doubles
Description: For large expressions containing functions whose names differ between MuPAD and MATLAB (for example, arctan  and atan ), the subs  function can return a symbolic object instead of an array of doubles.
Workaround: Convert the result Y returned by subs to a double format by calling double(Y).An alternative workaround in R2009b is put the attached mllib.mu file in matlabroot/toolbox/symbolic/mllib/mllib.mu.

Summary: Keyboard shortcuts to increase or decrease the font size of the Model Explorer, Simulink Library Browser, and block dialogs may not work on machines with Japanese keyboards
Description: The Ctrl++  and Ctrl+-  keyboard shortcuts to increase or decrease the font size of the Model Explorer, Simulink Library Browser, and many Simulink and Stateflow dialogs may not work on machines with Japanese keyboards, when you use the main keyboard keys. The shortcuts work when you use the numeric keypad.
Workaround: Use Ctrl++ and Ctrl+- with the numeric keypad.

Summary: VHDL code generated from Stateflow may cause a compile error
Description: Simulink HDL Coder can generate uncompilable VHDL code for a Stateflow chart that contains a relational expression with a scaled fixed point value as an operand. The requirements for this to occur are: The target language is VHDL. The model contains a Stateflow chart that uses Embedded MATLAB subfunctions. An Embedded MATLAB subfunction contains a relational operator (>, <, <=, >=, ==, ~=). One operand of the relational operator is an unsigned fixed point expression with a scaled binary point. (Note that scaled fixed point values may be inferred even when there are no explicit scaled fixed point data variables or values in the expression.) The other operand of the relational operator is not of a scaled fixed point type.  When all of these these conditions occur, the other (non-scaled) operand will be incorrectly cast to a signed type. VHDL does not support use of mixed signed/unsigned operands; this causes a compilation error.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, place an explicit cast around the scaled fixed point expression. The cast must remove any fixed point scaling from the expression. Alternatively, you can place the scaled fixed point expression into a local variable that is not scaled. Then, use the local variable as the operand of the relational operator.

Summary: Errors occur when building xPC Target models that contain Embedded MATLAB blocks configured with variable inputs and varaible outputs
Description: Errors occur when building xPC Target models that contain Embedded MATLAB blocks configured with variable inputs and varaible outputs. The model fails to build and the following error message is displayed: Error: File: \matlabroot \toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\mdlxml.tlc: 243 Column: 52Ambiguous reference to ChartData - must use array index to refer to one of multiple scopes
Workaround: As a workaround,Close MATLAB.Download the attached file tlc9b.zip and unzip it to a temporary location.Change directory to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc.From this folder, delete mdlxml.tlc and capi_xpc.tlc.Copy and paste the unzipped files mdlxml.tlc and capi_xpc.tlc from the temporary location to the tlc folder.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation: Square root algorithm for fixed-point datatypes may give incorrect results when "Signal storage reuse" is turned on
Description: If "Signal storage reuse" is turned on in the Optimization Section of the Configuration Parameters, the fixed-point square-root algorithm may give incorrect results. With this optimization active, the callsite to the square root algorithm uses the same variable as input and output making, in consequence, the ouput always equal to zero.
Workaround: Uncheck "Signal storage reuse" in the Optimization Section of the Configuration Parameters.

Summary: A bug in the JDK can produce a Null Pointer Exception from IMAQTOOL on Windows Vista.
Description: A bug in the JDK (Sun Java Bug ID #6668281) can produce a Null Pointer Exception from IMAQTOOL on Windows Vista. This bug can occur when choosing to save or export a configuration. ??? Java exception occurred: java.lang.NullPointerException at sun.swing.table.DefaultTableCellHeaderRenderer.getColumnSortOrder(Unknown Source) at com.sun.java.swing.plaf.windows.WindowsTableHeaderUI$XPDefaultRenderer.getTableCellRendererComponent(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.toolbox.imaq.browser.dialogs.objectExporter.ObjectExporterTable.createPanel(ObjectExporterTable.java:102)  at com.mathworks.toolbox.imaq.browser.dialogs.objectExporter.VariableNameTablePanel.<init>(VariableNameTablePanel.java:31)
Workaround: To work around this issue, set your Windows theme to "Windows Classic".

Summary: Incorrect contours generated by function mdistort
Description: Contours generated by function mdistort  may be incorrect in a variety of situations. In the case of azimuthal projections, for example, contours of map scale along meridians ('merscale' ) and parallels ('parscale' ) are incorrect unless the origin falls on the Equator and has no rotational term.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the mdistort.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/mdistort.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: mdistort.m.oldCopy the version of mdistort.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Interpolation Using Prelookup and Lookup Table (n-D) blocks give mismatched simulation and code generation results for mixed floating-point and fixed-point types
Description: When both double  and fixed-point data types are present, simulation uses double  and ignores the Intermediate results data type  on the block dialog box. But code generation uses the specification for Intermediate results data type .

Summary: Setting FontUnits to normalized when creating a uitable causes its font size to be set incorrectly
Description: Setting FontUnits  to normalized  when constructing a uitable causes the font size to be set to a large number.
Workaround: Create the uitable and set the fontunits to normalized separately. Instead of:T = uitable('Data', rand(4), 'Units', 'normalized', 'Position', [0.2 0.2 0.7 0.7], 'FontUnits', 'normalized'); use:T = uitable('Data', rand(4), 'Units', 'normalized', 'Position', [0.2 0.2 0.7 0.7]);set(t, 'FontUnits', 'normalized')

Summary: SegV produced when simulating a model containing Stateflow Truth Tables in parfor loop
Description: When a Stateflow Truth Table model is simulated inside a parfor loop, it may produce a SegV and crash MATLAB. This bug usually happens on Windows 64-bit platforms.
Workaround: Before simulating the model using parfor, make sure the model has been resaved using the following steps:In the main MATLAB session, open the Truth Table model.Update diagram.Resave the model, including all used library models that contain Truth Tables.

Summary: GETHMMTREE and PHYTREEREAD return an error indicating that the names for leaves and branches must be unique.
Description: Certain tree file formats store bootstrap values in the branch labels. The functions PHYTREEREAD  and GETHMMTREE  interpret them as actual branch labels. Non-unique labels prevent the PHYTREE  object constructor from creating a tree.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\phytreeread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\phytreeread.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfoRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect CONSTANT-REF for arrays in exported AUTOSAR datatype arxml file
Description: For Simulink models that have wide inports and outports, the exported datatype arxml file contains incorrect CONSTANT-REF  values. For example, <CONSTANT-SPECIFICATION> <SHORT-NAME>rt_Array_SInt32_2InitValue</SHORT-NAME> <VALUE> <ARRAY-SPECIFICATION> <SHORT-NAME>rt_Array_SInt32_2InitValue</SHORT-NAME> <TYPE-TREF DEST="ARRAY-TYPE">/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/rt_Array_SInt32_2</TYPE-TREF> <ELEMENTS> <CONSTANT-REFERENCE> <SHORT-NAME>SInt32_1</SHORT-NAME> <TYPE-TREF DEST="INTEGER-TYPE">/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/SInt32</TYPE-TREF> <CONSTANT-REF DEST="CONSTANT-SPECIFICATION">/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/Ground/SInt32InitValue/SInt32InitValue< /CONSTANT-REF> </CONSTANT-REFERENCE>   In this example, CONSTANT-REF should be: <CONSTANT-REF DEST="CONSTANT-SPECIFICATION">/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/Ground/SInt32InitValue</CONSTANT-REF>

Summary: Segmentation violation occurs when setting a filename or path that uses Japanese characters with the From Multimedia File or From Wave File blocks.
Description: When setting the filename or path to a file containing Japanese characters using the From Multimedia File  or From Wave File  blocks, the following error is observed: Stack Trace: [0] sdspwafi2.mexw32:void __cdecl mdlInitializeConditions(struct SimStruct_tag *)(0x25cf50e0, 0, 0x00c3a560 "???", 0x7b6633d4) + 122 bytes [1] sdspwafi2.mexw32:_mexFunction(0xfffffffe, 0x254e486c, 16, 0x25ca6ec8) + 964 bytes [2] libmex.dll:_mexRunMexFile(0xfffffffe, 0x254e486c, 16, 0x25ca6ec8) + 132 bytes [3] libmex.dll:private: void __thiscall Mfh_mex::runMexFileWithSignalProtection(int,struct mxArray_tag * *,int,struct mxArray_tag * *)(0xfffffffe, 0x254e486c, 16, 0x25ca6ec8) + 73 bytes [4] libmex.dll:public: virtual void __thiscall Mfh_mex::dispatch_file(int,struct mxArray_tag * *,int,struct mxArray_tag * *)(0xfffffffe, 0x254e486c, 16, 0x25ca6ec8) + 321 bytes [5] m_dispatcher.dll:public: virtual void __thiscall Mfh_file::dispatch_fh(int,struct mxArray_tag * *,int,struct mxArray_tag * *)(0xfffffffe, 0x254e486c, 16, 0x25ca6ec8) + 204 bytes [6] m_dispatcher.dll:void __cdecl mdCallFunction(int,struct mxArray_tag * *,int,struct mxArray_tag * *,class Mfh_MATLAB_fn *)(0xfffffffe, 0x254e486c, 16, 0x25ca6ec8) + 44 bytes [7] m_dispatcher.dll:void __cdecl mdDoMatlabFcnCall(int,struct mxArray_tag * * const,int,struct mxArray_tag const * * const,class Mfh_MATLAB_fn *,enum mpLmgrUse)(0xfffffffe, 0x254e486c, 16, 0x25ca6ec8) + 87 bytes [8] libmwsimulink.dll:0x6ebe653c(1, 0x6f173f40, 0xfffffffe, 0x254e486c) ...

Summary: linprog errors on some sparse problems.
Description: linprog  errors, complaining about an undefined variable: ??? Undefined function or variable "RxzHist1".  Error in ==> lipsol at 864  This bug may appear only under the following conditions: 1. You use the default large-scale interior-point algorithm. 2. The problem is sparse. 3.
