Summary: Incorrect code generation: For reusable function-call subsystem when Inline parameters is cleared
Description: Real-Time Workshop software might generate incorrect code under all of the following conditions: The model contains nested reusable function-call subsystem blocks Some of the reusable function-call subsystem blocks might be connected through signal lines Inline parameters  is cleared on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box  ?
Workaround: On the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select Inline parameters.

Summary: Incorrect coverage for MinMax blocks with fixed-point signals or different data types
Description: When your model contains MinMax blocks with fixed-point signals or MinMax blocks that have different data types for the inputs and outputs, the reported coverage might be incorrect.

Summary: We try to create repositories that are too long for the filesystem to handle.
Description: This problem originates with how custom repositories are uniquely identified. We are using the path to the products slblocks.m file as the unique identifier. We do this by adding the path to where custom repositories are stored (i.e. the prefsdir), and storing the repository in this new directory. If the user has a deeply-nested slblocks.m file, this will create a long identifier for the product, potentially too long for the filesystem to handle.
Workaround: This problem is possible with any customer created product. However, it can be worked around by creating a smaller path, i.e. moving the product to a directory higher up in the directory structure.

Summary: Data Type Conversion block configured as "stored integer" emits data copies when connected to an S-function block
Description: Suppose that a fixed-point signal, such as fixdt(1,16,15), is passed through a Data Type Conversion block configured to output an int16 value as a stored integer. In R2009a, this Data Type Conversion block is optimized away in the generated code.  However, in R2009b, the Data Type Conversion block introduces data copies if connected to an S-function block that uses TLC for code generation.

Summary: No default file extension set in MuPAD plot exports.
Description: When you export a plot to a file without specifying file extension, MuPAD does not automatically add any extension. For example, the following command writes a file filename  in the MuPAD plot format XVC without any extension: plot(sin(x), OutputFile="filename") The documentation of the OutputFile option states that MuPAD uses the default file extension .xvz (compressed xvc format).
Workaround: Always specify the extension of the preferred output format.

Summary: Model serialization problem due to file dependencies being represented as a comma delimited list.
Description: Crash occurs when a library in the library browser is on a path that contains a comma.
Workaround: Remove commas from the paths.

Summary: kstest returns the wrong test decision
Description: The first output from kstest  is a binary value indicating whether to reject the null hypothesis (H==1) or not (H==0). For a test against a two-sided alternative hypothesis, kstest  computes that value using the wrong significance level. Thus, kstest  will incorrectly fail to reject the null hypothesis in some cases. This computation is incorrect only when the type  input argument is 'unequal' . The test decision is correct when type  is 'larger'  or 'smaller' . The p-value returned by kstest  in the second output is computed correctly.
Workaround: To decide whether or not to reject the null hypothesis, compare the second output from kstest to the desired significance level. For examplex = randn(10,1);alpha = 0.05;[ignore,p] = kstest(x,[],alpha,'unequal');H = (p < alpha);If H == 0, then the null hypothesis (in this case, that x is a sample from a normal distribution) cannot be rejected. If H == 1, then the null hypothesis can be rejected at the significance level alpha.

Summary: PolySpace calculates wrong range in an array initialization loop when using hc12 target
Description: Launching a verification on the following code: static short myarray[4];int main(void){  int index;  for( index = 0; index < 4; index++ )  myarray[index] = index;  return 1;}  For the 'myarray ' index PolySpace Viewer displays 'full range [-2^8399 (2^8399)-1] ' instead of '[0..3] '. This occurs most frequently with the option -target hc12 .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: generated C++ encapsulation interface for referenced model might produce incorrect numerical result
Description: Generated code results for your model might not match simulation results for your model if both of the following conditions exist: Your model contains at least one Model block. You configured a C++ encapsulation interface for the model referenced by the Model block.  Under these conditions, in the generated code for the model, the parent model output function might call the referenced model's output function with incorrect input arguments. As a result, execution of the code might generate an incorrect numerical result.

Summary: Bitmap export from the separate MuPAD Graphics window creates defective images.
Description: When exporting a plot to a bitmap format (JPG, PNG, GIF, TIFF, BMP) from the MuPAD Graphics window, you may get a cropped image with large gray borders or a completely gray image. This issue affects the separate MuPAD Graphics window only. Exporting a plot from a MuPAD notebook itself does not create defective images.

Summary: dsNoSuchDirectory exceptions thrown when multiple processes are running in quick succession, using the same CTF cache.
Description: When the error occurs, you see the following exception: caught dsNoSuchDirectory exception: Tried to obtain a lock on a non-existent directory. C:\\.deploy_read_lock; re-throwing... MCR cache extracting component: ...

Summary: The RS-232 hardware receives signals of random FIFO overflow errors and becomes unresponsive
Description: The RS-232 hardwares receive signals of random FIFO overflow errors and becomes unresponsive. All composite xPC Target RS-232 subsystem blocks stop and unpredictably generate FIFO overflows. This behavior generates an error and halts the model. This behavior affects all blocks for the supported Quatech, mainboard, Diamond Systems, and Commtech Fastcom boards.
Workaround: As a workaround,Close MATLAB.Download the attached file, fifowrite.c, to the directory matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocksRestart MATLAB.Change folder to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks.In the MATLAB Command Window, type mex fifowrite.cChange back to your project directory and rebuild your model.

Summary: PID Controller and PID Controller (2DOF) blocks may not initialize correctly when model loads
Description: The PID Controller  and PID Controller (2DOF)  blocks may not initialize correctly when the Simulink? model loads. Under some conditions, the blocks load with the library block default under-mask circuits instead of the circuit specified by the parameters in the block dialog. In particular, if the block is not visible when the model loads (for example, the block is inside a Subsystem  block), the block will not initialize correctly.
Workaround: Unzip the attached file, and apply the attached patch as follows:Exit MATLAB?.Rename $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\configPID.m to $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\configPID.m.old. $matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory on your machine. To locate $matlabroot, type matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Rename $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\configPID2DOF.m to $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\configPID2DOF.m.old.Rename $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\PIDConfig.m to $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\PIDConfig.m.old.Save the attached files to $matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\+pidpack\@PIDConfig\.Restart MATLAB.At the MATLAB command prompt, type the following command to complete the installation:>> rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Optimize for HDL option does not work for FIR Decimator filters in fixed point mode in certain cases.
Description: If the Optimize for HDL  option is enabled for a fixed point FIR decimator (mfilt.firdecim) filter, where symmetric coefficients exist in any polyphase, a hard error occurs during HDL code generation.
Workaround: To fix this problem:Download the attached file, attachment_588863_20091110.zip.Unzip it to produce the file emit_par_preadd.pReplace the following file in your MATLAB installation with the new emit_par_preadd.p: MATLABROOT\toolbox\shared\hdlshared\@hdlfilter\@firdecim\emit_par_preadd.p

Summary: Stateflow Chart Loop component incorrectly loops over internal Truth Table block chart objects
Description: Stateflow Chart loops errors out when looping over an internal Truth Table chart objects. The error message looks like this: Looping on model "t1" Looping on system "t1" Looping on signal "In1<1>" Looping on signal "Truth Table<1>" Looping on block "In1" Looping on block "Out1" Looping on block "Truth Table" Looping on truthtablechart "Truth Table" In component Stateflow Snapshot : Object type "TruthTableChart" not found In component Chapter/Subsection : Object type "TruthTableChart" not found In component Logical If : Object type "TruthTableChart" not found In component Chart Loop : Object type "TruthTableChart" not found In component Chapter/Subsection : Object type "TruthTableChart" not found In component Logical If : Object type "TruthTableChart" not found In component System Loop : Object type "TruthTableChart" not found In component Model Loop : Object type "TruthTableChart" not found In component Chapter/Subsection : Object type "TruthTableChart" not found
Workaround: Back up the following files: matlab/toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/rgFindBlocks.pmatlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_chart_loop/loop_getLoopObjects.pmatlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_truthtable/makeTable.pExtract ZIP files to above directories.

Summary: Simulink Designer Verifier fails to generate a report when Report Generator is not installed.
Description: Simulink Designer Verifiers fails to generate a report. >>sldvreport('fileName') returns unsuccessfully Status = 0 Second argument = ''
Workaround: 1) Locate the directory <matlabroot>/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen, where <matlabroot> is the installation directory for MATLAB.2) Move the the file report.p to a backup location that is not on the MATLAB path.3) Copy the attached file report.p into the directory.4) Locate the directory <matlabroot>/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@cfr_paragraph/, where <matlabroot> is the installation directory for MATLAB.5) Move the the file execute.p to a backup location that is not on the MATLAB path.6) Copy the attached file execute.p into the directory.7) Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Channel objects report wrong filter delay if the first element of the PathDelay vector is non-zero
Description: RAYLEIGHCHAN, RICIANCHAN, and MIMOCHAN functions create channel objects to simulate Rayleigh, Rician, and MIMO fading channels, respectively. The ChannelFilterDelay property of these objects reports the delay caused by channel filtering. This property contains a wrong value if the first value in the PathDelay vector is non-zero.
Workaround: To obtain the correct delay, add the first path delay value, in samples, to the reported channel filter delay. This can be done as follows: trueFilterDelay = chan.ChannelFilterDelay + round(chan.PathDelays(1)/chan.InputSamplePeriod) where chan is the channel object.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Functions returning arrays of complex numbers in Embedded MATLAB or Stateflow may generate incorrect code
Description: An Embedded MATLAB function that returns an array of complex numbers may generate incorrect code when the calling function uses the array once in a single indexing expression. For example, the following code might cause this bug : ...A = foo();% A is a complex matrix.t = A(i); The bug may also occur if the variable has multiple uses, but optimizations usually eliminate all but one of the uses. For example, adding a third line, temp = A(i) , where temp  is never used, will not solve the problem because this line will be eliminated as useless code.
Workaround: Immediately after calling the function, add a conditional that:Is not constantAlways evaluate to falseContains code that assigns a value to the array of complex numbersFor instance, rewrite the above example as follows:...A = foo();if x == y % This condition must be false and not constant  A(:) = 0;endt = A(i);For this workaround to be effective, the if statement must appear in the generated code.

Summary: Installing 64-bit MCR for R2009b fails if computer already has R2009a MCR, displaying could not find MSI file message.
Description: When installing the R2009b 64-bit MCR (v7.11) on a computer that already has the R2009a MCR (v7.10), the MCR installer displays a dialog box indicating that the installation for MCR V7.10 will get updated. However, when you click OK, the MCR installer fails, displaying a message that it could not find the MSI file for MCR v7.11. The only way to install the R2009b 64-bit MCR (7.11) on your computer is to first uninstall the R2009a 64-bit MCR (7.10).
Workaround: To install both versions of the 64-bit MCR on your computer, follow this procedure.Copy the R2009a MCR \v710 subfolder to a temporary location. The default location for the R2009a MCR subfolder is in the MATLAB Compiler Runtime folder in your Program Files folder.Uninstall the R2009a MCR.Install the R2009b MCR. There should be a v711 subfolder in your MATLAB Compiler Runtime folder.Copy the R2009a MCR subfolder (\v710) from its temporary location back into the MATLAB Compiler Runtime folder.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier crashes if the Lookup method of a Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) is set to Interpolation-Extrapolation and the Input/Output interface of the block has different floating-point types.
Description: If the Lookup method of Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) block is set to Interpolation-Extrapolation and the Input/Output interface of the block has different floating-point types (for example, the input signal has type single and the output signal has type double), Simulink Design Verifer crashes when checking compatibility.
Workaround: 1. Back up these files to a location outside the MATLAB path:matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32.csf2. Download the attached files:slavteng.mexw32slavteng.mexw32.csf3. Save the downloaded files in matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv, replacing the existing versions of those files.

Summary: The Measurement Computing PCI-DIO48H digital input and digital output blocks do not work appropriately
Description: The Measurement Computing PCI-DIO48H digital input and digital output blocks do not work appropriately. Running models that contain these blocks cause the target PC to become unresponsive.
Workaround: As a workaround, contact MathWorks Technical Support for updated driver files.

Summary: Model with duplicate "Invoke AUTOSAR Server Operation" blocks does not build
Description: When a model contains multiple "Invoke AUTOSAR Server Operation" blocks with the same Client port name, Operation prototype, and Interface path, the model does not build because a validation check fails with the following error: Last validation failed.Error: the Data Element/Operation "op" of the Interface "/AUTOSAR/Interface" cannot be duplicated for AUTOSAR Port "port"
Workaround: Do the following:Download the attached files.Use these files to replace the following files:matlab/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@exporter/private/p_export_component.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@AutosarInterface/runValidation.pBuild your model.?

Summary: Behavior of Data Store Memory block is not well-defined when the InitialValue is complex but the memory is real
Description: Behavior of the Data Store Memory block is not well-defined when the InitialValue is complex, but the memory is real.  In simulation, the imaginary part of the InitialValue is ignored.  During code generation, the following assertion appears:   [0] libmwsimulink.so:assertion_func(void*, char const*, int, char const*)() + 67 bytes  [1] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::PropagateSizes::propAssign(CG::Node*, RTWCG::RTWCGType&)() + 698 bytes  [2] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::PropagateSizes::propNode(CG::Node*, RTWCG::RTWCGType&)() + 906 bytes  [3] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::PropagateSizes::propRHS(CG::Node*, RTWCG::RTWCGType&)() + 128 bytes  [4] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::PropagateSizes::apply()() + 217 bytes  [5] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWPropagateSizes(CG_Scope*, CG::Scope*)() + 119 bytes
Workaround: One workaround is to set the Signal type of the Data Store Memory block to be real.Another workaround is to use the real function in the InitialValue field, for example, real(IC).

Summary: Simulink Verification and Validation fails to generate requirements report.
Description: Report fails with: Error due to multiple causes: -> Changing the 'isDebug' property of rptgen.coutline is not allowed. -> Error evaluating 'OpenFcn' callback of SubSystem block (mask) 'slvnvdemo_fuelsys_htmreq/Report'. Changing the 'isDebug' property of rptgen.coutline is not allowed.
Workaround: 1) Locate the directory <matlabroot>/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen, where <matlabroot> is the installation directory for MATLAB.2) Move the the file report.p to a backup location that is not on the MATLAB path.3) Copy the attached file report.p into the directory.4) Locate the directory <matlabroot>/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@cfr_paragraph/, where <matlabroot> is the installation directory for MATLAB.5) Move the the file execute.p to a backup location that is not on the MATLAB path.6) Copy the attached file execute.p into the directory.7) Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier crashes if constant values are passed as inputs to Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) blocks where the input or output ports are type single or double.
Description: If the input to a Lookup Table block or any of the inputs to a Lookup Table (2-D) block is a constant, Simulink Design Verifier crashes during checking compatibility of the model. This failure happens only if any of the input or output types of the Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) block are type single or double.
Workaround: 1. Backup these two files to a location outside the MATLAB path:matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32.csf2. Download the attached files:slavteng.mexw32slavteng.mexw32.csf3. Save the downloaded files to matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv, replacing the existing files.

Description: On a Windows OS, PolySpace verification stops during the compilation phase if a tagged private controlled type is derivated The error message is similar to the following: **** Phase 3 (Subprograms access processing - 1/2) took 0.2real, 0.2u + 0s**** Phase 4 (Subprograms access processing - 2/2)**** Phase 4 (Subprograms access processing - 2/2) took 0real, 0u + 0s**** Phase 5 (Tagged Objects Processing)Loading and analyzing ALL\geck_ada_587529Process_Tagged_Objects.Process_Tagged_Objects.process_derived_type_definition : error Node_Variant1433 : {comes_from_source = true, sloc = "test.ada" line 50, column 28, parent = 1439,variant = Derived_Type_Definition{subtype_indication = 1438, record_extension_part = 0}} exception Common_unimplemented(Common_unimplemented) raised.  Here is an example that leads to the error: with Ada.Strings; use Ada.Strings;package Repro ispragma Pure;end Repro;  package Repro.Sequences ispragma Pure;end Repro.Sequences;  with Ada.Finalization;generictype Element is private;type Index_Type is range <>;type Element_Array is array (Index_Type range <>) of Element;with function "="(Left, Right : Element) return Standard.Boolean is <>;with function "="(Left, Right : Element_Array) return Standard.Boolean is <>;  package Repro.Sequences.Unbounded is  Null_Element_Array : Element_Array(1..0);type Sequence is private;Null_Sequence : constant Sequence;type Element_Array_Access is access all Element_Array;function To_Sequence(Source : in Element_Array) return Sequence;  privatetype Sequence is new Ada.Finalization.Controlled with recordLength : Integer := 0;Buffer : Element_Array_Access;end record;Null_Sequence : constant Sequence := (Ada.Finalization.Controlled with 0, null);end Repro.Sequences.Unbounded;  with Repro.Sequences.Unbounded;package Repro.Strings isnew Repro.Sequences.Unbounded (Character, Positive, String, Standard."=", Standard."=");with Repro.Strings;  package Geck_Ada_587529 issubtype Char_Array is Standard.String;type String is new Repro.Strings.Sequence;function To_String (Source : Char_Array) return Geck_Ada_587529.String renames To_Sequence;procedure Main;end Geck_Ada_587529;  package body Geck_Ada_587529 isprocedure main isbeginpragma assert(true);null;end main;end Geck_Ada_587529;  ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows system:1. Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_ADA%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32 2. Create a backup of allthe existing rte-fe*.exe  files in the folder above 3. Download the files '587529_fe*_Windows.zip' in attachment 4. Extract each zip file in %POLYSPACE_ADA%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32

Summary: MATLAB Report Generator fails to generate .rtf and .doc files on machines where the radix point is not a period
Description: MATLAB Report Generator fails to generate .rtf and .doc files on machines where the radix point is not a period. For example, if the locale settings specify 1/2 as "0,5" instead of "0.5", conversion generates these errors: ... dbprint.dsl:227:36:E: quantity "." undefineddbprint.dsl:225:44:E: quantity "." undefined dbprint.dsl:225:39:E: quantity "." undefined dbprint.dsl:225:34:E: quantity "." undefined ...
Workaround: Back up $matlabroot\sys\jade\bin\win32.Extract attached ZIP files to above directory.

Summary: Unable to clear classes after issuing dbquit command from inside event callback function.
Description: After you issue dbquit  from within a function that is a callback for an event, you might not be able to clear the related classes using clear classes . For example, given the following class definitions: % MyClass.m classdef MyClass < handle   properties(SetObservable)      prop   end   events      MyEvent   end   methods      function obj = MyClass()         obj.prop = 1;         addlistener(obj, 'MyEvent', @(src, event)MyClass.respondToEvent(src, event, obj))      end      function meth(obj)         data = MyEventData;         notify(obj, 'MyEvent', data);      end   end   methods(Static)      function respondToEvent(src, event, obj)         disp('Event detected');      end   endend % MyEventData.m classdef MyEventData < event.EventDataend When debugging MyClass , you get a warning message stating that you cannot clear MyEventData  and MyClass . For example, >> a = MyClass;>> dbstop 24 MyClass>> meth(a);24 disp('Event detected');K>> dbquit>> clear classesWarning: Objects of 'MyEventData' class exist. Cannot clear this class or any of its super-classes. Warning: Objects of 'MyClass' class exist. Cannot clear this class or any of its super-classes. ?
Workaround: There is no known workaround.

Summary: Adding a source to a listener when the listener is disabled causes the listener to behave as if it is enabled.
Description: If you add a source to the sources contained in the event.listener  Source  property while the event.listener  Enabled  property is set to false , the listener behaves as if the Enabled  property is set to true . For example, suppose you have an instance, p , of the class, ParentClass , with a property, ChildListener , that contains the handle of an event.listener . Disable the listener: p.ChildListener.Enabled = false; While the listener is disabled, add a new source, h , to the event.listener  Source  property: p.ChildListener.Source{end+1} = h; The listener in the ChildListener  property now behaves as if it is enabled.

Summary: Code generated from AUTOSAR Model with non-scalar Constant block connected to Invoke Server Operation block may not compile
Description: If an AUTOSAR model contains a Constant block that sends a non-scalar signal to an Invoke Server Operation block, then the generated code may fail to compile. The compilation error refers to an attempt to take the address of an address, for example, Rte_Call_port_op1(&&Model_ConstP.C1_Value[0]);
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:Apply a testpoint to the output signal of the Constant block.Place a Signal Conversion block on the output signal of the Constant block. Select the option Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization for the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: MATLAB? may crash while using mldivide to solve large sparse least-squares problems.
Description: When executing x = A\b; where A  is large, sparse, and rectangular, error conditions (such as out of memory) are not properly handled and may result in a crash.
Workaround: Instead of executing x = A\b; consider executing [c,R,E] = qr(A,b,0); y(E,:) = R\c;In cases where a crash might occur, this workaround will properly throw an error. In other cases, y will be the same as x (up to floating point round-off).

Summary: Define Abstract properties as Dependent in superclass if you want to define the property as Dependent in subclass.
Description: The documentation states that subclass implementations of Abstract  superclass properties must preserve the superclass values of the SetAccess  and GetAccess  attributes. However, due to a software defect, MATLAB currently requires you to also preserve the value of the Dependent  property attribute.
Workaround: If you define an Abstract property in a superclass that you want to define as Dependent in a subclass, you must define the property as Abstract and Dependent in the superclass.

Summary: MATLAB? Editor: Emacs key binding for yank (paste) inserts the line of text twice
Description: Under the following circumstances, the Emacs key binding for yank (paste) inserts two copies of the same line, instead of one: You select File > Preferences > Keyboard , and then under Editor/Debugger key bindings , select Emacs . Using key bindings for kill (Ctrl+K ) and yank (Ctrl+Y ), you do the following: kill a line, paste it, kill it again, and then paste it again.  ?

Summary: Function maptrims no longer works properly with north-to-south latitude limits
Description: Prior to R2009b, function maptrims  worked the same whether or not the latitude limits were provided in increasing order (southern limit followed by northern limit), or reversed order (northern limit followed by southern limit). In either case, the smaller value was interpreted as the southern limit and the larger value was interpreted as the northern limit. This changed inadvertently in R2009b, so that only the form in which the southern limit is followed by the northern limit works as expected. Reversing the limits in R2009b results in an output grid without any rows (with size 0-by-N, where N is the number of columns implied by the input grid and the longitude limits).
Workaround: To work around this problem, take care during interactive work to supply a latitude limit vector of the form [southern_limit northern_limit]. In scripts and functions that call maptrims, consider wrapping the latitude limit input in a call to the MATLAB sort function.Even better, download the improved version of maptrims.m available via Bug Report 610010, which includes a fix for this bug as well.

Summary: Uncompilable code from AUTOSAR server operation model with internal states that reference buses where header file is not set to 'Rte_Type.h'
Description: The code generated for your AUTOSAR Server operation model will not compile if the following are all true: Your model has internal states, for example, it contains a unit-delay block with a state attribute. A state attribute references a bus object whose header file property is not set to 'Rte_Type.h' .  You see the following compilation errors: c:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\Rte_Type.h(23) : error C2016: C requires that a struct or union has at least one memberc:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\Rte_Type.h(23) : error C2061: syntax error : identifier 'mystruct'c:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\Rte_Type.h(27) : error C2059: syntax error : '}'c:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\Rte_mbusmodel.h(34) : error C2143: syntax error : missing '{' before '*'mbusmodel.c(22) : error C2143: syntax error : missing ')' before '*'mbusmodel.c(22) : error C2143: syntax error : missing '{' before '*'mbusmodel.c(22) : error C2371: 'real_T' : redefinition; different basic types c:\work\scratch\pp\mbusmodel_autosar_rtw\rtwtypes.h(63) : see declaration of 'real_T'
Workaround: Set the header file of the bus object to 'Rte_Type', for example,mystruct.HeaderFile = 'Rte_Type.h'

Summary: avifile cannot create AVI files with Cinepak compression on Windows? 7 systems
Description: On Windows 7 systems, calls to avifile with the Compression  property set to 'Cinepak'  return an error. For example, running the follwing code: t = linspace(0,2.5*pi,40); fact = 10*sin(t); fig=figure; aviobj = avifile('example.avi', 'Compression', 'Cinepak') [x,y,z] = peaks; for k=1:length(fact)     h = surf(x,y,fact(k)*z);     axis([-3 3 -3 3 -80 80])     axis off     caxis([-90 90])     F = getframe(fig);     aviobj = addframe(aviobj,F); end close(fig) aviobj = close(aviobj); returns the error: ??? Error using ==> avi Can not locate compressor. Error in ==> avifile.addframe at 188       avi('addframe',rot90(frame,-1), aviobj.Bitmapheader, ... Error in ==> u4 at 12       aviobj = addframe(aviobj,F);
Workaround: To workaround this issue, specify 'None' for the compression type. For example:t = linspace(0,2.5*pi,40); fact = 10*sin(t); fig=figure; aviobj = avifile('example.avi', 'Compression', 'None') [x,y,z] = peaks; for k=1:length(fact)     h = surf(x,y,fact(k)*z);     axis([-3 3 -3 3 -80 80])     axis off     caxis([-90 90])     F = getframe(fig);     aviobj = addframe(aviobj,F); end close(fig) aviobj = close(aviobj);

Summary: The file rfa.jar is not automatically added to a deployed .ctf file.
Description: If you create a function that calls Reuters session objects and compile the function into a standalone application using the MATLAB Compiler, the rfa.jar  file is not automatically added to the deployed .ctf file.
Workaround: Explicitly include the following file with your standalone application:$MATLABROOT\java\jarext\rfa.jar where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory and may be determined byusing the command:matlabroot To do this:Copy this file to your project directory from the above mentioned location.If you are generating the application using DEPLOYTOOL, add rfa.jar to the"Other Files" section.If you are using the MCC command to generate the standalone executable, usethe -a option to include the rfa.jar file.The recompiled application should run correctly on a machine that only has theMCR installed.

Summary: H5S.set_extent_simple function fails when specifying default value for h5_maxdims parameter
Description: H5S.set_extent_simple fails when you specify empty brackets ([]) as the value of the h5_maxdims argument. In previous releases, if you specified [] for the h5_maxdims argument, the H5S.set_extent_simple function set the value of this parameter to be the same as the extent of the dataspace.  The following example illustrates this scenario, with the error returned: sid = H5S.create_simple(1,5,[])H5S.set_extent_simple(sid,1,3,[])   ??? Error using ==> hdf5lib2The rank of the dataspace does not correspond to the length of the maxdims argument.Error in ==> set_extent_simple at 41H5ML.hdf5lib2('H5Sset_extent_simple', id, rank, h5_dims, h5_maxdims);
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the following files in your existing MATLAB installation with the updated versions, attached. Follow this procedure:Download and unzip the attached zip file to a temporary folder. Use UNZIP to unzip the zip file.Type matlabroot and mexext at the MATLAB prompt.Quit MATLAB.Change folders into $matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/+H5ML.Backup the file hdf5lib2.mex$mexext. For example, if your computer architecture is win32, move hdf5lib2.mexw32 to hdf5lib2.mexw32.old.Copy the appropriate version of hdf5lib2.mex$mexext from the imagesci/+H5ML folder into the current folder.NOTE: When performing the following steps, take care to copy the correct version of write.m for each package. Several different packages each contain a write function.Change folders into $matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/+H5A and backup write.m to write.m.old.Copy +H5A/write.m from the temporary folder into the current folder.Change folders into $matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/+H5D and backup write.m to write.m.old.Copy +H5D/write.m from the temporary folder into the current folder.Restart MATLAB.Type rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB prompt.This will restore the R2009a behavior.

Summary: Spurious error indexing a constant property with a colon.
Description: In some cases, MATLAB issues an error for an indexed expression of the form c.x(..., :, ...), where c is the name of a class. Instead, MATLAB should treat c.x as a reference to a property of the class.
Workaround: Avoid using ':' as an index directly on c.x. For example, one could write:    tmp = c.x;        % Obtain the value of the property.   tmp(..., :, ...)  % Do the indexing.

Summary: Colon indexing involving structs with multiple left hands sides may cause MATLAB to crash.
Description: Colon indexing involving structs with multiple left hands sides may cause MATLAB to crash. For example,function bugx.s = zeros(10);for k = 1:1    [a b] = x(k).s(:);end
Workaround: Disable the JIT using "feature scopedAccelEnablement off" in the function causing difficulty. For example,function bugfeature scopedAccelEnablement off;x.s = zeros(10);for k = 1:1    [a b] = x(k).s(:);endIf this is not possible, the JIT can be disabled entirely using "feature accel off".

Summary: In Discrete 1st-Order Filter block, setting the Sample time parameter to -1 yields invalid simulation results
Description: The Discrete 1st-Order Filter block, from the Extra Library/Discrete Control Blocks sublibrary, cannot have its Sample time  parameter set to -1  (inherited sample time). This is a valid limitation; however, the block does not detect this limitation. Setting Sample time  to -1  generates incorrect filter coefficients, which results in invalid simulation.

Summary: Compressed images cannot be saved as MAT-files using True Compression 2-D Tool
Description: If you attempt to save a compressed image as a MAT file using the True Compression 2-D Tool, the file is not saved.
Workaround: For releases R2009a and R2009b, save the attachment file wc2dtool.m to toolbox/wavelet/wavelet.For release R2008b, save both attachment files, wc2dtool.m and getimgfiletype.m, to toolbox/wavelet/wavelet.

Summary: Custom library cannot use Signal Latch block in a subsystem
Description: If you create a library containing the Signal Latch block inside a subsystem, and copy the library block from your library to a model, then you might see these effects: Incorrect results during simulation. A warning like the following during model compilation:Warning: Unconnected output line found on 'mymodel/My Subsystem/Signal Latch' (output port: 1)
Workaround: Rename the following file by appending .old to the file name: $MATLAB\toolbox\des\desmasks\des_sgnlatchmask.m$MATLAB is the MATLAB root folder on your machine, as returned by entering this command in the MATLAB Command Window: matlabrootDownload the attached file, des_sgnlatchmask.m, to the $MATLAB\toolbox\des\desmasks folder.In the MATLAB Command Window, enter: rehash toolboxYou can now create libraries or use your library blocks as usual.

Summary: Assigning floating point numbers that are exactly halfway between two integers into to an array of integer types may yield the wrong result.
Description: Assigning floating point numbers that are exactly halfway between two integers into to an array of integer types may yield the wrong result, e.g.function c = assignTestc = zeros('int32');c(1) = 2.5;   % 0.5, 2.5, etc.>> assignTestans =    2      % Should be 3.
Workaround: Disable the JIT using "feature jit off".

Summary: Runnable within a multirunnable SWC with only Invoke AUTOSAR Server Operation block does not appear in XML file
Description: When a runnable within a multirunnable software component contains only an Invoke AUTOSAR Server Operation block, the runnable will not appear in the generated model_behavior .arxml file.
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:Add the internal behavior of the runnable using an AUTOSAR authoring toolAdd an inport or outport to the runnable

Summary: PPMAK -- Coefficients are not resized for univariate spline
Description: Sometimes the coefficients in a univariate spline created by PPMAK  don't have the right shape even though you specified the sizec  argument. For example this command, gives the wrong result:   pp1 = ppmak( 0:2, 1:6, [2,3]);  The size of the resulting coefficients, pp1.coefs , is not [2, 3].
Workaround: To work around this problem, do one of the following.Do not specify the sizec argument. Instead, specify the coefs as a row, e.g.,   pp1 = ppmak( breaks, coefs )Reshape the coefficients before passing to PMAK, e.g.,   pp1 = ppmak( breaks, reshape( coefs, sizec ), sizec )   pp1 = ppmak( 0:2, reshape( 1:6, [2, 3] ), [2, 3] )

Summary: Resimulating a model in Accelerator mode after deleting a Stateflow chart or MATLAB Function block from the model causes MATLAB to stop responding
Description: If a Simulink model contains any combination of two or more Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks, MATLAB will crash after the following series of actions: The model is simulated in Accelerator mode. One of the charts or blocks is deleted. The model is again simulated in Accelerator mode.
Workaround: As a workaround, after deleting a Stateflow chart or MATLAB Function block and before rerunning a simulation in Accelerator mode:In the MATLAB Command Window, type clear mex.In the current working folder, delete the previously generated accelerator mex file (modelName_acc.mexext, for example vdp_acc.mexw32).Rerun the simulation in Accelerator mode.

Summary: Simulink Printing ignores library blocks with inactive link status
Description: Simulink printing ignores library blocks with inactive link status.
Workaround: Back up matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/simprintdlg.m.Get appriopriate version of simprintdlg.m.Place renamed file at above directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Version R2009b of deploytool doesn't allow export of functions from added external C/C++ files
Description: When C/C++ files are added to the Other Files  section, they get packaged into the CTF archive instead of being compiled along with the generated code. This results in the functions in C/C++ code not being available as an exported function in the compiled application.

Summary: Vertical Eye Opening measurement for eye diagrams fails when the channel impairment on the signal is small
Description: The eye diagram object, commscope.eyediagram, can perform many eye diagram measurements including vertical eye opening. When the analyze method of this object is called for eye diagrams that have a narrow vertical histogram at the eye center, you might see the following error message: ??? Error using ==> calcEyeOpeningVer at 108 The analyze method cannot determine vertical eye opening. The collected data may not be valid. See documentation for description of valid eye diagrams.
Workaround: 1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Rename the dualDiracTailFitting.m file in your current MATLAB installation to another name, such as dualDiracTailFitting.m.old. This file is in MATLABROOT\toolbox\comm\comm\@commscope\@eyemeasurements\private, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by using the matlabroot function at the MATLAB command prompt.3. Download the attached file dualDiracTailFitting.m to the directory in step 2.4. Restart MATLAB. 5. Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache
