Summary: Current Folder browser can fail to show all of the contents of a folder containing a broken symbolic link on Unix and Macintosh platforms
Description: If the Current Folder Browser detects a broken symbolic link when reading the contents of a folder, it stops reading the remainder of the listing for that folder. Since the file system does not provide the list in any particular order (even when the view is sorted), any number of items in the folder may fail to display, and which items are shown may change from one attempt to another, especially when the contents of the folder are being modified.
Workaround: You can only use this workaround on the R2009b release of MATLAB. Download the appropriate patch for your platform. Versions are available for Maci32, Maci64, glnx86, and glnx64. Install the patch by placing and expanding the downloaded archive in your MATLAB root directory, as follows: In MATLAB, run the matlabroot command to determine where MATLAB is installed.In MATLAB, run the computer command. Download the attached .tar file whose prefix matches the output of the computer command. Save the .tar file in the location indicated by the matlabroot command.Exit MATLAB and open a terminal, such as the Mac Terminal application or XTerm.In the terminal, navigate to the location where you saved the .tar file and extract it using the command tar -xf <filename>

Summary: classregtree/eval can error out or return an incorrect value if SUBTREES argument is non-scalar
Description: If you call  [YFIT,NODE,CNUM] = EVAL(T,X,SUBTREES)   or  [YFIT,NODE] = EVAL(T,X,SUBTREES)   and supply a non-scalar SUBTREES argument, the node numbers for the first subtree NODE(:,1) are filled incorrectly due to an indexing bug. This causes classregtree/eval to error out if one of the node numbers gets to be zero or return an incorrect value if one of the node numbers gets to be an incorrect valid value between 1 and the number of nodes in the tree. This in turn results in incorrect values of YFIT and CNUM for these bad nodes.This affects only the first subtree. NODE(:,2:end) is always filled correctly. If SUBTREES is scalar (default), NODE is filled correctly.
Workaround: Replace lines 74-75nodes = classregtreeEval(X',Tree.node,Tree.var,Tree.cut,Tree.children,...    prunelist,subtrees);in classregtree/eval.m with the following:Nsub = numel(subtrees);nodes = zeros(Nsub,nr);for nsub=1:Nsub    nodes(nsub,:) = classregtreeEval(X',Tree.node,Tree.var,Tree.cut,Tree.children,...        prunelist,subtrees(nsub));end

Summary: When generating C++ shared library on the Mac using deploytool, install_name is set to a fully qualified path
Description: When generating a C++ shared library using the Deployment Tool on the Mac, install_name  is set to a fully qualified path. This happens because deploytool  fully qualifies the output name of the shared library during the link step and the install_name  option is not used.
Workaround: Use the the Macintosh install_name_tool, on the subsequently created executable, to corret the names of libraries with fully qualified paths.For instance, if the shared library named mylib.dylib is linked into an executable named myapp, issue the following command:install_name_tool -change <lib_install_name> @loader_path/mylib.dylib ./mylib.dylib<lib_install_name> can be determined by executing otool against the shared library as follows:otool -L ./mylib.dylib Note: Both install_name_tool and otool must be invoked from a command line from within the terminal application.

Summary: Publishing to PDF uses old versions of images after changes, instead of new ones
Description: If you publish a MATLAB? script to PDF and then change the script (for example, to change a figure's title) and then publish to PDF again, the images generated by the script do not update. The problem is that MATLAB caches the old images and does not update them, even though they have changed.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Each time you change the file, rename it before publishing it. This way, MATLAB gives the image files that the script creates different names each time you publish the script.Type edit /private/evalmxdom.m in the Command Window, and then in the Editor, add the following at line 20, just after [data,text,laste] = ... and then save the file:if strcmp(options.format,'pdf')    addRandom = @(x)sprintf('%s#%.0f',x,randi(1e10));    data.pictureList = cellfun(addRandom,data.pictureList,'UniformOutput',false); end?

Summary: LOADLIBRARY does not process typedefs with void pointers correctly in MATLAB 7.9 (R2009b).
Description: typedef  statements involving void and pointers (*) or arrays of void * lose one or more levels of indirection. For example, a library mylib  has the following header file myheader : typedef void* myType;int myFun(myType *a);  When you load mylib : loadlibrary('mylib','myheader'); libfunctions libmylib -full MATLAB creates the following signature: Functions in library libmylib: [int32, voidPtr] myFun(voidPtr) The correct signature is: Functions in library libmylib:[int32, voidPtrPtr] myFun(voidPtrPtr)
Workaround: Two workarounds are:Modify the header file to remove the typedef statement.Generate a prototype MATLAB file that can be edited to fix the broken types.Alternatively, you can install the attached file to restore R2009a functionality. To install this patch:Back up matlab/toolbox/matlab/general/private/prototypes.pl.Download prototypes_R2009b.zip.Extract prototypes.pl to the above directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Unexpected simulation results for a Simscape model using a local solver with small sample time and without fixed-cost consistency iterations
Description: For a Simscape model, unexpected simulation results might be computed when you use the local solver without fixed-cost consistency iterations, and the local solver sample time is very small (for example, less than 1e-7).
Workaround: To work around the issue, you can either decrease Consistency tolerance in the Solver Configuration block, or enable Use fixed-cost runtime consistency iterations and set Nonlinear iterations to be appropriate value.

Summary: checkEnvSetup function errors out when checking version number of NDK 2.00.00
Description: If you have installed NDK 2.00.00 from Texas Instruments, entering checkEnvSetup('ccs', 'c6747evm') on the MATLAB command line generates the following error message when checking the version number of the NDK installation: ??? Error using ==> times Matrix dimensions must agree. Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p>actualVersionLower at 618 Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p>isVersionCompatible at 585 Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p>envSetup at 205 Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p>checkEnvSetup_private at 68 ? Error in ==> checkEnvSetup at 131 checkEnvSetup_private(varargin); ?
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_584966.zip. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_584966.zip', 'install')Follow instructions printed out on the MATLAB command line to complete the installation. The attached patch modifies the following file in your MATLAB installation:matlab\toolbox\idelink\extensions\ticcs\envChecker\checkEnvSetup_private.p

Summary: Creating codistributed/NaN or codistributed/Inf matrix generates an error
Description: The documentation presents incorrect and unsupported mixed letter casing for the following methods: codistributed/Infcodistributed/NaNdistributed/Infdistributed/NaN The correct letter casing for these methods is all lower-case.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following supported, lower-case names for these methods:codistributed/inf codistributed/nan distributed/inf distributed/nan

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during 'linking C++ phase' with the message: "identifier is undefined"
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on C++ code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: *** Beginning C++ Linking Phase ... pst_file_main0.c, line [line number]: error: identifier "__intern_rand_yy" is undefined ...  The identifier involved is always an internal variable called '__intern_rand_yy ', where 'yy ' is a number. ?

Summary: A function with too many logical and conditional operators can cause scaling problems
Description: If a function contains a large number of logical and conditional operators (if, &&, ||...), it can lead to scaling problems in level 0. The verification will be stuck in level 0, phase P_PP : **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 13 (P_CG)**** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 13 (P_CG) took 0.5real, 0.4u + 0.1s (0gc)**** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 14 (P_R)**** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 14 (P_R) took 0.2real, 0.2u + 0s (0gc)**** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 15 (P_PP) ?
Workaround: ?If a function prevents verification from completing (and generating results), you can simply remove the definition of the function, and let PolySpace stub it automatically.?

Summary: Configuring multiple AUTOSAR runnables may fail
Description: Configuring multiple AUTOSAR runnables may fail if all of the following apply: You have not previously configured the runnables. The wrapper subsystem contains a Merge block that combines the outputs of the runnables. You select the button "Get Default Configuration".  As a result of the failure, you see an error message similar to the following: 'Model /Subystem ' is being invoked with multiple asynchronous rates that cannot be combined. To combine multiple asynchronous rates, assign the same priority to the rates and ensure that all rates use the base rate timer.
Workaround: One workaround is as follows:Before you configure the runnables for the first time, delete the Merge block.If the model will no longer compile, continue to make minimal, temporary modifications until you are able to compile it.Configure the multiple runnables, and click Apply or OK.Restore the Merge block and undo any other temporary model modifications that you made.Once you have carried out an initial configuration of the runnables, the issue no longer arises.

Summary: Priority Queue block might cause the application to crash
Description: The application may crash when the entity at the head of a priority queue and a newly arriving entity have the same arrival time and the same value of the sorting attribute.
Workaround: Modify the model to ensure that entities simultaneously in the Priority Queue block have different values of the sorting attribute. One approach is to perturb the sorting attribute values enough to produce distinct values, but not enough to change the entity sequence in the queue.Example of WorkaroundThe attached files illustrate the crash and the workaround. To observe the crash, use Release R2007b through R2009b. The original model uses TimeStamp as the sorting attribute. The workaround model differs from the original model in these ways:An Entity Departure Counter block assigns a Count attribute to each entity.An Attribute Function block uses the Count attribute to perturb the value of the TimeStamp attribute.The Priority Queue block sorts entities based on the perturbed value.The following excerpt of code from the Attribute Function block shows the perturbation algorithm. In your own models, you might need to modify the algorithm or tune the values of the MaxDistance and SmallPerturbation variables.function out_TimeStampWA  = fcn(TimeStamp, Count)% Choose MaxDistance based on what you expect to be the maximum difference % between the Count values of a newly arriving entity and a queue head.MaxDistance = 100;% Choose SmallPerturbation to satisfy these two requirements:% 1) Large enough to distinguish TimeStampWA of different entities% 2) Small enough that using TimeStampWA does not change the order of% entities in the queueSmallPerturbation = 10^(-4);% TimeStampWA -- a new attributeout_TimeStampWA = TimeStamp+mod(Count,MaxDistance)*SmallPerturbation;

Summary: Embedded MATLAB reports upper bounds errors when you define variable-size arrays in dead code.
Description: When you define a variable-size array in dead code and do not specify the upper bound explicitly using eml.varsize , Embedded MATLAB generates an unknown upper bounds  error. Dead code is code that the compiler determines will not be executed. Inconsistent assertions or if  conditions sometimes introduce dead code.
Workaround: Delete dead code from your MATLAB function.

Summary: When using a Matrox Solios XA or Helios XA, the device-specific InputGain property does not always change the acquired frames.
Description: When using a Matrox Solios XA or Helios XA, the device-specific InputGain property does not always change the acquired frames. For example, if I change the InputGain while previewing, then the InputGain seems to change the image. However, if InputGain is changed between acquisitions and while not previewing there does not seem to be a difference between the acquisitions.
Workaround: Follow these steps to replace the Matrox adaptors.Quit MATLAB?.Rename the matrox7.dll and matrox8.dll files in your current MATLAB installation to another name, such as matrox7.old and matrox8.old. These files are in MATLABROOT\toolbox\imaq\imaqadaptors\win32\, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by using the matlabroot function at the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached file and unzip the files to the directory in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: The open(model) function fails if the fullpath to the model contains an apostrophe (')
Description: If the fullpath to the model file specified to the open  function contains an apostrophe (single quote), executing: ????open(model)  causes an error: ????Unexpected MATLAB expression  For example, if the fullpath to a model is: ????/John's Models/mymodel.mdl  executing: ????open('mymodel')  causes the error to occur. The state of the model is unchanged.
Workaround: Several workarounds exist:Use open_system('mymodel')Double-click on the model file to open itEliminate all single quotes in the fullpath, substituting anything legal or nothing?

Summary: Building a read-only project file in the Deployment Tool, in version R2009b, hangs MATLAB.
Description: When trying to compille a readonly .prj  file in deploytool , an empty window with the title Save Failed  appears and MATLAB appears to hang.  You must use the Windows Task Manager to kill the matlab.exe  process.
Workaround: Compile the project using:mcc -F project_name.prj?

Summary: Reaction rate is incorrect for reversible mass action reactions rates in concentration units with non-unit stoichiometric coefficients
Description: SimBiology version 3.1 incorrectly calculates the reaction rate for reactions that meet all of the following criteria: The kinetic law is Mass Action The reaction is reversible The reaction rate is in concentration (which is determined by dimensional analysis of the reaction rate constants and species dimensions) The reaction is written with reactants or products with stoichiometric coefficients that are not equal to 1.
Workaround: NOTE: This fix only applies to customers running SimBiology R2009b version 3.1.Option 1: Change the reaction string to use only stoichiometric coefficients with a value of 1. For example, change the reaction "2 X <-> Y" to "X + X <-> Y".Option 2: Download an updated version of the file odebuilder.p:Find the $MATLABROOT directory where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB? root directory on your machine, as returned by typing:     matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file odebuilder.p to odebuilder.p.old.This file is located in    $MATLABROOT/toolbox/simbio/simbio/privateDownload and unzip the attached file for your system.Place the new file, odebuilder.p, in the same directory mentioned in step 3.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Linearizing a model with a linearization I/O at the output of a block with a space at the end of its name results in an error
Description: When you use the linearize  command to linearize a model with a linearization I/O located at the output of a block with a space at the end of its name, you receive this error: ??? Too many outputs requested. Most likely cause is missing [] aroundleft hand side that has a comma separated list expansion. Error in ==> slcontrol.Utilities.getUniqueBlockName at 66name = unblks{blk_ind};
Workaround: Rename the file getUniqueBlockName.m to getUniqueBlockName_old.m. This file is located in the folder [matlabroot]\toolbox\shared\slcontrollib\@slcontrol\@Utilities\. To locate the MATLAB? installation directory [matlabroot], type matlabroot at the MATLAB? command prompt.Download the attached file getUniqueBlockName.m and save it to the same directory.Restart MATLAB?.

Summary: matlabFunction produces incorrect results for factored symbolic expressions.
Description: matlabFunction produces incorrect results for factored symbolic expressions. For example, the following commands %% declare sym objectssyms x y%% create 'Factored' expressions 'F' and 'G'F = feval(symengine, 'factor', x^3-x^2)G = y*F%% create MATLAB function from 'G'matlabFunction(G, 'file', 'outputFile.m')%% view resultedit 'outputFile.m' produce the output file outputFile.m  function G = outputFile(x,y)...G = CF(x.^2.*y.*(x - 1), 0, \"double\"); with an incorrect function call CF(..., \"double\") . The same bug shows up when you call the MuPAD symengine code generator functions generate::C , generate::fortran  and generate::MATLAB   directly.
Workaround: Before creating a MATLAB function, call the MuPAD symengine function expr to convert any factored expression to a plain expression:%% create MATLAB function from 'G' by calling 'expr' firstG = feval(symengine, 'expr', G)matlabFunction(G, 'file', 'outputFile.m')The correct results is:function G = outputFile(x,y)...G = x.^2.*y.*(x-1.0);?

Summary: Attempting to assign a value to a property of an empty object returned from a class constructor can cause MATLAB to crash during array expansion.
Description: Given a class whose constructor returns an empty instance (using the class's static empty  ), using indexed assignment to expand an array of instances of this class while assigning a value to a property of the array can cause MATLAB to crash. For example, given a class MyClass  whose constructor returns an empty instance, creating an instance of the class creates a MATLAB object array of type MyClass  and size 0x0 : >> o = MyClass Indexed assignment that expands the array will call the class constructor, which returns another empty instance, so the size of object array o  remains 0x0 : >> o(1) = MyClass If array expansion is performed while at the same time assigning a value to a property of the array, MATLAB can crash: >> o(1).prop = 1;
Workaround: Do not return an empty object from a class constructor. A class constructor should always return a valid instance of the class.

Summary: When solving linear systems using mldivide (\) and ctranspose (') together (as A'\b), MATLAB? may return incorrect results if A is a permutation of a triangular matrix.
Description: When solving a linear system that involves mldivide  and the conjugate transpose of a matrix A  (as A'\b ), MATLAB? returns an incorrect result if the matrix A  meets all of the following conditions: A  is square and full, A  is not a scalar, A  is a permutation of a triangular matrix, A  is not a lower-triangular matrix, A  is not an upper-triangular matrix.
Workaround: To work around the issue, perform the conjugate transpose of the matrix separately from the division. Change:x = A'\b;toAt = A';x = At\b;

Summary: Enabling code cache generates error in VisualDSP++[Error li1074] ".\<model>":252 Unknown memory segment 'MEM_SDRAM0_BANK3' used in expression "MEMORY_END(MEM_SDRAM0_BANK3) - 75"?
Description: Enabling the L1_Code_CACHE  for a project with a Blackfin processor generates an error similar to: [Error li1074] ".\test_533_memsec_ldf.ldf":252 Unknown memory segment 'MEM_SDRAM0_BANK3' used in expression "MEMORY_END(MEM_SDRAM0_BANK3) - 75"
Workaround: To fix this issue:Download the attached patch_installer.m and patch_583343.zip.To install the patch, enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt :patch_installer('patch_583343.zip', 'install')Follow instructions printed out on the MATLAB command line to complete the installation.The attached patch modifies the following file in your MATLAB installation:toolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF522_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF522_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF523_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF523_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF524_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF524_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF525_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF525_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF526_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF526_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF527_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF527_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF531_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF531_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF532_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF532_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF533_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF533_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF534_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF534_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF536_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF536_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF537_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF537_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF538_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF538_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF539_ppd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/registry/BF539_spd.mattoolbox/idelink/extensions/adivdsp/blackfin/blackfin_ldf.tlc

Summary: Matrix multiplication (mtimes) may return incorrect results on certain 32-bit processors when MATLAB? is set to single-threaded mode.
Description: When MATLAB? is set to use a single computational thread, mtimes  may return an incorrect result on certain 32-bit processors. To determine if your processor may be affected by this: Start MATLAB using the -singleCompThread  startup flag. Execute the following commands:a = rand(2000);b = rand(2000);oneThread = a*b;save wrkspace;  Exit MATLAB and restart MATLAB normally (without using the -singleCompThread  startup flag). Execute the following commands in the new session:load wrkspace;autoThread = a*b;tol = 100*eps(class(autoThread))*size(a,1) %use a reasonable tolerancenumericallyCorrect = max(abs(oneThread(:)-autoThread(:))) < tol   If the value of numericallyCorrect  is not 1 (true ), this bug may be present for your processor.
Workaround: Contact technical support to obtain a patch that can resolve this bug.https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html

Summary: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder might fail to generate code for S-Function block under specific configuration
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder might fail to generate code for a model containing a S-function block and you select the following configuration parameters: Simulink block descriptions  on the Real-Workshop > Comments  pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog box Eliminate superflous local variables (Expression folding)  on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog box  The following error message displays: Error: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\mw\commentlib.tlc Line: 405 Column: 121Undefined identifier origblockError: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\mw\commentlib.tlc Line: 54 Column: 65Undefined identifier blockError: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\mw\commentlib.tlc Line: 187 Column: 27Undefined identifier blockError: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 2339 Column: 15 Undefined identifier block
Workaround: Clear one of the following configuration parameters:Simulink block descriptions on the Real-Workshop > Comments pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog boxEliminate superflous local variables (Expression folding) on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameter dialog box

Summary: The +/-2 and +/-1 volt output ranges of the National Instruments PCI-628x boards do not generate output
Description: The +/-2 and +/-1 volt output ranges of the National Instruments PCI-628x boards do not generate output. Contrary to some of the National Instruments documentation for these boards, these output ranges do not exist in the hardware. Attempts to use these ranges for these boards generate no output.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code for model containing Sign block might produce incorrect numerical results
Description: Executing the generated code for a model might produce incorrect numerical results if all of the following conditions exist in the model: The model contains a Sign block. The output type of the Sign block is an integer type for which the size exceeds the integer size displayed in the Hardware Implementation  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box (for example, output type int32  versus integer size 16 bits). The output of the Sign block feeds a Data Type Conversion block that converts the input integer to a fixed-point integer with a fraction length greater than the integer size displayed in the Hardware Implementation  pane (for example, fraction length 17 bits versus integer size 16 bits).  Under these conditions, building the model might generate code that contains a shift left of more bits than the target integer number of bits. This operation is undefined by the C language.

Summary: Digital Filter block produces incorrect results in some fixed-point modes when the leading denominator coefficient does not equal 1
Description: In R2009b and earlier releases, the Digital Filter block produces incorrect results when the leading denominator coefficient, a0 , does not equal 1 and the following conditions are met: The block is operating in a fixed-point mode. The block operates in a fixed-point mode when the Fixed-point instrumentation mode  of the Fixed-Point Tool is set to "Minimums, maximums and overflows , or the input to the block has a fixed-point or integer data type. The Coefficient source  is set to Dialog  or Input port(s) . If the Coefficient source  is set to Input port(s) , the block produces incorrect results when the First denominator coefficient =1, remove a0 term in the structure  check box is unchecked. The Transfer function type  and Filter structure  parameters are set to one of the following combinations:          o IIR (poles & zeros) - Direct form I         o IIR (poles & zeros) - Direct form I transposed         o IIR (poles & zeros) - Direct form II         o IIR (poles & zeros) - Direct form II transposed         o IIR (all poles) - Direct form         o IIR (all poles) - Direct form transposed   Similarly, the Digital Filter block produces incorrect results if the above conditions are met and the leading denominator coefficient is tuned from 1 to any non-unity value during simulation.
Workaround: To workaround this issue and ensure the Digital Filter block produces correct results, set the leading denominator coefficient to 1. To do so, scale all numerator and denominator coefficients by a factor of a0.

Summary: Creating analog input objects on National Instruments USB-9229 and USB-9239 devices causes errors
Description: When you use National Instruments USB-9229, USB-9229 (BNC), USB-9239 or USB-9239 (BNC) and create an analog input object you will see the following error: ai=analoginput('nidaq','Dev1')  ??? Error using ==> analoginput.analoginput at 121NIDAQmx error : Measurements: Task contains physical channels on one or moredevices that require you to specify the Sample Clock Rate. Specify a SampleClock Rate. Task Name: MWDAT0002 Status Code: -201087   ?
Workaround: To work around this issue modify the entries for these devices in the mwnidaqmx.ini file.1. In the MATLAB Command Window type:matlabrootand record the location of the MATLAB root.2. Exit MATLAB.3. In any text editor open the following file:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwnidaqmx.ini4. Find the section for each of the devices USB-9229, USB-9229 (BNC), USB-9239 and USB-9239 (BNC) and add the following line:requiresRateSetBeforeOps = trueFor example, the new entry for the USB-9229 device should now read:[USB-9229]hasAnalogInput = truenumAIChannels = 4requiresRateSetBeforeOps = truehasAnalogOutput = falsehasDigitalIO = false5. Save mwnidaqmx.ini and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Highlighting of Stateflow simulation initial states can fail
Description: The active state in the Stateflow simulation is not highlighted when both these conditions apply: Chart property "Execute (enter) Chart At Initialization" is enabled. No transition is taken after the initial states are entered.
Workaround: Back up matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private/remove_hilite.mDownload the attached zip file and extract it to matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private/remove_hilite.m

Summary: findpeaks misses peaks when MinPeakHeight and MinPeakDistance are specified
Description: When findpeaks  is called with a specified MinPeakHeight  and MinPeakDistance , it may miss small peaks next to a big peak. For example, for an input sequence of x = [0 3 2 0 0 1 0 0 1 0] , the function call of [pk loc] = findpeaks(x,'minpeakheight',0,'minpeakdistance',2)  returns loc = [2 9] , missing the small peak in location 6 .

Summary: Conversion of MATLAB arrays of 4 or more dimensions into .NET arrays is not correct.
Description: When the NET.convertArray  function is used to convert a MATLAB array of 4 or more dimensions into a .NET array, the resulting .NET array elements are not correctly aligned across the array dimensions.

Summary: boxplot labels do not remain in the correct position after loading a figure from a file
Description: If you use the boxplot  function to create a box plot, save the figure to a .fig  file, and then open the .fig  file again, the box labels do not remain in the correct position. If you re-size the figure window, the axes stretch to fit the new size, but the labels remain in their original positions.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during compilation with the message 'error: comment unclosed at end of file'
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on C source code that includes Japanese characters in comments, the verification stops during syntax and semantic checking phase with the following message in the log file: ...Verifying myCfile.cC:\PolySpace\sources\myCfile.c, line X: error: comment unclosed at end of file------------------------------------------------------------------- ------ Verifier has detected preprocessing error(s) in the code. ------ Please correct them and launch the analysis again. ------ --------------------------------------------------------------------   Opening preprocessed file myCfile.ci   located in <results folder>\C-ALL   shows that file ends in the middle of a C code comment.
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32Create a backup of the file edgcpfe.c.x86-mingw32.exeDownload the file 'cpfe_581927.zip' in attachmentExtract edgcpfe.c.x86-mingw32.exe from zip file and copy it in %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/x86-linuxCreate a backup of the file edgcpfe.c.x86-linuxDownload the file 'cpfe_581927.zip' in attachmentExtract edgcpfe.c.x86-linux from zip file and copy it in $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/x86-linuxTo workaround this issue on a Solaris system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/sparc-solarisCreate a backup of the file edgcpfe.c.sparc-solarisDownload the file 'cpfe_581927.zip' in attachmentExtract edgcpfe.c.sparc-solaris from zip file and copy it in $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/sparc-solaris

Summary: Compiled applications using Image Processing Toolbox may not deploy on Windows
Description: Some Image Processing Toolbox functions compiled on Windows in R2009b cause errors when running as deployed applications. Sample error messages include: ??? Invalid MEX-file ... imfilter_mex.mexw32 The specified module could not be found.  and ippl.mexw32 not found.  These errors may be caused by the following libraries missing from the deployed archive: $MATLAB/bin/$ARCH/ippl.dll  $MATLAB/bin/$ARCH/iptutil.dll  $MATLAB/bin/$ARCH/neighborhood.dll
Workaround: To workaround this bug, you will need to make a backup of the mcc.enc file in $MATLAB/toolbox/images/ and copy the attached version of mcc.enc to $MATLAB/toolbox/images/To apply a patched version of mcc.enc, please do the following:Copy $MATLAB/toolbox/images/mcc.enc to $MATLAB/toolbox/images/mcc.enc_bkpDownload the attached file and extract mcc.enc, copy it to $MATLAB/toolbox/images/mcc.enc$MATLAB refers to the root directory of your MATLAB installation. Once this patch is applied, mcc should now include the appropriate library dependencies in deployed applications. Please recompile and redistribute any application that is affected by this bug. Note that this patch should ONLY be applied to R2009b installations of MATLAB on Windows platforms. Older versions and non-Windows platforms are not affected by this bug.

Summary: Traceability report generation fails when name of DUT includes newline character
Description: When the device under test (DUT) subsystem name includes a newline character, and HDL code generation is initiated with traceability on, the coder will display the following error message: Traceability information has not been generated for model "...". Turn on 'Model-to-code navigation' and rebuild model" This happens only when code generation with traceability on is initiated from the GUI. ?
Workaround: To work around this problem, do either of the following:Remove the newline charcater from the DUT subsystem name.ORInitiate code generation from the MATLAB command line as follows:makehdl(gcb, 'traceability, 'on')???

Summary: Performance regression in los2, viewshed, and other functions that accept referencing vectors as input
Description: You may notice that los2 , viewshed , and other functions that accept referencing vectors as input take longer to run in R2009b than in R2009a. This is an unintended side effect of an R2009b enhancement that extended such functions to accept referencing matrices in addition to referencing vectors.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the following six files in your current installation:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/los2.mmatlabroot/toolbox/map/map/viewshed.mmatlabroot/toolbox/map/map/ltln2val.mmatlabroot/toolbox/map/map/private/convertToGeoRasterRef.mmatlabroot/toolbox/map/map/+internal/+maputils/GeoRasterRefKernel.mmatlabroot/toolbox/map/map/+internal/+maputils/IntrinsicRaster2D.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your R2009b MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. Proceed as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make backup copies of each of the six files listed above. For example, rename the existing version of los2.m, adding "old" to the file extension: los2.m.oldDownload and decompress the attached file, Bug581492.zip, and then copy its contents (files los2.m, viewshed.m, ltln2val.m, convertToGeoRasterRef.m, GeoRasterRefKernel.m, and IntrinsicRaster2D.m) to the directories listed above.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?In addition to mitigating performance loss, this workaround completely resolves the important numerical problem with the ltln2val function and functions that depend on it (including los2 and viewshed) covered in Bug Report 598579.As of June 4, 2010, it also resolves the problem (incorrect errors on certain referencing matrix input) described in Bug Report 613079.

Summary: Running out of memory during Real-Time Workshop code generation might cause a SegV
Description: If Real-Time Workshop runs out of memory while generating code for a large model, it might not report the out of memory condition. Instead, a segmentation fault might occur and cause the MATLAB session to close.

Summary: Segmentation violation when mapping shift-left operator output to replacement function input argument using custom target function library
Description: During code generation for a model that contains a shift-left operation, the software might generate a segmentation violation if all of the following conditions exist: You registered a shift-left replacement function in a custom target function library (TFL) and selected the TFL for the model. In the TFL table entry, you specified that the shift-left replacement function takes the output as a function argument, for example, "myShiftLeft(u1, &y1); ". The shift-left operation in the model meets the TFL match criteria and triggers the replacement.

Summary: UDP blocks do not work when target PCs are configured to use multicore processors
Description: UDP blocks do not work when target PCs are configured to use multicore processors. In these situations, these blocks cannot send or receive data.
Workaround: As a workaround, perform one of the following:Disable multicore processor support and use single core processor support.Contact MathWorks technical support for an R2009b (xPC Target Version 4.2) softwware patch.

Summary: MATLAB? Profile report for scripts: coverage results section may be erroneous?
Description: When profiling scripts,  the MATLAB? Profiler may provide erroneous coverage results because it treats executable lines as blank lines.
Workaround: Replace the getcallinfo.m file, as follows:Determine the location of the MATLAB? root folder on your machine, by issuing the matlabroot function in the Command Window.Exit MATLAB.Navigate to the MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\codetools folder (where MATLABROOT is the folder returned in step 1), rename getcallinfo.m to another name (such as getcallinfo.m.old), and then download the attached zip file and unzip getcallinfo.m file to this folder.Restart MATLAB.Issue the following command in the MATLAB Command window: rehash toolboxcache.

Summary: MATLAB appears frozen when entering invalid value for axes limits in the Property Editor on Mac OS X 10.6
Description: Validating axis limits in Property Editor may result in an unresponsive MATLAB. On a Mac OS X 10.6, edit axes limits by entering:  plot(1:10); propertyeditor;   Select the axes and enter a period as the lower X limit and press TAB . MATLAB appears to be frozen but isn't, because hitting a TAB  key triggers input validation and displays a modal error dialog. This dialog is hidden behind the Plot Tools window and the only way to dismiss it is to press ESC  key.
Workaround: Press ESC key to dismiss the hidden error dialog.

Summary: registerHpcServerSoaClient.bat fails if the installation path contains spaces
Description: If the path to the MATLAB installation location contains any spaces, execution of matlabroot /toolbox/distcomp/bin/registerHpcServerSoaClient.bat  does not complete successfully. A common source of this problem is an installation path that includes the folder "Program Files ". This affects only users of Microsoft Windows HPC Server 2008 and Microsoft Compute Cluster Server 2003.
Workaround: Download the attached registerHpcServerSoaClient.bat file, and use it to replace matlabroot\bin\registerHpcServerSoaClient.bat in your client installation.

Summary: strel objects created and saved in or before R2009a do not load in R2009b
Description: If you create a strel object in R2009a (or earlier) and save it, and then try to load it in R2009b, it will not load properly as an object. In R2009a, create and save a strel object: st = strel('ball',4,3.2);save('strelTest','st' ) Try to load it in R2009b: load('strelTest.mat')  You will get the following warning messages, and any subsequent attempt to use the object will result in an error. Warning: Error occurred while trying to call loadobj on a strel object:Illegal right hand side in assignment. Too many elements.  Warning: Class 'strel' is an unknown object class. Element(s) ofthis class in array 'st' have been converted to structures.
Workaround: You can regenerate the needed strel object in R2009b instead of calling load.

Summary: Deleting a row or a column from symbolic matrix results in error
Description: Deleting rows or columns of a symbolic matrix may result in error. For example: x = sym(eye(2)); x(2,:) = [] ??? Error using ==> mupadmexError in MuPAD command: Subscripted assignment dimension mismatch Error in ==> sym.sym>sym.subsasgn at 1420C = mupadmex('mllib::subsasgn',A.s,B.s,inds{:});

Summary: Error in default MCRInstaller path in MATLAB Compiler R2009b when opening a project from previous release that included MCRInstaller
Description: When opening a project that was created in a version of the Deployment Tool (deploytool) R2009a or earlier, the default location of MCRInstaller is incorrect. It points to: \\mathworks\devel\bat\Adeploy\perfect\matlab\derived\obj\\mcr_install_directory
Workaround: To workaround the issue:Remove the MCR from the project.Add the MCR back to the project.

Summary: DocPolynom class does not require coefficients to be of class double.
Description: The DocPolynom  example class in the documentation does not enforce the use of coefficients that are of class double . It is possible to specify non-numeric coefficients without causing an error.
Workaround: Specify coefficients only as doubles.

Summary: Creating a shared library from a MATLAB file with %#external fails during compilation
Description: While creating a C shared library with a MATLAB file from the command line, mcc  fails with errors while running the %#external  pragma.

Summary: Japanese encoding is not supported in the file MISRA-C-report.xml
Description: The MISRA-C-report.xml does not support paths containing Kanji characters. If you configure a project with source files located in a path with Kanji characters, the file MISRA-C-report.xml will not contain the Kanji characters. Note: there is no problem with the file MISRA-C-summary-report.xml.

Summary: Profiler: Attempt to drill down into the function being profiled returns an "index out of bounds" error message
Description: MATLAB? returns an error message such as the following when you profile a function and the last line of the file containing the function ends with neither a newline character, nor a semicolon, nor a comma: ??? Attempted to access o.lnos(25); index out of bounds because numel(o.lnos)=24. (The numbers 24 and 25 are file-specific.) ?
Workaround: Add one of the following characters after the last line in the function file:NewlineSemicolonComma

Summary: Symbolic Math Toolbox can display negative floating-point numbers using more digits than requested.
Description: The following code produces an output where the negative numbers have more than three digits: x = 2*pi*[.1:.3:.9];y = cos(x);vpa(poly2sym(y),3) ans =0.809*x^2 - 0.80901699437526986002922058105469*x - 0.30900000000002592059900052845478
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Simulink crashes when used interactively on Windows Vista with Tablet PC Input Service running.
Description: Simulink crashes when used interactively on Windows Vista with Tablet PC Input Service running. For example: At the MATLAB command prompt, run cscdesigner .  Once the GUI loads, close the GUI and run cscdesigner  again.   There may be other reproduction steps that cause this issue that have not yet been identified.  This issue is related to bug report 537581 and some of those reproduction steps may cause the same behavior on Vista machines running the Tablet PC Input Service.
Workaround: To workaround this issue:From the Start Menu, select Control Panel > System and Services > Administrative Tools. Double-click Services in the list on the right. Find the Tablet PC Input Service in the list and disable it.

Summary: Runtime Error when using Report Generator: Visual C++ R6034
Description: When using PolySpace Report Generator with either a Windows PolySpace Server or from the PolySpace Viewer, you can get the runtime error R6034.
Workaround: To workaround this issue:Locate the file %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\ReportGenerator\MCR\x86-win32\bin\win32\tbbmalloc.dll.Rename this file tbbmalloc.dll.bak to preserve a backup copy.Unzip the attached file and copy it to the following location: %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\ReportGenerator\MCR\x86-win32\bin\win32\tbbmalloc.dllNote: You can also copy the tbbmalloc.dll file from the MATLAB R2009b install.

Description: If a model contains a function-call Model block, then the model may crash during simulation or code generation if either of the following is true: The referenced model has a bus input, and there is a virtual bus entering the corresponding inport on the Model block. The referenced model has a bus output, and the output port has the parameter Output as nonvirtual bus in parent model  set to off .  The crash may have a stack trace that contains these frames: Stack Trace:  [0] libmwsimulink.dll:_gg_window(0x5645ee20 "BlockDiagram_tag@@@Z", 0, 0x57469c20, 0x5645ee20 "BlockDiagram_tag@@@Z") + 6 bytes   [1] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl MSW_DeleteBlockToolTip(class slBlock const *) (0x57469c20, 0x57469dc0, 0x57469c20, 0x56d3b020 "?v?V0?@W") + 115 bytes   [2] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl sgb_owner(class slBlock *,struct slGraph_tag *) (0x57469c20, 0, 0x57469c20, 0x56d3b020 "?v?V0?@W") + 52 bytes   [3] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl RemoveBlocksInsertedBfProp(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *)(0x25447880, 0x49188680, 0x25447880, 0x25447880) + 137 bytes   [4] libmwsimulink.dll:public: void __thiscall slModel::shutdownModel(void)(0x49188680, 0x00d3a558, 0, 0x00d3a530 "???") + 1391 bytes  [5] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl slDestroyModel(class slModel *) (0x49188680, 0x0a362c20, 3, 0) + 94 bytes   [6] libmwsimulink.dll:__catch$?DoRTWGen@@YAPAVslsvErrMsgQueue@@PAURTWInfo@@PAUmxArray_tag@@HQAPAU3@@Z$13(0x00d3a500 "on", 0x245ec560, 3, 0x00d3a7a0) + 303 bytes

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong Answer: Using absolute-time temporal logic can result in incorrect answer (only for simulation) under rare circumstances
Description: Assume the following circumstances: The user is using absolute-time temporal logic (where the second argument to a temporal logic operator such as "after" is "sec"). The user has specified a "small" integer size (such as 16 bits) for "int" in the Hardware Implementation  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. The user has specified the stop time and step size in the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog such that the simulation proceeds for more than 2^16 cycles.  Under these circumstances, there can be an occasional glitch in the answer returned by the "after" absolute-time temporal logic operator, which leads to incorrect answers.
Workaround: Please set the target int size to 32 bits in the Hardware Implementation pane when simulating the model. You can reset the target int size to 16 bits when generating Real-Time Workshop code from the model.

Summary: Hyperlinks to files do not work in the Simulink Diagnostic Viewer, Model Explorer, and Model Properties dialog box
Description: Hyperlinks to some files and directories do not work for the following Simulink tools: Simulink Diagnostic Viewer -- hyperlinks to text files in displayed messages (for example, Stateflow and Embedded MATLAB messages) Model Explorer -- hyperlinks to directories containing generated code for models Model Properties dialog box -- hyperlinks to model files
Workaround: Close MATLAB.Make a backup copy of MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@directory/getDialogSchema.p, where MATLABROOT is the root of the MATLAB installation directory on your system.Download a copy of the version of getDialogSchema.p attached to this report.Replace MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@directory/getDialogSchema.p with the downloaded copy of getDialogSchema.p.Make a backup copy of MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/modelddg.m.Download a copy of the version of modelddg.m attached to this report.Replace MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/modelddg.m with the downloaded copy of modelddg.m.Make a backup copy of MATLABROOT/toolbox/shared/dastudio/@DAStudio/@DiagMsg/findhtmllinks.m.Download a copy of the version of findhtmllinks.m attached to this report.Replace MATLABROOT/toolbox/shared/dastudio/@DAStudio/@DiagMsg/findhtmllinks.m with the downloaded copy of findhtmllinks.m.

Summary: MATLAB Compiler documentation doesn't state that both System and User locales must match to ensure proper execution of compiled applications
Description: Acccording to MATLAB documentation, both System and User locales must match to ensure proper execution of applications. The same holds true of MATLAB Compiler and applications produced with MATLAB Compiler. ?
Workaround: No workaround for missing documentation.

Description: PolySpace verification fails at compilation phase with Shift-JIS encoded character files. In the following example, PolySpace stops with a compilation error: typedef unsigned char A1; // ?\ ?\ -\#define AA 0 // ?\void foo(void){  A1 xx = AA;  // -- \'¨¨-\  int a = 0; // ?@"\  int b;  // b = a  b = a; // line 13}  The compilation message is: "C:\PolySpace\sources\sample.cpp, line 13: error: identifier "b" is undefined| b = a;| ^"
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder: %POLYSPACE_CPP%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32Create backup copies of the following three files:edgcpfe.gnu.x86-mingw32.exeedgcpfe.microsoft.x86-mingw32.exe edgcpfe.x86-mingw32.exeDownload the file '578919_Windows.zip' that is attached to this bug report.Extract the files edgcpfe.gnu.x86-mingw32.exe, edgcpfe.microsoft.x86-mingw32.exe and edgcpfe.x86-mingw32.exe from the zip file.Copy these three files to: %POLYSPACE_CPP%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder: $POLYSPACE_CPP/Verifier/bin/x86-linuxCreate backup copies of the following three files:edgcpfe.gnu.x86-linuxedgcpfe.microsoft.x86-linuxedgcpfe.x86-linuxDownload the file '578919_Linux.zip' that is attached to this bug reportExtract the files edgcpfe.gnu.x86-linux, edgcpfe.microsoft.x86-linux and edgcpfe.x86-linux from the zip file.Copy these three filese to: $POLYSPACE_CPP/Verifier/bin/x86-linuxTo workaround this issue on a Solaris system:Browse to the folder: $POLYSPACE_CPP/Verifier/bin/sparc-solarisCreate backup copies of the following three files: edgcpfe.gnu.sparc-solarisedgcpfe.microsoft.sparc-solarisedgcpfe.sparc-solarisDownload the file '578919_Solaris.zip' that is attached to this bug reportExtract the files edgcpfe.gnu.sparc-solaris, edgcpfe.microsoft.sparc-solaris and edgcpfe.sparc-solaris from the zip file.Copy these three files to:$POLYSPACE_CPP/Verifier/bin/sparc-solaris

Summary: geoshow function may color the wrong area when displaying counter-clockwise polygons
Description: In the rare case in which a latitude-longitude polygon consists of only (one or more) counter-clockwise rings, and none of the rings intersect the map frame boundary, geoshow  colors in the areas on the left-hand sides of the curves instead of the right-hand sides.

Summary: Traceability matrix incorrectly indicates MinMax block is optimized and not traceable
Description: When the model contains a MinMax block with array inputs, the traceability matrix incorrectly lists the Model Object Optimized  column as Yes  and the Model Object Rationale  column as notTraceable .
Workaround: To include the MinMax block traceability information:Verify manually that the MinMax block is represented in the code.Add a comment column to the traceability matrix.Note the location of the MinMax block in the comment column.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded MATLAB functions accessing Stateflow local vector data may generate incorrect code.
Description: Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts that access Stateflow local vectors of size [n 1]  or [1 n]  may generate incorrect code in Accelerator mode or Real-Time Workshop. The following conditions may trigger the bug: At least one Embedded MATLAB function accesses the local vector using single-dimension indexing (for example, V(i) ). At least one function uses the local vector without defining it.  Due to interactions with other optimizations (primarily function inlining), some models that meet all of these conditions do not exhibit the bug. When the bug does occur, an incorrect optimization converts chart-level vectors into C stack variables (for example, auto variables). Once this incorrect conversion happens, subsequent optimizations may lead to further mangling of the code. Symptoms of this bug can range from uncompilable code, warnings in the generated code, or silent wrong answers.
Workaround: Do not use single-dimensional indexing to access chart local vectors from Embedded MATLAB functions.For example, for row-vector R of size [1 n], either use two-dimensional indexing of the form R(1,i) or change the size of R to[n]. Note, however, that the latter option converts R from a row-vector into a column-vector and may change the behavior of your MATLAB functions.For column-vector C of size [n 1], change the size to [n]. This change does not affect the behavior of your MATLAB code.The attached MATLAB files implement a Model Advisor rule that warns you of syntactic occurrences of chart local vectors in your model that may require attention. Note that this rule does not detect situations where chart local sizes are parameterized. It works only when you enter sizes literally in the size field.To add this rule to the Model Advisor, unzip the attached file, and place the resulting MATLAB files on your MATLAB path. Then, run the Model Advisor by selecting it from the Tools menu in Simulink.

Summary: MATLAB segmentation fault when using Tiff object to read images with old JPEG compression
Description: Using the Tiff  object to read an image that has been compressed with the Old-JPEG (OJPEG) codec can cause a segmentation fault. These images have a YCbCr photometric interpretation. Old-JPEG compression is strongly deprecated by "TIFF Technical Note No. 2" (http://www.remotesensing.org/libtiff/TIFFTechNote2.html) in favor of "regular" JPEG comression within TIFFs.

Summary: Dependency Report: Index out of bounds error
Description: Running the Dependency Report on a file which does not have a blank line at the end of it returns the following error: ERROR : Attempted to access o.lnos(42); index out of bounds because numel(o.lnos)=41.
Workaround: Either add a blank line to the end of the file, or do the following:Determine a value for MATLABROOT by running the matlabroot function at the MATLAB? command prompt.Quit MATLAB.In MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\codetools, rename getcallinfo.m to another name, such as getcallinfo.m.old.Download the attached ZIP file, and unzip getcallinfo.m to MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\codetools.Restart MATLAB.Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Band-Limited Op-Amp and Finite Gain Op-Amp blocks generate incorrect results for nonzero values of Output resistance, Rout parameter
Description: In the Band-Limited Op-Amp and Finite Gain Op-Amp blocks, the product of the output resistance and output current has the wrong sign. This will generate incorrect results if block parameter Output resistance, Rout  is set to a value other than zero.
Workaround: To work around this issue, set the block parameter Output resistance, Rout to zero, and add an external resistor connected to the op-amp output. Set the value of this resistor to the op-amp output resistance value you require. The other resistor port is now the op-amp output.

Summary: Incorrect results when simplifying a term involving derivatives of dirac
Description: Differentiation of the following expression involving dirac  returns incorrect results: syms t simplify(diff(dirac(t)/exp(t),t))  ans =dirac(t, 1) - dirac(t)
Workaround: Do not apply simplify or simple to derivatives of the dirac function.

Summary: on Mac OS X, sometimes a From Workspace block dialog box's edit  fields may appear editable but is not
Description: On Mac OS X, when you open the Block Parameters dialog box for the From Workspace block, sometimes you cannot enter any text in the edit fields.
Workaround: Click any other window to allow the dialog box to lose focus, then return to the dialog box.

Summary: Function etopo returns inaccurate referencing vector
Description: The referencing vector returned by function etopo  is inaccurate. It is off by half the size of the output cell. (The output cell size is dependent on the sampling factor.)
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the etopo.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapformats/etopo.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your R2009b MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: etopo.m.oldCopy the version of etopo.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.The workaround is only valid for the R2009b release. The workaround adds support for the newer ETOPO2v2c format, removes the westward sampling bias when a samplingfactor other than 1 is specified, and requires longitude limits to be in the interval of [0, 360] when reading ETOPO5 data.

Summary: Release notes indicate ISO 26262 support for incorrect MathWorks product
Description: The release notes for the IEC Certification Kit product indicate that the IEC Certification Kit product supports ISO 26262 tool qualification for the Simulink Verification and Validation product. However, the IEC Certification Kit product does not support ISO 26262 tool qualification for the Simulink Verification and Validation product. The IEC Certification Kit product supports ISO 26262 tool qualification for the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder product.
Workaround: The IEC Certification Kit release notes have been corrected on the MathWorks web site.

Summary: "What's This?" help does not work for the Embedded IDE Link pane in Configuration Parameters
Description: "What's This?" context-sensitive help does not work for the Embedded IDE Link pane, located under Real Time Workshop, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: In the online help, search for "Embedded IDE Link Pane". Then use the location information displayed above the help topic to select the correct search result for your IDE. For example, when you select the first search result, the location information is "Embedded IDE Link > For Use with TI's Code Composer Studio..."Alternatively, use the Contents pane in online help. Select the "For Use with..." node that corresponds to your IDE. Then select User Guide > Configuration Parameters > Embedded IDE Link Pane.

Summary: Incorrect initialization value when input signal for Rate Transition block uses data type int8 or uint8
Description: When all of the following conditions are true, building a model that contains a Rate Transition block incorrectly optimizes out the Rate Transition block initialization code: In the Rate Transition block parameters, Ensure data integrity during date transfer  is selected and Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  is cleared. The input signal to the Rate Transition block is scalar and the data type is int8  or uint8 . The rate transition is from a slow rate to a fast rate. The Rate Transition block has a nonzero initial condition.  Because the Rate Transition block initialization code is optimized out, running the model produces an initial value of zero, not matching the specified initial condition.
Workaround: You can work around this bug in either of the following ways:Cast the input signal data type to a different integer data type before the Rate Transition block, and then cast the data type back to int8 or uint8 after the Rate Transition block.Use a Simulink alias type in place of int8 or uint8. For example, add the following lines in the model load callback:myUint8 = Simulink.AliasType; myUint8.BaseType = 'uint8';myInt8 = Simulink.AliasType; myInt8.BaseType = 'int8'; Then, in the model, replace uint8 and int8 with myUint8 and myInt8. See the attached model and callback, which illustrate this approach.

Summary: Parallel Computing Toolbox cannot submit jobs to a worker when using a borrowed license.
Description: The Parallel Computing Toolbox does not work when running MATLAB using a borrowed license (disconnected from the license server). You will see error messages similar to this: >> pmode startStarting pmode using the parallel configuration 'local'.Performing parallel job cleanup...Done.??? Failed to create and submit a parallel job.This is caused by:Error using ==> featureCould not get feature list.  License server may be down.Error in ==> pmode at 83       client.start('pmode', numlabs, config, 'opengui');

Summary: Repeatedly copying and pasting Simulink(R) function from Stateflow(R) chart to Simulink model leads to multiple copies of Simulink function-call subsystem
Description: Starting from R2009b, it is possible to copy a Simulink function-call object from a Stateflow chart and paste it in a Simulink model. When this action is done, the previous contents in the Simulink clipboard are not cleared. Hence, if there were existing items in the Simulink clipboard, a subsequent paste in a model would paste the originally copied objects in addition to the newly copied Simulink function. The correct behavior is that when you copy a Simulink function from the Stateflow chart and paste it into a Simulink model, only the Simulink function is pasted in the Simulink Editor.
Workaround: The workaround below needs write access to the MATLAB(R) installation area:Download the attached SimulinkMan.mAt the MATLAB prompt, type: which Stateflow.SLINSF.SimulinkManOverwrite the file referenced by the previous command with the downloaded versionRestart MATLAB

Summary: bootstrp does not return sample indices when the input function is missing
Description: The bootstrp  function generates a set of random subsamples of an input array, and calls a user-provided function for each sample. It returns the function results as the first output and the indices of the samples as the second output. For example: x = rand(20,1);[results,bootsam] = bootstrp(1,@mean,x);resultsresults =    0.6140mean(x(bootsam))ans =    0.6140 If the user-provided function is empty, the function is intended to return an empty value as the first output, and the bootstrap sample indices as the second output. However, the second output array is entirely zero. Example: [~,bootsam] = bootstrp(1,[],x);mean(x(bootsam))??? Subscript indices must either be real positive integers or logicals.
Workaround: Any of the following commands provide a simpler way to generate bootstrap sample indices without calling the bootstrp function:bootsam = ceil(n*rand(n,nboot)) bootsam = randi([1 n],n,nboot) bootsam = randi(n,n,nboot) where n is the number of rows in X.

Summary: The Automatic Orange Tester (AOT) does not instrument checks when you launch verification from a Windows client to a UNIX server
Description: When you launch a verification from a Windows client using the option -prepare-automtic-tests , and send it to a UNIX server, the Automatic Orange Tester (AOT) does not instrument any checks. This causes a "Non-instrumented error " error message for each test specified in the test campaign configuration  field. This message appears in the log file 'Testgenerator*.out ' located in the results sub-folder 'PolySpace-Instrumented '.
Workaround: Note: This fix applies only to PolySpace server systems running a UNIX operating system.On a Linux OS:Locate the file /usr/local/PolySpace/PolySpaceForCandCPP_R2009b/Verifier/bin/x86-linux/instrument.x68-linuxRename this file instrument.x68-linux.oldDownload and unzip the attached file 577622_instrument.x86-linux.zip, then copy it to the same location.On a Solaris OS:Locate the file /usr/local/PolySpace/PolySpaceForCandCPP_R2009b/Verifier/bin/sparc-solaris/instrument.sparc-solarisRename this file instrument.sparc-solaris.oldDownload and unzip the attached file 577622_instrument.sparc-solaris.zip, then copy it to the same location.?

Summary: For an algebraic loop, the logsOut object contains an incorrect path to the signal logging of block outputs
Description: If the outputs of blocks within an algebraic loop are selected for logging via a 'right-click' selection, then the path to the signal in the resulting logsOut object is incorrect. The path contains the name of a synthesized subsystem that Simulink inserted to enclose the algebraic loop.
Workaround: Use a scope block instead of the right-click feature to turn on signal logging.

Summary: Basic Service client ports with zero arguments cause import process to error out with non-descriptive message
Description: If a Software Component has a Basic Service port that is configured as a client with no arguments, then the import process fails with the following error message: Error using ==> p_create_component at 131Should not be here
Workaround: Either remove all operations with no arguments from the the arxml file you want to import, or do the following:Download the attached file.Use this file to replace the following file:matlab/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@importer/private/p_create_component.p?

Summary: Simulink HDL Coder displays error when model contains annotation blocks
Description: When a model contains annotation blocks, checkhdl does not report any error or warning. However, makehdl reports the following error message: Message: Simulink HDL Coder Error Source: cru_top6 Reported By: Simulink HDL Coder Summary: Error from hdlcoder:pirudd:Simulink:SL_AddAnnoCantAdd Error: Evaluation of generateSLBlock function on class hdldefaults.AnnotationHDLEmission failed with the error message: A new annotation named 'gm_myannotation' cannot be added.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can locate all annotation blocks by entering either of the following commands at the MATLAB command prompt:x = find_system('<<your_model_name>>,'FindAll','on','type','annotation') ORx = find_system('<<your_model_name>>','FindAll','on','LookUnderMasks','all','type','annotation')After executing one of the above commands, delete the annotation blocks. You can delete all the annotation blocks at once using the following command:delete(x) Alternatively, you can delete the annotation blocks individually until the error message goes away, as follows:delete(x(1)), delete(x(2))... ??

Summary: fprintf and sprintf print floating-point values instead of integers with the 'l' modifier
Description: On some platforms, if you specify the 'l'  modifier to an integer format for any values greater than 2^32 - 1, fprintf  and sprintf  print floating-point values.  Integer format specifiers include 'x' , 'd' , 'o' , 'i' , or 'u' . For example: fprintf('%lx', 2^32)   % correct result 100000000  returns 4.294967e+009  Note: This behavior is expected for integer formats without the 'l'  modifier, because the values require more than 32 bits.  However, the intent of the 'l'  modifier is to print integers up to 64 bits.
Workaround: For releases before R2010a, display numbers in hexadecimal format using the dec2hex function.  For example:dec2hex(2^32)returns the string'100000000'

Summary: conv2 has a performance regression for matrices that contain mostly zeros.
Description: conv2(A,B)  has a performance regression since R2008b in the following cases: numel(B) >= numel(A)  and B  contains mostly zeros, numel(A) > numel(B)  and A  contains mostly zeros.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during 'Software Safety Analysis Level 1' with the message: '@FFT: * unknown field assigned_min_index in { }'.
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on C++ code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: ****** Software Safety Analysis Level 1 - 1.1.19 (P_SR) ---------------------------------------------------------------------- @FFT: * unknown field assigned_min_index in { } |An internal consistency problem has been detected. |In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information, |the computation has been halted. |Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support site |https://www.mathworks.com/support. |Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report. ---------------------------------------------------------------------- malloc exception Match(Match) raised. This problem occurs when the source code to analyse handles exceptions mechanism.

Summary: Using AUTOSAR target can cause data type propagation error
Description: If your model is configured for the AUTOSAR target, code generation can fail with a data type propagation error during model compilation, even though this error does not occur with a different Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder target.
Workaround: This error occurs because the software uses heuristics during data type propagation. To identify what activates these heuristics:Select Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity.Under Signals, in the Underspecified data types field, select either warning or error from the drop-down list.If you modify your model so that the software does not use heuristics, then inconsistent data type propagation will cease to be an issue in most cases.

Summary: TLC errors might occur when generating C++ encapsulation interface for referenced model invoked by Stateflow chart using function-call signal?
Description: Generating code for your model might produce TLC errors if both of the following conditions exist: The model contains a Stateflow chart that invokes a referenced model using a function-call signal. The referenced model is configured with a C++ encapsulation interface.  Under these conditions, code generation might produce TLC errors similar to the following: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefutil.tlc Line: 809 Column: 59 Indices may not be negative Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefutil.tlc Line: 810 Column: 56 Indices may not be negative Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefutil.tlc Line: 811 Column: 34 Unable to find DWork within the Block scope Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\mw\modelrefutil.tlc Line: 811 Column: 44 Unable to find CPPObjDWorkIdxInSubsys within the Block scope
Workaround: To work around this bug, avoid using the direct function-call signal from the Stateflow chart to the referenced model. Instead, place the referenced model inside a noninlined function-call subsystem, and make the subsystem reusable by opening the Subsystem Parameters dialog box and setting the Real-Time Workshop system code parameter to Reusable function.?

Summary: Failure during code generation for AUTOSAR model with sub-bus connected to root outport
Description: Code generation can fail for an AUTOSAR model that has a sub-bus connected to a root outport, for example, a Bus Selector block connected to the root outport. The code generation can fail with an assertion like the following: CGIR assertion in .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_autosar_transform.cpp(164): 'DerefExpr::isa(assignExpr->rhs())'[0] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl assertion_func(void *,char const * const,int,char const * const)() + 46 bytes[1] cg_ir.dll:void __cdecl client_assertion_failed(char const *,int,char const *)() + 27 bytes[2] rtwcg.dll:public: void __thiscall RTWCG::RTWCGTransformForAutosar::apply(void)() + 636 bytes[3] rtwcg.dll:void __cdecl RTWCG::RTWTransformForAutosar(class RTWCG::RTWFcnConst *,class RTWCG::RTWContext *,struct PtrSet *)() + 124 bytes[4] rtwcg.dll:public: bool __thiscallRTWCG::RTWFcnConst::OptimizeAndEmitCode(int,class RTWCG::TLCOutPortInfo * const,class RTWCG::RTWCGVector &)() + 1194 bytes
Workaround: If the sub-bus is a single signal, place a Signal Conversion block between the Bus Selector block and the root outport. Right-click this Signal Conversion block and select SignalConversion Parameters from the context menu. In the Block Parameters dialog box, select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization check box.

Summary: AUTOSAR model containing root-level I/O with multi-word data type produces compilation error
Description: When you build an AUTOSAR model containing root-level I/O associated with a multi-word data type, the software generates a compilation error, for example: mfb_fixpt.c(323) : error C2440: '=' : cannot convert from 'int96m_T *' to 'int96m_T' Multiword data types should not be used as the data types at the root level IO
Workaround: You must not use multi-word data types for root-level I/O. The software now reports an error if multi-word data types are used for root-level I/O.

Summary: iec.ExportTraceReport incorrectly generates error when working folder is network drive
Description: When you run the iec.ExportTraceReport  function and your working folder is a mapped network drive, the following error message is displayed: 'path \file_name ' cannot be found. Check your spelling or try a different path.  The error message is incorrect. The file is in the location specified by path \file_name  .
Workaround: To avoid the error, specify a local drive for your working folder.  Otherwise, ignore the error message.

Summary: The xPC Target PCI-6011 D/A driver incorrectly scales output for the National Instruments PCI-6011 board
Description: The xPC Target PCI-6011 D/A driver incorrectly scales output for the National Instruments PCI-6011 board. The output voltage is 16 times higher than it should be.
Workaround: As a workaround, do one of the following:Divide the value you give the board by 16.Contact MathWorks Technical Support and ask for a corrected version of the PCI-6011 D/A driver for your xPC Target version.

Summary: Missing AUTH files in CTF Archive
Description: Recurring sporadic failure in some toolbox deployability tests at run-time, where a MEX-file's AUTH file cannot be found, have been noted. When the failure occurs, the spared component cache directory evidences that there is no AUTH file for the MEX-file referenced in the error. However, other MEX-files may have AUTH files extracted. The failure is random with respect to which MEX-file will be missing its AUTH file. If you rerun the application, the same error occurs.

Summary: SISOTOOL error when loading a project saved with a design requirement in the Nichols editor
Description: Loading a SISOTOOL project saved with a gain margin or phase margin design requirement in the Nichols editor results in this error: left hand side that has a comma separated list expansion . The project loads without the saved gain or phase margin design requirement.
Workaround:
Apply the attached patch:Save the attached .zip file to $matlabroot. $matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory on your machine. To locate $matlabroot, type matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Rename $matlabroot	oolbox\control\ctrlguis\@sisogui\@nicholseditor
