Summary: PolySpace installation does not upgrade the Queue Manager service when the service already exists with an older version
Description: When PolySpace server is installed on a machine without uninstalling a previous release, the PolySpace installer does not upgrade the Queue Manager service. At the end of installation, the Queue Manager service of the previous version continues to run. When this occurs, the following error message appears on the PolySpace Client when it is upgraded and tries to send a new verification to the Queue Manager: Error: The versions of client and Queue Manager are incompatible :| Client version : X_Y_Z| Queue Manager version : A_B_C.An error occured while sending the verification to the queue.   ?
Workaround: To avoid this problem, stop and restart the service on the PolySpace server.On a Unix PolySpace server commands are '/etc/init.d/polyspaced stop' and '/etc/init.d/polyspaced start'On a Windows PolySpace server '%POLYSPACE_COMMON%\RemoteLauncher\PSTRLManager.exe' command allows to stop and restart the service.

Summary: FPRINTF unable to correctly handle standard error stream
Description: A customer reported that the following FPRINTF statments should produce identical results to different streams (stdout and stderr, therefore different colors).  However, it appears that this is not the case and the hyperlink for the standard error stream is incorrectly shown on the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: mmreader returns distorted images for video files on Macintosh? OS X 10.6 Snow Leopard systems
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.6 Snow Leopard systems, mmreader  returns garbled image data for all video files. For an example of a distorted image, see the attached file garbled_image.png.
Workaround: NOTE: This fix applies only to customers running OS X 10.6 Snow Leopard or greater.For MATLAB versions R2009a or R2009b, download an updated version of the file quicktimekitreader.dylib:Find the $MATLABROOT directory where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB? root directory on your machine, as returned by typing:     matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file quicktimekitreader.dylib to quicktimekitreader.dylib.old.On 32-bit systems (R2009a and R2009b), this file is located in    $MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/audiovideo/bin/mmreader/plugins/maciOn 64-bit systems (R2009b only), this file is located in     $MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/audiovideo/bin/mmreader/plugins/maci64Download the attached file for your system:For R2009a systems, download quicktimekitreader_R2009a.zip.For R2009b 32-bit systems, download quicktimekitreader_R2009b_32.zip.For R2009b 64-bit systems, download quicktimekitreader_R2009b_64.zip.Unzip the file, and place the new file, quicktimekitreader.dylib, in the same directory mentioned in step 3.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Documentation error for Instruments argument when using the fitFunction, fitSvennson, fitNelsonSiegel, fitSmoothingSpline methods of the @IRFunctionCurve class
Description: The 2008b documentation incorrectly states that the Instruments  argument for fitFunction , fitSvennson , fitNelsonSiegel , fitSmoothingSpline  methods of the @IRFunctionCurve  class use the dirty price of the bond.
Workaround: When using the Instruments argument for fitFunction, fitSvennson, fitNelsonSiegel, fitSmoothingSpline methods of the @IRFunctionCurve class, use the clean price of the bond in the third column of the matrix.

Summary: Missing model coverage reports, outdated time stamps, or broken links when using test models to qualify Simulink Verification and Validation model coverage capability
Description: Some of the test models used to qualify the model coverage capability of the Simulink Verification and Validation product require licenses for additional MathWorks products. The license requirements documented in the Simulink Verification and Validation Test Cases, Procedures, and Results document do not indicate all of the required licenses. When a required product license is not available, the model coverage report for that product is not generated. If no previous report has been generated, the link to the model coverage report does not work. If a previous report has been generated, or you copied the shipped report into the working folder, the link points to the previous or copied report. The time stamp in the previous or copied report is outdated, indicating that the report was not generated during the last execution of the test model. Depending on the missing license, a temporary warning message might be displayed. However, when the report generation finishes, the message might clear automatically. The following list includes the required licenses for the specified test files: Stateflow license - qualkitdo_slvnv_tcpr3.rpt , qualkitdo_slvnv_tcpr5.rpt  Simulink Fixed Point license - qualkitdo_slvnv_tcpr2.rpt , qualkitdo_slvnv_tcpr4.rpt  Simulink Design Verifier license - qualkitdo_slvnv_tcpr6.rpt   A qualification report with missing or outdated model coverage reports cannot be used as evidence for a successful qualification. The workaround provides a fix for license issues with Stateflow, Simulink Fixed Point and Simulink Design Verifier. If you do not have a license, an error message is displayed in the report. Additionally, the workaround provides fixes for the following bugs: EBR 956711  EBR 954249  EBR 1012251  EBR 1052010  EBR 1055372
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to <MATLAB> as in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of the <MATLAB>/toolbox/qualkits/do/slvnv/r2009b folder.Delete the indicated files by following the steps below. The result files are not valid. cd to <MATLAB>/toolbox/qualkits/do/slvnv/r2009brmdir('qualkitdo_slvnv_qualificationreport*_html_files','s');rmdir('scv_images','s');delete ('*.html');Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*:Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the folder. If prompted, click yes to for permission to replace the files.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zipfile in the folder. To save the .zip file in the folder, open this EBR in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zipfile.Extract the contents of the file to your folder.

Summary: SimBiology models with notes containing HTML tags may be incorrectly exported to SBML
Description: Importing and exporting an SBML model that includes HTML tags can result in an invalid SBML file. The sbmlimport function will then fail when trying to import this invalid SBML file.This problem will occur if the original SBML file declares an XMLnamespace for the HTML tags. This namespace declaration is omitted inthe invalid SBML file, which causes sbmlimport  to fail when encountering HTML tags in the notes. For instance, if you use sbmlimport  to import an SBML file that includes an HTML note such as the following: <notes><html:p>GENE_ASSOCIATION: </html:p><html:p>PROTEIN_ASSOCIATION: </html:p></notes>  And then export the model to a new SBML file using sbmlexport . The original SBML file includes this declaration header:<sbml xmlns="http://www.sbml.org/sbml/level2" xmlns:html="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" level="2" version="1">  but the xmlns:html declaration is missing from the new header: <sbml xmlns="http://www.sbml.org/sbml/level2" level="2" version="1">  If you try to re-import the exported model, you receive the following message: The following error occurred while importing the SBML file:A fatal error occurred reading in this file
Workaround: Option 1: Remove HTML fields from the XML file before importing it into SimBiology. sbmlexport will then produce a valid SBML file.Option 2: Manually add the XML namespace declaration to an SBML file produced using sbmlexport. For example, change the declaration header of the SBML file from <sbml xmlns="http://www.sbml.org/sbml/level2" level="2" version="1"> to <sbml xmlns="http://www.sbml.org/sbml/level2" xmlns:html="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" level="2" version="1">

Summary: Real-Time Workshop software might generate uncompilable code for bitfield structure
Description: Real-Time Workshop software might generate uncompilable code under all of the following conditions: The model contains Boolean data or Stateflow charts; The model is configured to generate bitfields through one of the following parameters on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box: Pack Boolean data into bitfields , Use bitsets for storing state configuration , Use bitsets for storing Boolean data  On the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the value for Tasking mode for period sample times  is Auto  or MultiTasking  Tasking mode for period sample times  is set to Auto  and the model has more than one periodic sample rates  The generated code might use the same name for two bitfield structures, which are both within the same structure. This causes a code compilation error.
Workaround: On the Optimizations pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, disable the bitfield optimizations: Pack Boolean data into bitfields, Use bitsets for storing state configuration, and Use bitsets for storing Boolean data.

Summary: grpstats dataset array method generates error: "All variables must have the same number of rows"
Description: The grpstats  method for the dataset array class generates the following error if one of the grouping variables contains a missing value (e.g., an empty string, NaN, or <undefined> value): ??? Error using ==> dataset.dataset>dataset.dataset at 244All variables must have the same number of rows.  For example, ds = dataset(randi(3,10,1),randn(10,1));ds.Var1(ds.Var1==3) = NaNgrpstats(ds,'Var1','mean','DataVars','Var2')
Workaround: Pass a subset of the array to the grpstats method. For example,grpstats(ds(~isnan(ds.Var1,:),'Var1','mean','DataVars','Var2')Alternatively, edit MATLABROOT/toolbox/stats/private/dsgrpstats.m, and add the following line:    firsts = firsts(1:ngroups);immediately after the line that reads    [dum,firsts] = unique(group,'first');(line 199 in the current version of dsgrpstats.m).?

Summary: Incorrect fixed-point word size determined when importing from an arxml file
Description: AUTOSAR uses the lower and upper values of integer types to deduce the word length for the datatype. However, for the AUTOSAR target, the software incorrectly imports the lower and upper values using real-world minimum and maxium values rather than the minimum and maximum values of the stored integer.
Workaround: Do the following:Download the attached files.Use these files to replace the following:matlab/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@importer/private/p_add_data_type.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/messages/autosar/en/xlate

Summary: Assertion occurs when chart bus data contains field of Simulink.StructType
Description: When chart bus data contains a field of Simulink.StructType, an assertion occurs.
Workaround: Stateflow does not support Simulink.StructType. Do not use Simulink.StructType to define chart data types.

Summary: Verification stops during CDFA phase with the following error:  INTERNAL ERROR unexpected node
Description: Verification stops during CDFA phase with a fatal error. The error message will be something like: - analyzing PROG._PST95_ADA-INTERNAL__DEEP_FINALIZE : #<procedure>ana : INTERNAL ERROR unexpected node #30457:{comes_from_source = false, sloc = "file.adb" line 10, column 70, parent = 30459, variant = Selected_Component{prefix = 30439, selector_name = 30438, expr_info = {etype = 1543, flags = {assignment_ok=false,do_range_check=false, raises_constraint_error=false,is_static_expression=false}}}}exception Match(Match) raised.FATAL ERROR for file.atz  ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ------ Verifier has encountered an internal error. ------ Please contact your technical support. ------ ---------------------------------------------------------------------- This problem occurs when you get a record-component's attribute of private record type variable within a protected type when the component's type is a subtype array depending on one of the discriminants. Example of code: type Ring_Buffer_Type(Buffer_Size: uint32) isrecord Buffer : Integer (1..Buff_Size);-- OK when Buffer does not depend on Buffer_Size Tail: Integer;;end record; protected type Protected_Type(Rx_Buffer_Size: uint32) is procedure XXX;private Rx_Buffer: Ring_Buffer_Type(Rx_Buffer_Size);end Protected_Type; protected body Protected_Type is procedure XXX is A : System.Address := Rx_Buffer'Address; -- OK A1 : System.Address := Rx_Buffer.Tail'Address; -- OK A2 : Long_Integer := Long_Integer (Rx_Buffer.Buffer'Size); -- BUG A0 : System.Address := Rx_Buffer.Buffer'Address; -- BUG begin null; end Send;end Protected_Type; ?

Summary: The status of 'reviewed check' is not saved for a parent node
Description: Here is a scenario describing the behavior: 1. Select Edit > Preferences > Table options , then enable the "Reviewed " and "Comments " columns. 2. Load the DEMO_C .rte file: RTE_px_O2_Demo_C_LAST_RESULTS.rte  3. Right-click example.c , then select Expand all nodes . 4. Manually check all reviewed check boxes of the child nodes of "Close_To_Zero() ". 5. Save the project and close it. 6. Open the Viewer again and reload the project, expand all nodes, all check boxes should still be checked. No problem here. 7. Now check the parent node "Close_To_Zero() " (accept the warning). 8. Save the project and close the Viewer. 9. Reopen the Viewer, expand all nodes and you will notice that the check for the parent node was not saved.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the parent node to uncheck all child nodes and then check the parent node again so that all children get checked as well.

Summary: zbtprice and zbtyield do not produce correct results when Basis is 8 through 11
Description: The results for zbtyield  should be similar, but are significantly different when using a Basis  value of 8: Maturity = [734601 734688]'; CouponRate = [0.035 0.05]'; Face = [100 100]'; Period = [1 1]'; Basis = [0 0]'; Yields = [0.0118 0.0134]'; Settle = today + 1;Bonds1 = [Maturity, CouponRate, Face, Period, Basis]; [zerorate1 date1] = zbtyield(Bonds1, Yields, Settle) zerorate1 = 0.01180.0134 date1 = 734601734688 Basis = [8 8]'; Bonds2 = [Maturity, CouponRate, Face, Period, Basis]; [zerorate2 date2] = zbtyield(Bonds2, Yields, Settle) Warning: Could not solve for the rate zerorate2 =  0.0236 0.0269 date2 =  734601 734688
Workaround: Attached is the fix for the problem.To workaround this issue, replace the version of zbtprice.m and zbtyield.m functions in your current installation with the updated version attached.1. Exit MATLAB?.Make a backup copy of the following files.matlabroot\toolbox\finance\zbtprice.mmatlabroot\toolbox\finance\zbtyield.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing files by adding "old" to the file extension, zbtprice.m.OLD and zbtyield.m.OLD.2. Download the attached file and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.3. Restart MATLAB.4. Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Data cursor interpolation calculation is incorrect for a log axis
Description: If you place a data cursor on a log-scale plot, such as the one generated by the following code, semilogx([1,100],[0,-20]) and you select Mouse Position  as the Selection Style  of the cursor, the data tip can appear in an incorrect location on the plot, due to improper interpolation.

Summary: Monte Carlo option pricing function BASKETBYLS in the Financial Derivatives Toolbox fails for users without the Econometric Toolbox. The function applies the Longstaff and Schwartz method to equity basket options driven by geometric Brownian motion.
Description: The original simulation methods were shipped in the Econometrics Toolbox, and there is a line of code in three related object constructors that checks the version number of the Econometrics Toolbox. As of R2009b, some of this functionality migrated to the support directory of the Financial Toolbox, upon which both Econometrics and Financial Derivatives Toolboxes depend. In the event that a client has a license for the Financial Derivatives Toolbox but does NOT have a license for the Econometrics Toolbox, the basket option pricing function fails.
Workaround: Attached is the fix for the problem.Download the attached files DIFFUSION.M, SDE.M and DRIFT.M and replace them for the originals on the following path$MATLABROOT\toolbox\finance\finsupportwhere $MATLABROOT is the path returned be executing MATLABROOT in the Command Window.

Description: When running an FTP object's DIR method, you might sometimes see this error: ??? Attempt to reference field of non-structure array. Error in ==> ftp.dir at 45         listing(i,1).date=char(form.format(cal.getTime));

Summary: Multi-Winding Transformer block with taps incorrectly evaluates leakage inductance and resistance in SI units
Description: The primary winding leakage resistance and inductance of the Multi-Winding Transformer block are not correctly evaluated when taps are specified. This issue exists only when the Units  parameter is set to SI . When the Tapped winding  parameter is set to no taps , the primary winding leakage inductance and resistance are correct.

Description: The North Arrow and N  text, created by the function northarrow , are clipped when dragged to the edge of the axes and disappear when dragged outside the axes limits.
Workaround: To work around the problem, replace the northarrow.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/northarrow.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: northarrow.m.oldCopy the version of northarrow.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB fails to build due to trailing ones in the dimensions of a bus element.
Description: The bug causes an assertion failure during model update (Ctrl-D). The bug might occur when Embedded MATLAB uses a bus where the dimensions of one or more elements has trailing ones. For example, a dimension with a trailing one looks like this in the bus editor: Dimensions: [3 1]
Workaround: Use the bus editor to remove trailing ones from the dimensions of bus elements, as in this example:Dimensions: 3This statement specifies that the bus element has one dimension of length 3. It is functionally equivalent to [3 1], but avoids the bug in Embedded MATLAB.

Summary: setString method of impoint leaves behind label when called multiple times
Description: When the setString  method of impoint  is called more than once, the original label that was specified remains drawn on screen and is not deleted as expected. For example, enter the following code:%% Display image and create an impoint objectimshow('pout.tif');h = impoint;h.setString('One') %% Add new string and then drag the impoint objecth.setString('Two') If you drag impoint  around after executing this code segment, the string "One" remains on screen in the former location of impoint .

Summary: PolySpace verification incorrectly reports a red NIV check on generated code
Description: This behavior occurs most frequently when you generate code with Real-Time Workshop? Embedded Coder? software, and use the option -allow-ptr-arith-on-struct
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder: %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\binCreate backup copies of the following two files:.\x86_64-mingw32\iabc-c2if.cx86_64-mingw32.exe .\x86-mingw32\iabc-c2if.cx86-mingw32.exeDownload the attached file: 574839_Windows.zip.Extract the .exe files from the zip archive and copy them to the following sub-folders: .\x86-mingw32 .\x86_64-mingw32To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/binCreate backup copies of the following two files:./x86_64-linux/iabc-c2if.cx86_64-linux ./x86-linux/iabc-c2if.cx86-linuxDownload the attached file: 574839_Linux.zipExtract the -linux files from the zip archive and copy them to the following sub-folders: ./x86-linux ./x86_64-linuxTo workaround this issue on a Solaris system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/binCreate a backup copy of the following file: ./sparc-solaris/iabc-c2if.csparc-solarisDownload the attached file: 574839_Solaris.zipExtract the -solaris file from the zip archive and copy it to the following sub-folder: ./sparc-solaris

Summary: Memory leak in MATLAB COM Automation client interface.
Description: A memory leak occurs when MATLAB calls COM Automation object functions that return SAFEARRAY**  data type as an [out]  parameter. For example, COM Automation server function with the following IDL signature: HRESULT GetSafeArray([out] SAFEARRAY(double)* ppData) This can affect the system performance when large amounts of data are passed to MATLAB from COM Automation server applications.

Summary: Pressing arrow keys to change the view with the Camera Toolbar causes the scene to rapidly gyrate without end
Description: When you create a figure and set its Units  are set to anything other than the default (pixels ), construct a 3-D plot, open the Camera Toolbar and attempt to change the view with any arrow key, the axes gyrates very rapidly without stopping. For example: figure('Units','Characters');surf(peaks);cameratoolbar If you now press an arrow key, the view rotates uncontrollably until you click the mouse within the figure window. ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, set the Units property of the figure to 'pixels'.

Description: After using framem , gridm , plabel , or mlabel  (in some cases with a specific syntax), the axes properties of 'Visible ', 'Box ', 'DataApectRatio ', and 'NextPlot ' are reset to their default values. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: axesm.m.oldCopy the version of axesm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?

Summary: Bus editor fails to open if a bus object has an element that specifies its data type to be a Simulink.StructType
Description: The Bus Editor fails to open when a bus object in the base workspace has a bus element that specifies its data type to be a Simulink.StructType. You see an error message similar to one of the following messages: ??? Error using  ==> subsrefThe specified key is not present in this container.  Bus object cycle is detected. The data type of bus element 'BusObject.a' was 'X' which is directly or indirectly referencing bus object 'BusObject'. The bus element data type was changed to double to eliminate the cycle.  Once this error occurs, the Bus Editor will not launch successfully unless MATLAB is restarted.
Workaround: Consider creating a Simulink.Bus object instead of Simulink.StructType and specifying that instead as the bus element's data type

Summary: When solving polynomials and polynomial systems with large integer or rational coefficients, MuPAD hangs
Description: When solving a polynomial or a system of polynomials with large integer or rational coefficients, the computation cannot be interrupted. The following example hangs your session. Save your data before trying it: In MuPAD: n := 2^1000 + 1: solve(x^2 + n*x + 43, x) In MATLAB: syms x;solve('x^2 + (2^1000 + 1)*x + 43') ?
Workaround: Replace large integers or rationals by symbols and then substitute them back into the solution.In MuPAD:solve(x^2 + C*x + 42, x) | C=2^1000 + 1In MATLAB:syms x n;s = solve('x^2 + n*x + 43');subs(s, n, '2^1000 + 1')Also, to compute a floating-point solution in MuPAD, use the float command:solve(x^2 + float(2^1000 + 1)*x + 43, x)The result is:{(-1.071508607)*10^301, (-4.01303356)*10^(-300)}?

Description: On Windows, selecting File > Print  on a Stateflow chart residing in a masked subsystem causes an error.

Summary: Standalone application fails to start when executed by relative path invocation (./) versus absolute path invocation.
Description: This occurs when the standalone resides within a folder that contains spaces in the folder name..

Type clear classes to start using the new version of the file. If the issue is not resolved at that point, then restart MATLAB.

Summary: Problems with setm when the map axes contains contourgroups
Description: If there's more than one projected contourgroup (created with contourm  or contourfm ) present in a map axes and you change the projection geometry with setm  (modify the MapProjection  or Origin  properties, for example), an error results. If you save the handle to a projected contourgroup in a variable and then the change the geometry of the parent map axes via setm , your variable will no longer refer to a valid handle. An error will result if you pass it to a function (like get ) that expects a handle.
Workaround: To work around this issue in R2007b through R2009b, replace the setm.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/setm.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: setm.m.oldCopy the version of setm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: rootmusic documentation incorrectly describes the output argument POW
Description: The rootmusic  documentation incorrectly states that the output argument POW 's unit is dB per radian/sample, which should be used to describe power spectral density. In fact, rootmusic  returns the power rather than the power spectral density and the power is not returned in dB.
Workaround: The elements of POW should be interpreted as the estimated magnitude square of the P complex exponentials making up the signal subspace.

Summary: Sporadic System.AccessViolationException occurs in MWArray.dll
Description: MWNumericArray  constructors that have signatures of the form: public MWNumericArray(int, int, T[]);public MWNumericArray(int, int, T[], bool);public MWNumericArray(int, int, T[], bool, bool); can cause sporadic crashes if the size of the byte array passed in is larger than the size of the MWNumericArray  being created. This results in an access violation error in MWArray.dll . In the above example, T  refers to CLS-compliant numeric types.
Workaround: Ensure the number of elements in T[] match the dimension specified in the MWNumericArray constructor.In this example, m and n are the specified dimensions and data is the .NET array passed in. The following code checks the size :if (data.LongLength != m*n){    throw new ArgumentException("Array size mismatch", "data");}MWNumericArray a = new MWNumericArray(m,n,data);

Summary: randsample returns the error "Edge vector must be monotonically non-decreasing"
Description: For sampling with replacement, randsample  accepts a weight vector, and normalizes it by dividing by its sum. However, this normalization can fail to correctly handle cases where the last two or more elements are very small relative to the sum, and randsample  can error. For example, w = [ones(1,13)/13 0 0];randsample(15,1,true,w)??? Error using ==> histcEdge vector must be monotonically non-decreasing.  Error in ==> randsample at 101               [~, y] = histc(rand(k,1),edges);
Workaround: Compute a weight vector that is correctly normalized, and pass that into randsample: p = w/sum(w);e = min([0 cumsum(p)],1);e(end) = 1;p = diff(e);randsample(15,1,true,p)

Summary: Error when trimming data with duplicate polar vertices
Description: When using geoshow  to view data for a region that includes duplicate polar vertices, or when using vmap0ui  to import VMAP0 data for a region that includes or is close to the poles, the following error may occur: "Error using ==> adjustPolarVertices>adjustPV at 544 polar vertices: Too many."
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the preprocessLatLonPolygons.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapproj/private/preprocessLatLonPolygons.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: preprocessLatLonPolygons.m.oldCopy the version of preprocessLatLonPolygons.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: AUTOSAR validation fails to identity issue that is reported during code generation
Description: When a model uses the AUTOSAR target, the signals entering the root output ports must not have a non-Auto  storage class. However, some violations of this restriction are not identified during AUTOSAR validation. Instead, these violations are reported during code generation.

Summary: PolySpace for C/C++ does not handle correctly modulo operator '%' on strictly negative values
Description: On C or C++ code with modulo operator '%' and with a strictly negative right operand, PolySpace does not consider that results could include the value 0. In the following example, all ranges given by PolySpace on the results of operator modulo '%' are wrong. They should contain 0: int main(void) {  volatile int alea ;  int z, x = alea ;  int y = alea ;  assert(x<0);  z = x % y ; /* wrong range on z: [-2^31+1 .. -1] and [-2^31 +1 .. 0] was expected */ assert(x == -4) ;  assert(y == 4) ;   if (z == (-4 % 4)) { /* wrong range on z: [-3 .. -1] and [-3 .. 0] was expected */  assert(1); /* grey code that should be green */ }}

Summary: Blocks generated for Box-Cox Transformations contain operators that fail
Description: The blocks that Model-Based Calibration Toolbox software generate for Box-Cox Transformations contain vector operators (for example, ., *, ./, .|, .^) that cause an error to occur.
Workaround: Replace the files mbcmodeloutput.m, YinvBuild.m, and YinvDiffBuild.m in the following locations of your MATLAB installation folder with the attached files:/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@mbcmodelooutput.m/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@xregmodel/YinvBuild.m/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@xregmodel/YinvDiffBuild.mThen, restart MATLAB.

Summary: Designing IIR filters with fdesign objects generates unnecessary license manager errors
Description: Invoking the design  method on a fdesign.response  object to design Butterworth, Chebyshev, or elliptic IIR filters unnecessarily checks out a Filter Design ToolboxTM  license. If a license is not available, the license manager throws an error. For example: >> h = fdesign.bandpass('n,fp1,fp2,ap');>> d = design(h,'cheby1');License checkout failed.License Manager Error -5Cannot find a license for filter_design_toolbox.Make sure an INCREMENT line for filter_design_toolbox exists in thelicense file, and that the formatting of the license file is correct.  Notice that despite the error message, the correct filter d  is returned by the design  method. The same error occurs when designing IIR filters with the filterbuilder  GUI.
Workaround: Follow these steps to replace the p-file schema.p.Determine the MATLAB? root directory on your machine. You can find the MATLAB root directory by using the matlabroot function at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename the MATLABROOT\toolbox\signal\sigtools\@fdopts\@sosscaling\schema.p file in your current MATLAB installation to another name, such as schema.p.old. MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory.Download the attached file schema.p to the directory in step 3.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Bioinformatics Toolbox functions GETBLAST and BLASTREAD fail to read some BLAST reports.
Description: GETBLAST  and BLASTREAD  can not find all the alignment records in a BLAST  report due to one or more of the headers of the alignments containing the word 'lambda' or extra '>' characters.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\blastread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\blastread.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfoRestart MATLAB.?

Summary: The analyze method of the commscope.eyediagram class errors out when an eye crossing time is within the [-0.5/Fs, 0] seconds interval.
Description: When you create an eye diagram object (commscope.eyediagram) and run the analyze method, an error occurs if the resulting eye diagram has a crossing that falls within the [-0.5/Fs, 0] seconds interval. The error message will be: ?? Subscript indices must either be real positive integers or logicals.
Workaround: Add a half-symbol duration delay to the current value in the MeasurementDelay property of the eye diagram object. This additional delay repositions the eye in the approximate center of the scope.

Summary: Numerical convergence problems when applying functions reckon and distance on an ellipsoid
Description: If you are working with an ellipsoid and apply the distance  function or the reckon  function to single-precision input, the internal computations will follow code paths that assume double-precision input. This can affect the quality of your result. In the worst-case situation, a while  loop may fail to converge, so that the MATLAB interpreter never returns from your function call. This problem does not apply when working with distance  or reckon  on a sphere, only on an ellipsoid with a non-zero eccentricity.
Workaround: If you're working with latitudes, longitudes, azimuths, or distances in single precision on an ellipsoid, make sure to convert all inputs to double before calling distance or reckon. After returning from these functions, you may convert their outputs to single precision if you wish.

Summary: When using PolySpace Model Link SL (for Simulink?), clicking a back to model link in PolySpace Viewer produces the following message: "Warning: Conversion rounded non-integer floating point value to the nearest int32 value."
Description: When using PolySpace Model link SL with a Simulink? model and Real-Time Workshop? Embedded Coder? generated code, clicking a "back to model" link in PolySpace Viewer produces the following warning:  Warning: Conversion rounded non-integer floating point value to the nearest int32 value  Note:  Most of the "back to model" links ususally work, only a few produce this warning.
Workaround: Note: This workaround works only for R2009b.On a Windows PolySpace Client system:Browse to the following folder:  %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Viewer\binCreate backup copies of the following files: PSThilite.exe  PSThilite-x64.exeDownload the attached file:  572473_Viewer.zip.Extract the two .exe files from the zip archive and copy them to the folder: %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Viewer\bin

Summary: Uncompilable code for server operation model when generating SIL block
Description: The software may produce uncompilable code when generating a SIL block for a server operation model. For example, where an Rte_<SWC>_PIM.h  file is referenced, you may see the following compilation error: c:\work\scratch\mforblock2_autosar_rtw\Rte_mforblock2_PIM.h(1) : error C3409: empty attribute block is not allowedNMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"c:\program files\microsoft visual studio 9.0\VC\BIN\cl.EXE"' : return code '0x2'Stop. ?
Workaround: Either avoid creating a SIL block, or insert any block that results in some instance memory being created, for example,  a Unit Delay block.

Summary: Peak Finder block may throw an internal error during code generation
Description: When you set the Maximum number of peaks to find  parameter of the Peak Finder block to 1 , an internal error can occur during code generation. The error message appears as follows: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: .\rtwcg_utils \rtwcg_prop.cpp line 1689: lhsType.isPointer()". Please report this to The MathWorks if you can cause it to recur.
Workaround: To work around this code generation issue in the Peak Finder block, set the Maximum number of peaks to find parameter to 2 (or any other integer value greater than 1). You can then generate code and use a Selector block to extract the first element of the output vector.

Summary: Setting breakpoints on Stateflow supertransitions does not work and might crash MATLAB
Description: Breakpoints set on Stateflow supertransitions do not break during a debugger session. Setting breakpoints on supertransitions during a debugger session might also crash MATLAB.
Workaround: In your chart, add a connecting simple transition and junction ahead of the supertransition for which you can set breakpoints.

Summary: Seg fault during compatibility/translation of models with Stateflow or Embedded MATLAB
Description: When you run Simulink Design Verifier software on a model containing Stateflow transitions or Embedded MATLAB code that uses the short-circuited operators && or ||, you might encounter a seg fault with a stack trace similar to: Stack Trace:  [0] libmwcgir_cgel.so:post_order_traverse_node(CG::Node*, CG::TypeChecker*)(0x100000001, 0x200000002, 0x100000004, 5) + 23 bytes  [1] libmwcgir_cgel.so:post_order_traverse_node(CG::Node*, CG::TypeChecker*)(0, 0x407e79b0 "p|~@", 0x0bd0df20,  0x2b2128e6c0e0) + 63 bytes   [2] libmwcgir_cgel.so:post_order_traverse_node(CG::Node*, CG::TypeChecker*)(0x407e7a50, 0x2aaacf8b7900, 0x0d178ce0, 0x407e7ca8) + 63 bytes  [3] libmwcgir_cgel.so:post_order_traverse_node(CG::Node*, CG::TypeChecker*)(0x0cf38ae0 "?1j??*", 0x0cdf5def, 0x3000000010, 81) + 63 bytes  [4] libmwcgir_cgel.so:cgl_yyparse()(0x407e7ff0, 0x2aaad846d8a3, 0x0bd0df20, 0x407e83d0) + 4941 bytes    ?
Workaround: You can patch this problem on 32-bit Windows installations by replacing the Simulink Design Verfier MEX-file with the attachment in this geck.1. Backup these two files to a location outside the MATLAB path:matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32.csf2. Download the attached files:slavteng.mexw32slavteng.mexw32.csf3. Save the downloaded files to matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv, replacing the existing files.

Summary: SIM and RTW target rebuild for a referenced model may fail if the model uses a library that is renamed or deleted
Description: When you have a referenced model that uses a library with a sublibrary, if you rename or delete the sublibrary, you can get a failure when rebuilding the Simulink (SIM) or Real-Time Workshop (RTW) target for that referenced model. This failure can occur if you do not save the referenced model before rebuilding the targets.
Workaround: One workaround is to save and rebuild the model that failed to rebuild.Alternatively, for R2009b only, you can prevent this problem from occurring by replacing matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/private/rtwinfomatman.p with the attached file, rtwinfomatman_R2009b.p.This file is only compatible with R2009b. If you would like a patch for other versions of MATLAB, please contact The MathWorks Technical Support at https://www.mathworks.com/support/service_requests/contact_support.do.

Summary: Simplifying expressions that involve derivatives of the dirac function can return wrong results
Description: The following code returns the simplified result that is incorrect: syms t y Y Z = solve('D2y + 3*Dy + 2*y = dirac(t, 2)', 'y(-2) = 0','Dy(-2) = 0', 't'); Z = simple(Z); pretty(Z) ans =  2 dirac(t) + exp(-t) heaviside(t) - 4 exp(-2 t) heaviside(t)
Workaround: Do not apply simplify or simple to derivatives of the dirac function.

Summary: Error message when building AUTOSAR server operation model with atomic function subsystem or atomic reusable function subsystem
Description: If your AUTOSAR server operation model has an atomic function subsystem or atomic reusable function subsystem, you see the following error message when you try to build the model: 'mbitfield2/SubSystem' can not generate a reusable function. Consider deselecting the option 'Generate reusable code' on the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop > Interface page. ?
Workaround: For the atomic subsystems, set Subsystem Parameters > Real-Time Workshop system code to Inline.

Summary: Code generation fails at compilation time when building an xPC Target model that contains IRQ Source or Scope (xPC) blocks and any blocks with labels IN or OUT
Description: Code generation fails at compilation time when building an xPC Target model that contains IRQ Source or Scope (xPC) blocks any blocks with labels IN  or OUT . The compilation errors look like: error C2143: syntax error : missing ':' before '['error C2059: syntax error : '['error C2059: syntax error : '}'error C2061: syntax error : identifier 'rc1_B'error C2059: syntax error : ';'
Workaround: As a workaround:Close MATLAB.Download the attached files xpcinterruptfunctions.tlc and scblock.tlc.Change folder to the MATLAB installation folder: matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/targets/xpc/target/build/tlcRename the orignal file xpcinterruptfunctions.tlc to xpcinterruptfunctions_Orig.tlc.BAK.Copy the new xpcinterruptfunctions.tlc file into the tlc folder.Change folder to the MATLAB installation folder: matlab/toolbox/rtw/targets/xpc/target/build/xpcblocks/tlc_cRename the original file scblock.tlc to scblock_Orig.tlc.BAK.Copy the new scblock.tlc file into the tlc_c folder.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Name of runnable for multi-runnable software component with one single function-call subsystem exported incorrectly
Description: A multi-runnable software component with only one single function-call triggered subsystem incorrectly picks up the step runnable and event name from the configuration of the top model instead of the subsystem.
Workaround: Set the step runnable and event name of the top model to be the same as the step runnable and event name of the subsystem.

Summary: Empty subsystems are not included in the repository manifest, preventing the library browser from updating correctly.
Description: Empty subsystems are not included in the repository manifest. This causes an internal error that prevents the library browser from updating correctly, but does not prevent it from functioning as expected otherwise.

Summary: When you save sym('D') to a file, the file fails to load properly into the MATLAB workspace
Description: If a sym  object uses a variable name 'D'  then saving it to a file and loading it again interprets 'D' as the MuPAD differential operator D .
Workaround: After loading the file, use the subs command to replace the 'D' value. For example: syms D y = 5*D+1; save test.mat y load test.mat y = feval(symengine,'subs',y,'D=DMLVar');

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Real-Time Workshop code generated for constant sample time signals output by a Model block may not reflect the true value of the output signal
Description: Real-Time Workshop generated code gives the wrong output value for a signal which meets all of the following conditions, the signal: Is output by a Model block Has a constant sample time (ts=inf) Has either an ImportedExtern  or ImportedExternPointer  storage class

Summary: SimMechanics AVI animation recording failure on some computers with older graphics card or graphics driver
Description: The ability to save AVI animation files in SimMechanics visualization in R2009a and R2009b uses an advanced graphics card feature which is not supported by some older graphics cards. If you cannot save an AVI animation file because of this bug, SimMechanics displays the following error: Warning: The AVI frame could not be captured. Your graphics card driver might not provide the required support. Update your graphics driver. This feature is not available on Windows Remote Desktop.
Workaround: Complete the following steps to resolve this problem in SimMechanics from R2009a and R2009b.Rename the file $MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\pm_visimpl\pm_visimpl\+PM_VIS\AnimFigVis.p to AnimFigVis_OLD.p.Download the file attached to this bug report and save or place it in $MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\pm_visimpl\pm_visimpl\+PM_VIS\.Enter the following commands at the MATLAB prompt:clear allclear classesrehash toolboxcacheNote: With this workaround, the saved AVI recording uses an older method for older graphics hardware, and the saved AVI animation might look different from a saved AVI animation from a newer graphics card. With the older graphics card and driver, the AVI saving method also might produce incorrect AVI recordings if other windows are brought on top of the SimMechanics visualization window during simulation.

Summary: Incorrect answer at root Outport block if input block has continuous sample time
Description: If a block is set to a continuous sample time and connects to the root Outport block, the value for the root Outport block from the Real-Time Workshop generated code might be incorrect. To determine if the input block to the root Outport block has a continuous sample time, in the Simulink model window, choose Format > Sample Time Display > Colors . You can not use signal logging to verify the existence of this bug. Even though signal logging result you get from Real-Time Workshop generated code is correct, the value from the Real-Time Workshop generated code might still be incorrect.
Workaround: Set a test point at the input signal of the root Outport block.

Summary: The BCH encoder object, fec.bchenc, produces wrong results if you set the GenPoly property to a generator polynomial that uses a non-default primitive polynomial
Description: BCH encoder object, fec.bchenc, can accept arbitrary generator polynomials through the GenPoly property. The default generator polynomial is the output of bchgenpoly(N,K) that uses the minimum weight primitive polynomial. You can also specify a generator polynomial that uses a non-minimum weight primitive polynomial, i.e. bchgenpoly(N,K,primPoly) In this case, the encoder ignores the defined primitive polynomial and uses a generator polynomial with a minimum weight primitive polynomial. Therefore, the encoded data do not conform to the generator polynomial in the GenPoly property.
Workaround: Follow these steps to replace the file encode.m.1. Quit MATLAB?. 2. Rename the encode.m file in your current MATLAB installation to another name, such as encode.m.old. This file is in MATLABROOT\toolbox\comm\comm\@fec\@bchenc\, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by using the matlabroot function at the MATLAB command prompt. 3. Download the attached file encode.m to the directory in step 2. 4. Restart MATLAB. 5. Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: The documentation incorrectly states that a stopping criteria of the patternsearch MADS algorithm is (mesh size) < (mesh tolerance).
Description: The documentation incorrectly states that a stopping criteria of the patternsearch  MADS algorithm is (mesh size) < (mesh tolerance). The correct stopping criterion is: (poll parameter) < (mesh tolerance).

Summary: The copy method of a channel object has unexpected behavior
Description: This bug is always observed in R2009a and earlier versions of Matlab. In the R2009b version the bug is only observed when legacychannelsim is true. The random number generator state of a channel object is not copied correctly to the new object. The effect of this is that the copied object starts to generate channel coefficients as if it had been reset.
Workaround: For R2009b, setting legacychannelsim to false avoids this problem by using the latest version of the random number generator.For R2009a and earlier versions of Matlab, there is no workaround.

Summary: On Windows OS architecture, PolySpace Queue Manager Server fails as soon as a 'Follow-Progress' is used more than two times on same analysis.
Description: Trying to follow-up stages of a verification using 'Follow-Progress' could stop PolySpace Queue Manager Server and fails verification on a CPU (server performing the analysis). It happens when using 'Follow-Progress' many times on same medium analysis running on the CPU. Note that it happens only on a Windows Server OS architecture.
Workaround: It is recommended to use 'View log File' instead of 'Follow-Progress' to see progression of an analysis.You can install the 'patched' version of the spooler in attachment. Installation instructions are given in the archive. Note that the following attachments only works for R2009b release.?

Summary: PolySpace does not support two levels of renaming of an enum value for a function
Description: When an enum value is used in a case statement, two levels of renaming the enum value for a function is not supported (one level is ok). The error messages will look like this: Verifying test-> Verifier found an error in file3.ada:50:17: choice given in case statement is not static-> Verifier found an error in file3.ada:38:39: dynamic or empty choice in aggregate must be the only choice You could get one or both of these messages, depending on the code. Here is a reproduction of the problem: package T2 is type TYPE_R is (A, B, C); for TYPE_R use (A => 0, B => 1, C => 2);end T2;with T2; use T2; package T5 is function A return TYPE_R renames T2.A;end T5;package T3 is  type ENUM_2 is (F_2, F_3);end T3;with T3; package T4 is subtype ENUM_2 is T3.ENUM_2; function F_2 return ENUM_2 renames T3.F_2; function F_3 return ENUM_2 renames T3.F_3;end T4;with T5; with T2; use T2;with T4;with T3;package T6 is function A return TYPE_R renames T5.A; subtype ENUM_2 is T4.ENUM_2;  function F_2 return ENUM_2 renames T4.F_2; function F_3 return ENUM_2 renames T4.F_3;end T6;with T6; package Test is type ARRAY_T is array (T6.ENUM_2) of Integer; MY_ARRAY : constant ARRAY_T := (T6.F_2 => 1, --> Verifier found an error: dynamic or empty choice in aggregate must be the only choice T6.F_3 => 2); procedure Main;end Test;with T2; package body Test is Var : T2.TYPE_R; procedure Main is  begin case Var is when T6.A => null; -- > Verifier found an error: choice given in case statement is not static when others => null; end case; end Main; end Test;

Summary: Unexpected millisecond precision warning
Description: Certain values of the Period and StartDelay properties of the Timer issue a millisecond precision warning when they should not. For example, the following statement issues such a warning:     timer('StartDelay', 2.001)  The cause of the problem is a loss of precision when MATLAB evaluates whether sub-millisecond timing has been requested.
Workaround: Ignore the warning.

Summary: Japanese Issue: Simulink "Dead Zone" block has a mistranslation.
Description: On Simulink "Dead Zone" block UI on a Japanese PC, "Start of dead zone" and "End of dead zone" are mistranslated as "˛»¸ĐŽˇ¤ÎÉĎĎŢ" and "˛»¸ĐŽˇ¤ÎĎÂĎŢ", respectively. The two translations need to be switched.
Workaround: - Download the attached trFixInDeadZoneBlock.p- Start MATLAB- Run trFixInDeadZoneBlock on the Command Window as>> trFixInDeadZoneBlock

Summary: Accessing a variable after it is subscripted in a parfor loop generates an error if MATLAB cannot unambiguously determine that its name refers to a variable
Description: When you have a script that contains a parfor  loop with a subscripted input variable, if you access that variable after the parfor block, you get an error unless MATLAB can unambiguously determine that the name refers to a variable. For example, given the script repro.m : parfor i = 1:1   T(i) end T  If you enter the following at the command prompt: >> T = 1:10; >> repro  it generates the error: ??? Undefined function or variable 'T'. Error in ==> repro at 4 T  In the example, repro.m  has a sliced input variable T  for the parfor  loop, then tries to access T  after that loop.
Workaround: To work around this problem, assign variables used in the parfor body to temporaries before the parfor, and use those temporaries in the parfor block. The first line in the following example ensures that T1 is recognized as a variable:T1 = T;parfor i = 1:1   T1(i) end T1

Summary: PolySpace does not support partial paths when a father or son package is called
Description: PolySpace is not permissive on partial paths when a father or son package is called. Example: package P1 isend;  package P1.H1 isend;  package P1.H1.H2 isend;  package P1.H1.H2.F2 is  procedure F1(I:Integer);end;  package P1.I1 isend;  package P1.I1.I2 is  procedure PROC;end P1.I1.I2 ;  -- with P1.H1; -- a possible workaroundwith P1.H1.H2.F2;package body P1.I1.I2 is  procedure PROC is  begin  H1.H2.F2.F1(1); -- problem here  end PROC;end P1.I1.I2 ;  package Test is  procedure Main;end;  package body Test is  procedure Main is  begin  null;  end;end;  The error message will be something like :%%%ERROR%%%Verifying p1-i1-i2-> Verifier found an error in file.ada:26:07: "H1" is not visible-> Verifier found an error in file.ada:26:07: non-visible declaration at file.ada:4%%%ERROR%%%
Workaround: See the example : providing a with clause will remove the ambiguity.

Summary: Resizing on derived types is not supported
Description: Resizing on derived types is not supported and will lead to the error : -> Verifier found an error in exemple_resizing.adb:78:08: incorrect size for type "RECORD_PACK_1", declared as 216 bits. Must be at least 256 (the values 216 and 256 depend on the code obviously) Example : %%%CODE%%% package Test is  SIZE_16_C : constant := 16;  type INT16_T is range -(2 ** (SIZE_16_C - 1)) .. (2 ** (SIZE_16_C - 1) - 1); for INT16_T'SIZE use SIZE_16_C;  SIZE_8_C : constant := 8;  type INT8_T is range -(2 ** (SIZE_8_C - 1)) .. (2 ** (SIZE_8_C - 1) - 1); for INT8_T'SIZE use SIZE_8_C;  type UINT16_T is mod 2**SIZE_16_C; for UINT16_T'SIZE use SIZE_16_C;  type REAL_T is digits 6;  type ENUM_1 is (VALUE_1, VALUE_2); for ENUM_1 use (VALUE_1 => 0, VALUE_2 => 1);  for ENUM_1'size use 8;  subtype TYPE_1 is INT8_T range -1 .. 1;  for TYPE_1'value_size use INT8_T'size;  subtype INDEX_PACK_1 is ENUM_1;  type PACK_1 is array (INDEX_PACK_1) of UINT16_T;  pragma pack (PACK_1);  type ENUM_2 is (VALUE_2,VALUE_3);  for ENUM_2 use (VALUE_2 => 0,VALUE_3 => 1);  for ENUM_2'size use 8;  subtype INDEX_PACK_2 is ENUM_1;  type PACK_2 is array (INDEX_PACK_2) of TYPE_1;  pragma pack (PACK_2);  subtype INDEX_PACK_3 is ENUM_1;  type PACK_3 is array (INDEX_PACK_3) of REAL_T;  pragma pack (PACK_3);  type RECORD_1 is  record  FIELD_1 : INT16_T;  FIELD_2 : PACK_1;  FIELD_3 : PACK_1;  FIELD_4 : ENUM_2;  FIELD_5 : PACK_2;  FIELD_6 : PACK_3;  FIELD_7 : UINT16_T;  FIELD_8 : REAL_T;  end record;  for RECORD_1 use  record  FIELD_1 at 0 range 0 .. 15;  FIELD_2 at 2 range 0 .. 31;  FIELD_3 at 6 range 0 .. 31;  FIELD_4 at 10 range 0 .. 7;  FIELD_5 at 11 range 0 .. 15;  FIELD_6 at 16 range 0 .. 63;  FIELD_7 at 24 range 0 .. 15;  FIELD_8 at 28 range 0 .. 31;  end record;  for RECORD_1'size use 256;  type RECORD_PACK_1 is new RECORD_1;  for RECORD_PACK_1 use  record  FIELD_1 at 0 range 0 .. 15; -- 16 bits  FIELD_2 at 0 range 16 .. 47; -- 32 bits  FIELD_3 at 0 range 48 .. 79; -- 32 bits  FIELD_4 at 0 range 80 .. 80; -- 1 bit  FIELD_5 at 0 range 81 .. 96; -- 16 bits  FIELD_6 at 0 range 97 .. 160; -- 64 bits  FIELD_7 at 0 range 161 .. 176; -- 16 bits  FIELD_8 at 0 range 177 .. 208; -- 32 bits  end record;  for RECORD_PACK_1'size use 216; -- Problem : incorrect size for type "RECORD_PACK_1", declared as 216 bits. Must be at least 256  procedure Main;end Test;package body Test is  procedure Main is  begin  null;  end Main;end Test;  %%%CODE%%%

Summary: Why do I receive an error when reading a GSE formatted file using GEOSERIESREAD or GETGEODATA?
Description: The GEOSERIESREAD  function does not properly handle missing values. Missing values are represented with the string "Null " in GSE formatted files.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\geoseriesread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\geoseriesread.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarrayRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Macintosh? running 64-bit MATLAB? with JRE 1.6: Data tip in the Editor may show old version of a variable
Description: When running Macintosh? 64-bit versions of MATLAB?, data tips do not update properly, and therefore are not always correct.
Workaround: For MATLAB? Version 7.9 (R2009b), install the patch, patch.jar, from the attached zip file as follows:Start MATLAB.Determine the MATLAB root path by typing matlabroot in the Command Window.Download patch.jar.Copy patch.jar to matlabroot/java, where matlabroot is the path returned in step 1.In the MATLAB Command Window, type the following:cd(matlabroot); cd('java'); unzip('patch.jar');Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Verification fails sporadically with the message: 'signal 11, signal 5, virtual free decommit failed or fread()'
Description: PolySpace verification fails sporadically at different stages with different types of messages. The failure messages include the following:: * Level 1 Software Safety Analysis - Possible Scalar OVFL conversion from[0..255] to [-128..127] found "Ff7.ada" line 243, column 18 - analyzing V719.V71A::VDF1 : #<procedure>Received signal 5exception SysErr(OS.SysErr(name="Function not implemented: kill",syserror=nosys)) raised. **** Software Safety Analysis Level 1 - 3 (Saving files)**** Software Safety Analysis Level 1 - 3 (Saving files) took 0real, 0u + 0s Generating GUI filesExited on signal 11 > cache 100% full, cleaning up. - 54s elapsed (wall clock). - analyzing V1.V2FAB : #<procedure>VirtualFree decommit failedFATAL ERROR while processing v1.dfa The problem occurs on both Windows and on Linux.  ?
Workaround: Note that the following workaround only corrects the 'fread()' problem.To workaround this issue on a Windows system (on client side and server side):Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32Create backup copies of the 10 following files: alias-server.cx86-mingw32.exe, iabc-c2if.cx86-mingw32.exe, iabc-il2be.cx86-mingw32.exe, rte-kernel.cx86-mingw32.exe, edgcpfe.c.x86-mingw32.exe, iabc-cpp2if.cx86-mingw32.exe, iabc012.cx86-mingw32.exe, summary-rules.cx86-mingw32.exe, gen-gui-files.cx86-mingw32.exe and iabc-il.cx86-mingw32.exeDownload 569781_Windows_[1-7].zip files in attachment to this bug report.Extract all files from each zip.Copy the 10 files into the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-mingw32To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/x86-linux.Create backup copies of the 10 following files: alias-server.cx86-linux, gen-gui-files.cx86-linux, iabc-cpp2if.cx86-linux, iabc-il2be.cx86-linux, summary-rules.cx86-linux, edgcpfe.c.x86-linux, iabc-c2if.cx86-linux, iabc-il.cx86-linux, iabc012.cx86-linux and rte-kernel.cx86-linuxDownload 569781_Linux_[1-7].zip files in attachment to this bug report.Extract all files from each zip.Copy these 10 files into the folder $POLYSPACE_C/Verifier/bin/x86-linux

Summary: SEGV on property access through handle where property can be different subclass instances.
Description: Accessing properties of different subclass instances through a handle may cause a SEGV. If obj1 is an instance of a handle class with a property obj2, and obj2 is an instance of a handle class with multiple subclasses, then calling the function foo, as shown here, can cause MATLAB to crash:    % A is a common superclass of B and C   obj1.obj2 = B;  % B is a subclass of A with property p.   foo(obj1);   obj1.obj2 = C;  % C is a subclass of A with property p.   foo(obj1);   function x = foo(obj)   x = obj.obj2.p;   % This can crash.
Workaround: Add the following command at the beginning of the function that causes the SEGV:    feature scopedAccelEnablement off

Summary: Drag and drop from the Simulink Library Browser into the Simulink model editor may not work on some multi-display configurations on Mac OS X.
Description: On multi-display Mac OS X systems where the Mac main menu bar is not on the leftmost display, drag and drop from the Simulink Library Browser to a Simulink model editor window fails. Either nothing is added to the model, or the block is added in a location far from where you dropped the block. Drag and drop between Simulink model editor windows are unaffected by this issue.
Workaround: Either:Right-click the Add to <model_name> menu item to insert the block in the foremost editor window.Use the Mac OS X Displays preference pane to reposition your menu bar.To reposition your menu bar:Open System Preferences from the Apple menu.Click on the Displays icon.Click on the Arrangement tab.Drag the iconic representation of the menu bar so that it is on the leftmost display.Close System Preferences, and relaunch MATLAB.

Summary: Displaying expression value of bus type with an enumerated element crashes MATLAB
Description: Omitting the semicolon after an expression in a chart displays the results of the expression in the MATLAB Command Window. If the value of an expression is of bus type containing one or more enumerated elements, displaying the expression value crashes MATLAB.
Workaround: If display of an expression value is not intended, complete the expression in your chart with a semicolon, so that the result does not appear. Otherwise, display elements of a structure value in the MATLAB Command Window by using multiple expressions.

Summary: Bad inputs in randsample can produce infinite loop of warning messages
Description: The randsample  function accepts a population or population size as its first input argument, and the number of values, K , to select as its second input argument. If you incorrectly provide a non-integer value for K , randsample  may produce a warning message that does not describe the error. If K  is less than 1, randsample  may produce an infinite loop of warning messages.
Workaround: Use ctrl-C to stop the warning messages, and correct the syntax by providing an integer value for K.

Summary: Simulating a model containing a Repeating Sequence Interpolated block might cause an error?
Description: Simulating a model containing a Repeating Sequence Interpolated block causes an error if you do the following: Add a Repeating Sequence Interpolated block from the Simulink/Sources library to a new model. Save and close the model. Reopen the model. Simulate or update the model.  Opening the saved model changes the output data type of the Repeating Sequence Interpolated block from its default data type double  to an slDataTypeAndScale string. Simulink generates an error when you simulate or update the model because it cannot evaluate this slDataTypeAndScale string.
Workaround: To fix this problem, use one of the following solutions.Manually change the output data type of the Repeating Sequence Interpolated block to double:Double-click the block to open the Source Block Parameters dialog box.Click the Signal Attributes tab.Set the Output data type of the block to double.Save the model.Or, use the attached script to fix the issue automatically:Open the model.Unzip the attached file, attachment_569568_20090814.zip.Save the script from the zip file, fixRepeatedSequenceDialogError.m, in a folder on your MATLAB path.Run the script from the MATLAB command line, passing in the name of the model as a parameter. For example:  fixRepeatedSequenceDialogError('my_model')Save the model.

Summary: Lookup Table (n-D) block might generate shared utility function among blocks when unique functions should be generated
Description: When a model has multiple Lookup Table (n-D) blocks, a shared utility function might be generated when the utility functions should be unique.  This behavior can occur only when the following conditions are true: The fraction is floating point (from explicit specification or inheritance via internal rule, when any port is floating point).The table and the intermediate results are both fixed-point data types.There are multiple Lookup Table (n-D) blocks with these types and the same output types.All the tables have the same Word length  and all the intermediate results have the same Word length .The difference between the Fraction length  of the table and intermediate results is the same for different blocks.The Fraction length  for the table data type is unique for different blocks. Under these conditions, all but one of these blocks might return the wrong result.

Summary: Functions ltln2val and setpostn no longer accept logical grids
Description: Changes introduced in R2008a while fixing bugs in functions ltln2val  (see Bug Report 416944 ) and setpostn  (see Bug Report 173338 ) caused these functions to become overly strict when checking their input arguments. Through R2007a their first input argument, a regular geographic data grid Z , was allowed to be either numeric or logical. After the changes, Z  was no longer allowed to be class logical. This limitation means that the following toolbox clients of ltln2val  and setpostn  no longer support logical grids either: filterm , mapprofile , maptrims , and neworig .
Workaround: When calling any of the functions listed above with a logical grid, you can convert the input Z using the MATLAB(R) uint8 function.For a more comprehensive workaround, modify the versions of ltln2val.m and setpostn.m in your current installation as follows:Make backup copies of the following files:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/ltln2val.mandmatlabroot/toolbox/map/map/setpostn.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: ltln2val.m.oldIn ltln2val.m, edit the line:validateattributes(Z,{'numeric'},{'2d','real'},mfilename,'Z',1)to read as follows:validateattributes(Z,{'numeric','logical'},{'2d','real'},mfilename,'Z',1)In setpostn.m, remove the line:validateattributes(Z,{'numeric'},{'2d'},mfilename,'Z',1)Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?

Summary: For a Merge block inside an Iterator Subsystem, the Simulink run-time diagnostic may incorrectly produce an error message.
Description: When the Configuration Parameter, Detect multiple driving blocks executing at the same time step , is set to error  and a Merge block is inside a For Iterator Subsystem or a While Iterator Subsystem, the following may occur, Simulation Diagnostics window displays an error message. The error message indicates multiple driving blocks are concurrenlty updating the Merge block even though the control logic for such driving blocks is mutually exclusive in each iteration step.
Workaround: In the Simulink window and from the Simulation menu, select Configuration Parameters.In the left pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, open the Diagnostics section, and then select Data Validity.From the Detect multiple driving blocks executing at the same time step list, select either warning or none.From the Underspecified initialization detection list, select Classic.?

Summary: linkage function warns incorrectly about a non-monotonic tree
Description: The linkage  function may warn about a non-monotonic hierarchical cluster tree even when the tree is monotonic.  A non-monotonic  tree is one where a parent node is at a lower join height than one of its children.  This warning can occur when the dissimilarity/distance matrix that you pass into linkage  contains a large number of dissimilarities/distances that are zero or extremely small relative to other distances in the matrix.
Workaround: If you receive a warning about a non-monotonic tree, you can check if the warning can safely be ignored, using the values in the third column of the matrix linkage returns. That column contains the heights of the nodes in the tree. Compare their first differences to the values themselves. For example, if Y is your distance matrix,Z = linkage(Y)diff(Z(:,3)) ./ Z(1:end-1,3)If all of the values are either positive, or negative but very small in magnitude (on the order of a few multiples of -eps), you can safely ignore the warning. If you find negative values larger than that, the tree is non-monotonic and the warning is correct. This typically happens for the median and centroid linkages, and is an indication that you should use another type of linkage.

Summary: Parameters used in the If and Fcn block expressions are not tunable
Description: Parameters used in the If and Fcn block expressions are not tunable in both normal and accelerated mode simulations, or in any generated code. This means that any parameters used in the expression cannot be tuned from a Model block referencing the model containing the If or Fcn block by using Model arguments.

Summary: The standard function "sqrt" produces an unjustified red overflow check with a double, 32-bit target
Description: When the target uses a 'double ' type on 32 bits, there is a red overflow check in __polyspace__stdstubs.c  in sqrt() line 882: APPLY_CONSTRAINT(result < 1.3409e154); // sqrt(DBL_MAX)  This occurs because the software assumes that a double is on 64 bits.
Workaround: To avoid red overflows in the "sqrt" function, relaunch your verification with the option: '-D __polyspace_no_sqrt'. PolySpace will stub the 'sqrt' function instead of providing its own function.

Summary: Memory leaks during Simulink and Stateflow printing
Description: When printing from Simulink or Stateflow systems, there is a resource leak that?can lead to crash in MATLAB or memory growing out of bounds.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using data type replacement to map boolean_T to an unsigned integer data type might cause incorrect code to be generated
Description: Using data type replacement, you can map the boolean_T  built-in data type to an unsigned integer data type, such as uint32_T , or to a signed integer data type, such as int32_T , in the generated code. However, mapping boolean_T  to an unsigned integer might change the numerical result of executing expressions in the generated code, due to the application of C language integral promotion rules. For example, if a boolean_T  operand in a relational operation maps to an unsigned integer, the other operand in the operation is promoted to an unsigned integer. If that other operand was signed and contained a negative value, it is promoted to a positive value. Therefore, the numerical result of executing the expression might be different than if the boolean_T  data type was mapped to a signed integer. Note:  This issue occurs only if you use data type replacement specifically to map boolean_T  to an unsigned integer data type. It does not  occur under any of the following conditions: Data type replacement is not used. Data type replacement is used but boolean_T  is not specified for replacement on the Data Type Replacement  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Data type replacement is used to map boolean_T  to a signed integer data type.
Workaround: To work around this bug, you can do either of the following:Avoid using data type replacement to replace the boolean_T built-in data type.Replace boolean_T only with a signed integer data type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrete Transfer Fcn block can generate incorrect results in simulation and code generation
Description: The Discrete Transfer Fcn block incorrectly allows you to specify slope and bias data types on the block dialog. The block correctly prevents signals with non-zero bias and non-power-of two slope from coming in on the input or from being defined in the 'Fixed point' mode of the Data Type Assistant. However, when you select 'Expression' for the 'Mode' parameter in the Data Type Assistant, the block incorrectly allows you to define a data type with non-zero bias and/or non-power-of-two slope. Doing so gives incorrect results in both simulation and code generation.
Workaround: When you select 'Expression' for the 'Mode' parameter in the Data Type Assistant, do not define a data type with non-zero bias and/or non-power-of-two slope.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrete Filter block can generate incorrect results in simulation and code generation
Description: The Discrete Filter block incorrectly allows you to specify slope and bias data types on the block dialog. The block correctly prevents signals with non-zero bias and non-power-of two slope from coming in on the input or from being defined in the 'Fixed point' mode of the Data Type Assistant. However, when you select 'Expression' for the 'Mode' parameter in the Data Type Assistant, the block incorrectly allows you to define a data type with non-zero bias and/or non-power-of-two slope. Doing so gives incorrect results in both simulation and code generation.
Workaround: When you select 'Expression' for the 'Mode' parameter in the Data Type Assistant, do not define a data type with non-zero bias and/or non-power-of-two slope.

Summary: Opening System Design Description report setup file can cause spurious warnings
Description: Opening the System Design Description (SDD) rerport setup file in the Report Explorer causes spurious warnings to appear at the MATLAB command line on installations that do not include the Simulink Verification and Validation toolbox. This happens, for example, if you select Customize report...  on the SDD dialog box.
Workaround: Copy the attached files into the MATLABROOT/toolbox/rptgenext/rptgenext/@StdRptDlg/@SDD directory on your system. Note that the attached file, SDD.rpt, replaces the SDD.rpt file installed on your system.

Summary: MuPAD Command Bar inserts command template with misspelled option name.
Description: The MuPAD Command Bar menu entry General Math > Combine > Sine / Cosine Hyp  inserts the template combine(#, sincosh) . The option name is misspelled, and the evaluation of the expression fails. The correct name for the option is sinhcosh .
Workaround: After you insert the template combine(#, sincosh), add the missing letter h to get combine(#, sinhcosh).

Summary: S-Function Builder returns warning messages while generating code for an S-function with more than 9 parameters
Description: The S-Function Builder returns warning messages while generating code for an S-function that uses more than 9 parameters. If you are using a GCC compiler, the warning messages are as follows: warning: multi-character character constant warning: overflow in implicit constant conversion

Summary: Feature filling error or incomplete table filling
Description: The default values for table filling can incorrectly include a scalar value for normalizer variables. These defaults can cause an error when you try to fill a feature, or result in a limited number of table cells being filled.
Workaround: To work around this problem, you must set the input variables for normalizers to the normalizer values. When you use the Feature Filling Wizard, manually set all variables of normalizers to the normalizer values by clicking the 'Initialize from Normalizer' button on screen 3 of the wizard.

Summary: S-functions in the Simulink Fixed Point API error out unnecessarily when  operating on numeric types with no scaling
Description: S-functions using functions in the Simulink Fixed Point API error out unnecessarily when they operate on numeric types that are aliases to fixed-point types with no scaling, for example, a binary type with a fraction length of 0. The error message is "The data type id does not correspond to a Simulink builtin or fixed-point data type". The affected functions in the API include:  ssFxpConvert  ssFxpConvertFromRealWorldValue ssFxpConvertToRealWorldValue  ssFxpGetU32BitRegion  ssFxpSetU32BitRegion  ssGetDataTypeBias  ssGetDataTypeFixedExponent  ssGetDataTypeFracSlope  ssGetDataTypeFractionLength  ssGetDataTypeFxpContainWordLen  ssGetDataTypeFxpIsSigned  ssGetDataTypeFxpWordLength  ssGetDataTypeIsFixedPoint  ssGetDataTypeIsFloatingPoint  ssGetDataTypeIsScalingPow2  ssGetDataTypeIsScalingTrivial  ssGetDataTypeNumberOfChunks  ssGetDataTypeStorageContainCat  ssGetDataTypeStorageContainerSize  ssGetDataTypeTotalSlope  ssRegisterDataTypeFxpBinaryPoint  ssRegisterDataTypeFxpFSlopeFixExpBias  ssRegisterDataTypeFxpScaledDouble  ssRegisterDataTypeFxpSlopeBias
Workaround: When calling a Simulink Fixed Point API S-function, replace the data type ID that you supply to the function with its underlying base type.To obtain the base type, use the function "ssGetDataTypeIdAliasedThruTo". For example, newId = ssGetDataTypeIdAliasedThruTo(S, oldId); ?
