Summary: Extra characters in code for IconEditor example in MATLAB GUI documentation prevents it from executing
Description: The doc example file iconEditor.m contains extraneous apostrophe characters that cause the following errors when it is run: run ([docroot '\techdoc\creating_guis\examples\iconEditor'])??? Undefined function or method 'ctranspose' for input arguments of type'function_handle'.Error in ==> iconEditor at 91hIconFileButton =   uicontrol(...Error in ==> run at 57          evalin('caller', [s ';']); The problem is caused by spurious apostrophes ("' ") at the end of three function handles, in lines 94, 124, and 130:  'Callback',@hIconFileButtonCallback',... 'Callback',@hOKButtonCallback'); 'Callback',@hCancelButtonCallback'); This example is referenced by the MATLAB documentation page "Examples of GUIs Created Programmatically:Icon Editor" that you can open with the following command: web([docroot '/techdoc/creating_guis/f14-998767.html#f14-1002054'])
Workaround: You can edit iconEditor.m and remove the last apostrophes in lines 94, 124, and 130 of iconEditor.m to make the example execute:edit([docroot '\techdoc\creating_guis\examples\iconEditor.m'])Or, you can download the attached file and store it as[docroot '\techdoc\creating_guis\examples\iconEditor.m']where docroot is the root of the MATLAB documentation tree as returned by the docroot command.Depending on how MATLAB has been installed, you might or might not have write access to documentation directories. If you do not, you cannot save the edited file where it currently exists. Instead, place the updated example code file in a writable folder on the MATLAB path. Make sure that the directory you use is closer to the top of the path than docroot\techdoc\creating_guis\examples\, which you can add to the path by clicking certain links within GUI examples in the Help Browser.

Summary: MetaData constructor does not search the MATLAB path for the input file when the PATH parameter is not specified.
Description: MetaData  constructor does not search the MATLAB path for the input file when the PATH  parameter is not specified. It searches the local path only. This problem will cause examples in the documentation about the MetaData  object to fail, and maexptdemo  to fail if run from outside the biodemos directory.
Workaround: Download the attached file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\+bioma\+data\@MetaData\MetaData.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\+bioma\+data\@MetaData\MetaData.m.old, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\+bioma\+data\@MetaData\Restart MATLAB.?

Description: If a Physical Modeling XML file contains one or more bodies with more than 20 attached coordinate systems, the mech_import  command throws the following error while importing the XML file. ??? Error using ==> CSBuildInterface.build at 83Body block (mask) does not have a parameter named 'CS21Pos'.Error in ==> S:\Aphysmod_jul16\matlab\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@CSUpdater\build.p>build at 5hThis = thisUpdater.BuildInterface.build(parentBuildInterface,thisUpdater.Reader,thisUpdater.Graph);
Workaround: Use the following steps to resolve this problem in MATLAB releases R2009a and R2008b.Rename the files$MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@CSBuildInterface\build.ptobuild_old.p$MATLABROOT is your MATLAB root directory, as returned at the command line by the function matlabroot.Download the attached build.p file and place it in the $MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@CSBuildInterface directory.Enter the following at the MATLAB command line:clear allclear classesrehash toolboxcache

Summary: Physical mixer blocks handle user-specified phase noise data incorrectly
Description: A bug in RF Blockset 1.0 (R14) affects how the following blocks perform phase noise analysis: General Mixer S-Parameters Mixer Y-Parameters Mixer Z-Parameters Mixer
Workaround: Follow these steps to download a revised version of the file analyze.p:Determine the MATLAB root folder (matlabroot) by entering the command matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Find the folder containing the file analyze.p:If you are using RF Blockset 1.3.1 (R2006b) or later, navigate to the folder matlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblks\@rfbbequiv\@nonlinear\If you are using RF Blockset 1.3 (R2006a) or earlier, navigate to the folder matlabroot\toolbox\rfblks\rfblks\@rfmodel\@nonlinear\Rename the file analyze.p to analyze.p.old.Download the attached revision of the file analyze.p. Place it in the same folder as analyze.p.old.Restart MATLAB.Enter the command rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB command prompt.?

Summary: In Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode, a model may fail to rebuild after an internal change to an S-function
Description: If a standard S-function or an S-function associated with the S-Function Builder experiences a change to its internal algorithm, Simulink should rebuild the code needed for Accelerator mode or Rapid Accelerator mode simulation. However, Simulink might not perform this task. In the case of Accelerator mode simulation, Simulink will fail to recognize the need to rebuild if the S-function is using TLC for the Accelerator. In the case of the Rapid Accelerator mode simulation, Simulink will fail to recognize the need to rebuild for any internal change to the S-function. ?
Workaround: As a workaround, perform one of the following steps as appropriate.For Accelerator mode simulation, as applicable, either:refrain from using a TLC wih the Accelerator, orwhen making an internal change to an S-function, delete the Accelerator MEX-file (modelName_acc.mexext) to force a rebuild.For Rapid Accelerator mode simulation, delete the Rapid Accelerator executable which is in the folder 'modelName_raccel_rtw' and which has the name 'modelName'.

Summary: MATLAB? TODO/FIXME Report: Japanese characters typed in the NOTE field do not display properly.
Description: Japanese and other special characters are not compatible with the NOTE  field in the TODO/FIXME Report. If you enter such characters in the NOTE  field, they do not appear as expected. If you attempt to run the report anyway, it indicates that no files contain the characters you attempted to specify, even if they exist in one or more of the files evaluated for the report.

Summary: In Embedded MATLAB, using eml.varsize on a variable with a use that is always an empty matrix may cause an assertion
Description: If you use eml.varsize  to force a variable to have varying size when some of its uses are always empty matrices, you may get an assertion like this: Assertion failedisa(aType) at line 57 of file ".\base_core\type\PointerType.cpp".
Workaround: Choose one of these options:Do not use eml.varsize with the variable.If only some of the uses of the variable are always empty, replace those uses with empty matrices.For example, if you have an Embedded MATLAB function in which x is always an empty matrix when you assign its value to y, replace y = x with y = [].Assign the variable to a non-empty matrix inside a conditional expression that is false and not constant.For example: ...eml.varsize('x',[1 100]);x = [];if y == u %Conditional expression must be false and not constant   x = [1 2];end...

Summary: Cannot enable multicore processor support for xPC Target Standalone mode
Description: Cannot enable multicore processor support for xPC Target Standalone mode. Even if you select multicore processor support, the executable on the target PC still uses the single core kernel. This issue does not exist for other boot options: Boot floppy, CD Boot, DOS Loader, Network Boot.
Workaround: As a workaroundClose MATLAB.Download the attached file xpcload.m and save it to: matlabroot\toolbox/rtw/targets/xpc/xpc/privateRestart MATLAB.Create a new Standalone mode kernel.Reboot your target PC with the new kernel.Rebuild your model.

Summary: "timeout expired" error occurs when you use AI getsample
Description: A "timeout expired" error occurs when you use AI getsample for certain slow sample rate devices such as the NI USB-9219. AI = analoginput('nidaq','DeviceID');chan = addchannel(AI,1);samples= getsample(AI)??? NIDAQmx error : Measurements: Specified operation did not complete, because the specified timeout expired. Device: Dev1 Task Name: MWDAT0037 Status Code: -200474Error in ==> analoginput.getsample at 40 samples = getsample(uddobj);Error in ==> testni at 18 data=getsample(AI);
Workaround: To workaround this issue, add an entry to the mwnidaqmx.ini file,Quit MATLAB.Go to the MATLAB root directory and find mwnidaqmx.ini file:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwnidaqmx.ini Make a copy of this file and rename it to mwnidaqmx.ini.old.Open the file mwnidaqmx.ini in a text editor:Find the entry for the device (e.g. USB-9219)Add this line: timeoutForNIDAQmxOps = 1For example, the new entry for the USB-9219 device should now read: [USB-9219] hasAnalogInput = true numAIChannels = 4 hasAnalogOutput = false hasDigitalIO = false timeoutForNIDAQmxOps = 1Save mwnidaqmx.ini, close the file and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect timing for C674x models
Description: Code generated for C674x processors may run faster than expected. Rates in a Simulink model runs at (12.5/15) times the original. For example, a one second rate in your model runs every 0.833 seconds on the processor.
Workaround: To obtain the correct timing, open the Target Preferences block (e.g., C6747EVM) in your model and change the CPU Clock (MHz) parameter from 300 MHz to 360 MHz.

Summary: Using version 6 of the Microsoft Visual C/C++ compiler to build models that contain J1939 blocks fails
Description: Using version 6 of the Microsoft Visual C/C++ compiler to build models that contain J1939 blocks fails. In this case, the log contains unreserved external symbols such as the following: j1939lib_vc.lib(j1939lib.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol ___security_cookie j1939lib_vc.lib(j1939core.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol ___security_cookie j1939lib_vc.lib(j1939lib.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol @__security_check_cookie@4 j1939lib_vc.lib(j1939core.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol @__security_check_cookie@4 mxpc_compile_empty_xpc.dll : fatal error LNK1120: 2 unresolved externalsNMAKE : fatal error U1077: 'link' : return code '0x460'
Workaround: As a workaround,Close MATLAB.Download the attached files cantransportlib_vc.lib and j1939lib_vc.lib and save them to:matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\libRestart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: CDF library can become vulnerable when accessing compromised CDF
Description: NASA has released version 3.3.0 of the CDF library to address a potential issue of the library becoming vulnerable when accessing a compromised CDF. Earlier version of the library have this vulnerability, including version 3.2.1, the version of the library that ships with R2009b. For detailed information about the vulnerability, see:?https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2009-2850 .
Workaround: To workaround this issue, upgrade the CDF library in your existing MATLAB installation to version 3.3.0 (attached). Use the following procedure:Type matlabroot and mexext at the MATLAB prompt.Download the attachment to a temporary folder and unzip the contents using UNZIP. The path to this temporary folder will be referred to as $tmp in the steps that follow.Change folders into matlabroot.Take one of the following series of steps depending upon your particular computer architecture. glnx86: Change folders into bin/glnx86 .Backup the libcdf library, i.e. mv libcdf.so.3.2.1 libcdf.so.3.2.1.oldmv libcdf.so.3 libcdf.so.3.oldmv libcdf.so libcdf.so.oldCopy the CDF library from the temporary folder into the current folder, i.e. cp $tmp/bin/glnx86/libcdf.so.3.3.0 `pwd`cp -d $tmp/bin/glnx86/libcdf.so.3 `pwdcp -d $tmp/bin/glnx86/libcdf.so `pwd`glnxa64: Change folders into bin/glnxa64 .Backup the libcdf library, i.e. mv libcdf.so.3.2.1 libcdf.so.3.2.1.oldmv libcdf.so.3 libcdf.so.3.oldmv libcdf.so libcdf.so.oldCopy the CDF library from the temporary folder into the current folder, i.e. cp $tmp/bin/glnxa64/libcdf.so.3.3.0 `pwd`cp -d $tmp/bin/glnxa64/libcdf.so.3 `pwdcp -d $tmp/bin/glnxa64/libcdf.so `pwd`maci: Change folders into bin/maci .Backup the libcdf library, i.e. mv libcdf.dylib.3.2.1 libcdf.3.2.1.dylib.oldmv libcdf.3.dylib libcdf.3.dylib.oldmv libcdf.dylib libcdf.dylib.oldCopy the CDF library from the temporary folder into the current folder, i.e. cp $tmp/bin/maci/libcdf.3.3.0.dylib `pwd`cp -d $tmp/bin/maci/libcdf.3.dylib `pwdcp -d $tmp/bin/maci/libcdf.dylib `pwd`maci64: Change folders into bin/maci64 .Backup the libcdf library, i.e. mv libcdf.dylib.3.2.1 libcdf.3.2.1.dylib.oldmv libcdf.3.dylib libcdf.3.dylib.oldmv libcdf.dylib libcdf.dylib.oldCopy the CDF library from the temporary folder into the current folder, i.e. cp $tmp/bin/maci64/libcdf.3.3.0.dylib `pwd`cp -d $tmp/bin/maci64/libcdf.3.dylib `pwdcp -d $tmp/bin/maci64/libcdf.dylib `pwd`win32: Change folders into bin\win32 .Backup the file libcdf.dll to libcdf.dll.oldCopy the file $tmp\bin\win32\libcdf.dll to the current folder.win64: Change folders into bin\win64 .Backup the file libcdf.dll to libcdf.dll.oldCopy the file $tmp\bin\win64\libcdf.dll to the current folder.Restart MATLAB.By default, this version of the CDF library performs additional file validation upon opening a CDF file. This can result in slower performance of cdfread, cdfwrite, and cdfinfo. To turn off file validation, set the CDF_VALIDATE environment variable from the MATLAB prompt, i.e. setenv('CDF_VALIDATE','no')

Summary: Exported Simulink fixed-point data types may cause import errors in an AUTOSAR authoring tool
Description: AUTOSAR uses the min/max value of integer types to deduce the word length needed for the datatype. However, for the AUTOSAR target, the software incorrectly exports the real-world min/max value rather than the stored integer min/max value. For example, the software exports, fxp5 = Simulink.NumericType DataTypeMode: 'Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling' Signed: true WordLength: 16 Slope: 2^-5 Bias: 0 IsAlias: true HeaderFile: 'Rte_Type.h' Description: '' as: <INTEGER-TYPE> <SHORT-NAME>fxp5</SHORT-NAME> <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS> <COMPU-METHOD-REF DEST="COMPU-METHOD">/mTestFxpWrdSz_pkg/mTestFxpWrdSz_dt/COMPU_fxp568ed7_27c9e278c34e8e38</COMPU-METHOD-REF> </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS> <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">-1024</LOWER-LIMIT> <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">1023.96875</UPPER-LIMIT></INTEGER-TYPE> This is incorrect. The Upper-Limit and Lower-Limit should be the upper limit and lower limit of the stored integer representation: <INTEGER-TYPE> <SHORT-NAME>fxp5</SHORT-NAME> <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS> <COMPU-METHOD-REF DEST="COMPU-METHOD">/mTestFxpWrdSz_pkg/mTestFxpWrdSz_dt/COMPU_fxp568ed7_27c9e278c34e8e38</COMPU-METHOD-REF> </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS> <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">-32768</LOWER-LIMIT> <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">32767</UPPER-LIMIT></INTEGER-TYPE> This bug may result in the following: Import errors in an AUTOSAR authoring tool RTE generator defines an incorrect word length
Workaround: Download the attached file and use this to overwrite the existing file. Ensure that you install the correct version in the following location:/matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@exporter/private/p_export_component.p

Summary: If you supply a Hessian function for the fmincon interior-point algorithm, and have nonlinear equality constraints, fmincon has slow convergence or fails to converge.
Description: You can supply a Hessian function for the fmincon  interior-point algorithm by setting the Hessian option to 'user-supplied' and the HessFcn option to the function handle of your Hessian function. The documentation states that you should write your Hessian function with the sum of Lagrange multipliers times constraint functions. However, for equality constraints, fmincon  erroneously expects equality Lagrange multipliers to be subtracted, not added.
Workaround: The documented syntax for a Hessian function is, with D2 representing the matrix of second derivatives:HessFcn = D2(objective) + sumi(lambda.eqnonlin(i)D2(equality constraint(i))) + sumj(lambda.ineqnonlin(j)D2(inequality constraint(j))).However, the Hessian function works correctly only when you subtract the nonlinear equality multipliers:HessFcn = D2(objective) - sumi(lambda.eqnonlin(i)D2(equality constraint(i))) + sumj(lambda.ineqnonlin(j)D2(inequality constraint(j))).

Summary: Timeseries object getsampleusingtime can fail to match an absolute time value from getabstime on the same timeseries
Description: For a time series defined with an absolute time vector (TimeInfo.StartDate  set to a datestr ), the getabstime  method returns a cell array of date strings representing the absolute times. If one of these date string times is converted to a datenum  and used to find the corresponding time series sample (using the getsampleusingtime  method), the result should not be empty since a sample with that time does exist. In some cases, however, rounding errors can cause getsampleusingtimean  to return an empty time series from datenum time values. For example, the following code returns empty: % Construct a time series using absolute time startDate = datenum('14-Jul-2009 13:10:20', 'dd-mmm-yyyy HH:MM:SS'); data = randn(3600*24,1); tsA = timeseries(data, 'Name', 'AbsTime'); tsA.TimeInfo.StartDate = '14-Jul-2009 13:10:20'; tsA.TimeInfo.Units = 'seconds'; % Try to get the 14th time using the result of getabstime tsA.getsampleusingtime(datenum(tsA(14).getabstime))
Workaround: For MATLAB 7.3 or later, replace the tsgetrelativetime static timeseries object method that is defined in:matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@timeseries/timeseries.m by the following code:function t = tsgetrelativetime(date,dateRef,unit) % This method calculates relative time value between date abd % dateref. vecRef = datevec(dateRef); vecDate = datevec(date); t = (datenum([vecDate(:,1:3) zeros(size(vecDate,1),3)])-datenum([vecRef(1:3) 0 0 0]))*... tsunitconv(unit,'days')+ ... (vecDate(:,4:6)*[3600 60 1]'-vecRef(:,4:6)*[3600 60 1]')*... tsunitconv(unit,'seconds');endBefore replacing the tsgetrelativetime method, back up timeseries.m, saving it as timeseries.m_old, in case you make any improper edits.

Summary: Closing MATLAB from the Dock while MATLAB is starting up will cause a segmentation violation on Macintosh.
Description: Closing MATLAB from the Dock while it is starting up will cause a segmentation violation in MATLAB.

Summary: A bug in MATLAB can result in an erroneous case sensitive warning message when setting the get or set method for a dynamic property. The functionality of MATLAB is not affected.
Description: When assigning the get or set methods for a dynamic property, the following warning message can be triggered if the name of the get or set method differs from another function's name only in case. Warning: Could not find an exact (case-sensitive) match for 'getStatus'.C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008b\toolbox\matlab\uitools\getstatus.m isa case-insensitive match and will be used instead.You can improve the performance of your code by using exactname matches and we therefore recommend that you update yourusage accordingly. Alternatively, you can disable this warning usingwarning('off','MATLAB:dispatcher:InexactCaseMatch').This warning will become an error in future releases.   testObj handle with no properties.   The class that generated the warning is defined as follows. The getStatus  method caused a case-sensitive conflict with and existing function in the uitools  directory: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008b\toolbox\matlab\uitools\getstatus.m  classdef testObj < dynamicprops  methods   function obj = testObj()    ph = obj.addprop('status');    ph.GetMethod = @getStatus;    ph.SetMethod = @setStatus;   end   function out=getStatus(obj)    out = obj.status;   end   function setStatus(obj,val)    obj.status = val;   end endend
Workaround: To avoid the warning message, use a name for the get/set method that is different in more than just case. For example, getStatusProp.

Summary: The PolySpace excel macro does not count SHF checks (related to a shift operation)
Description: If your verification results contain SHF checks, the checks are displayed in the "RTE checks sheet" tab, but they do not appear in the other tabs, including the statistics. For example, the "Check synthesis" tab displays a count of 0 for SHF checks, and the "orange check distribution," does not count the SHF checks.
Workaround: Note that following workaround is only available for release R2007aplus.To workaround this issue under Linux: 1. Create a backup copy of the following file:$POLYSPACE_C/lib/PolySpace_Macros.xls for a Linux or Solaris machine 2. Download the attached file attachment_566264_client_R2007aplus_linux_2009-07-20.zip. 3. Browse to the Polyspace installation folder (/usr/local/Polyspace by default under Linux) 4. Unzip the file in this installation folder and confirm the replacementsTo workaround this issue under Windows: 1. Create a backup copy of the following file:POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\lib\PolySpace_Macros.xls for a Windows machine 2. Download the attached file attachment_566264_client_R2007aplus_win_2009-07-20.zip. 3. Browse to the Polyspace installation folder (C:\Polyspace by default under Windows) 4. Unzip the file in this installation folder and confirm the replacements

Summary: Extra line breaks when importing a MATLAB program file into a Word document
Description: When using the Import File component to import a MATLAB program file as "Syntax highlighted MATLAB code" in a Microsoft Word document, extra line breaks may appear in the generated report.
Workaround: To avoid this issue:1. Use the attached script (removeCrLf.m) to remove carriage returns in the file to be imported. For example:removeCrLf('myFile.m', 'myModifiedFile.m');where myFile.m is the original file, and myModifiedFile.m is the name of the cleaned-up file.2. In the Import File component, set file name to be the name of the cleaned-up file.3. Run the report to import the file into a Word document.

Summary: Saving and loading very large container arrays in V7.3 MAT-files is slow
Description: Saving very large container arrays (cell arrays or structs) to V7.3 MAT-files may appear to hang MATLAB, although the process is making incremental progress. Loading the file may also be slow. In this case, "large" refers to an array that contains more than a million elements, approximately.
Workaround: Allow the save or load process to finish, although it is slow.

Summary: Why do I get the error cmd is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch filewhen trying to compile an application
Description: After every compilation using either MCC or MBUILD, the above mentioned error occurs. The compilation does seem to have finished successfully. This error occurs when the system path variable has  %SystemRoot% .
Workaround: Download the attached files.Backup mbuild.bat and mcc.bat in <matlabroot>/bin directoryCopy the downloaded files into <matlabroot>/bin directory

Summary: Compiler error when generating code for models that contain the C6000 UDP Receive block
Description: Generating code for models that contain the C6000 UDP Receive block may produce compilation errors with certain versions of the NDK. The following error message is reported while compiling the generated code: line 51: error: argument of type "struct sockaddr_in *" is incompatible with parameter of type "PSA" ? ?
Workaround: To install the patch:Download the attached files, patch_installer.m and patch_565889.zip.Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt:patch_installer('patch_565889.zip', 'install')Complete the installation by following the instructions on the MATLAB command line.The attached patch files modify the following files in your MATLAB installation:matlab\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\blks\mex\tlc_c\c6000_ip_config.tlcmatlab\toolbox\target\extensions\processor\tic6000\blks\mex\tlc_c\c6000_udp_recv.tlc?

Summary: The scope figure window of Discrete-Time Eye Diagram Scope Block, found in Communications blockset, sometimes disappears unexpectedly on 64-bit MAC platforms.
Description: The Discrete-Time Eye Diagram Scope block, found in Communications blockset, has the following issue when only one scope is present in your model:If you change a parameter of the scope block (such as color fading or Eye diagram to display) while a simulation is running, and then click the ?Apply? button on the block mask, the scope figure window disappears.
Workaround: Click 'OK' button on the block mask dialog to bring back the scope figure window.

Summary: Two MEX-files used by dicominfo and dicomread have memory leaks
Description: Repeated calls to dicomread and dicominfo may run out of memory.  The dicomparse and dicomlookup_helper private MEX-files have small memory leaks that can add up to large resource waste.
Workaround: Restarting MATLAB will free the leaked memory.

Summary: Binary min and max may give incorrect results when passed complex inputs.
Description: The following code illustrates the problem:    X = zeros(1e6,1);  % Vector with a mostly zero imaginary part   X(end) = eps * i;   out = max(X-0.1, -0.2);   out(1)             % Should be -0.2 instead of -0.1   out(end)           % Correct result -0.2
Workaround: Disable the JIT by typing the following at the MATLAB command line:   feature accel off

Summary: Stateflow transition callouts are truncated.
Description: Stateflow transition callouts are limited to 32 characters.
Workaround: Back up and replace getObjectName.p with the attached file, getObjectName.p.The path to getObjectName.p is %/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@propsrc_sf/getObjectName.p

Summary: Saving model to earlier release produces error message
Description: When you save a model to an earlier release, the following error message appears: Output argument "newRules" (and maybe others) not assigned during call to "C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009bd\toolbox\simulink\simulink\slsaveassup.p>PreProcessModelForEarlierRelease This error is related to how SaveAs deals with links to write-protected blocks in customer libraries.
Workaround: As a workaround, perform one of the following tasks.Download the appropriate attached p-code file to the folder [matlabroot '/toolbox/simulink/simulink'].Disable any links associated with the affected blocks. To determine which blocks are affected, select your model and run find_system(gcs,'RegExp','on','Permissions','NoReadOrWrite|ReadOnly','LinkStatus','resolved').

Summary: MATLAB Builder JA may fail at Java compilation (javac) phase when project contains paths longer than 90 characters.
Description: The Java code generated in this case is incorrect because line-wrapping is applied. For example: Compiler version: 4.__ (R_____)[...]Executing command:[...][parsing started C:\Documents and Settings\User Name \My Documents\Projects\Software\Workfolder MATLAB\Test_java\Test_java\src\Test_java\Test_javaMCRFactory.java]C:\Documents and Settings\User Name \My Documents\Projects\Software\Workfolder MATLAB\Test_java\Test_java\src\Test_java\Test_javaMCRFactory.java:58: unclosed string literal  "Documents and Settings/User Name /My Documents/Projects/Software/Workfolder  ?
Workaround: There are two workarounds for this bug:Move project-related files to a shorter path in the file system.Use MCC -K to specify "keep generated files on failure," and edit the generated Java file to correct the syntax error (by either unwrapping the line or closing the string literal and appending another string literal beginning on the next line with continuation character '+' ).?

Summary: Callback errors when switching to AUTOSAR target without Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder license
Description: If you do not have a Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder license and switch your system target file to autosar.tlc , the software generates callback errors: Warning: The following error occurred processing the System Target File '': Undefined function or method 'autosar_rtwoptions_callback' for input arguments of type 'cell'. > In stf2target at 61 In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b_noec\toolbox\rtw\rtw\@RTW\@TargetBrowser\uploadTarget.p>uploadTarget at 19 In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b_noec\toolbox\rtw\rtw\private\targetBrowserCloseCB.p>targetBrowserCloseCB at 9 In rtwprivate at 12Warning: The following error occurred processing the System Target File '': Undefined function or method 'autosar_rtwoptions_callback' for input arguments of type 'cell'. > In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b_noec\toolbox\rtw\rtw\@RTW\@TargetBrowser\uploadTarget.p>uploadTarget at 19 In C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2009b_noec\toolbox\rtw\rtw\private\targetBrowserCloseCB.p>targetBrowserCloseCB at 9 In rtwprivate at 12Warning: The following error occurred processing the System Target File '': Undefined function or method 'autosar_rtwoptions_callback' for input arguments of type 'cell'. > In stf2target at 61 In simulink\private\configDlgAction at 108 In slprivate at 12Warning: The following error occurred processing the System Target File '': Undefined function or method 'autosar_rtwoptions_callback' for input arguments of type 'cell'. > In simulink\private\configDlgAction at 108 In slprivate at 12
Workaround: Either obtain a Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder license, or avoid specifying autosar.tlc as your system target file.

Summary: Stateflow sometimes creates non-inlined functions for states even when Function Inline Option is Inline
Description: Under the following conditions, the code for a given state is not inlined into the parent state even when the Function Inline?Option  is set to Inline : The state has a single default and a single inner transition Both the default and inner transition reach the same junction The flow graph downstream of the common junction is sufficiently complex  The attached model bug_inline.mdl shows this problem. Even though the state "MAIN" in the Stateflow chart has the Function Inline  Option ?set to Inline , in the Real-Time Workshop generated code, there is a non-inlined function bug_inline_inner_defaults_MAIN .
Workaround: The above pattern is most commonly used when the user wishes to have a common entry and during action. In order to achieve the same behavior but avoid the problem, consider placing the common actions into a graphical function and setting the Function Inline Option?for that graphical function to Inline. This step prevents the emission of a non-inlined function in the generated code.See the attached model bug_inline_workaround.mdl to see the workaround for this bug.

Summary: Warnings while starting MATLAB, mcc or mex on Macintosh? OS X 10.6 Snow Leopard systems: "NSCalibratedRGBColor autoreleased with no pool in place"
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.6 Snow Leopard systems, typing matlab , mcc  or mex  from the command line report several lines of the following warning,  __NSAutoreleaseNoPool(): Object ... of class NSCalibratedRGBColor autoreleased with no pool in place - just leaking This issue does not impact the behavior or performance of MATLAB and can be ignored.

Summary: The function hsv2rgb may sometimes return negative RGB values.
Description: In a small number of cases, hsv2rgb  may sometimes return negative RGB values. An example of this is: hsv2rgb([(1+eps)/3,1,1])ans =-4.4409e-016 1 0

Summary: Entering an invalid Outport block port number might cause Simulink to stop running
Description: While interacting through an Outport block dialog, Simulink expects the 'Port number' parameter to be specified as a positive integer. If this parameter is specified as, for example, a character or a negative number, Simulink will reject the change and report an error. However, subsequent interaction with the block diagram, including simulation, may cause Simulink to stop running or to report an 'Unexpected exception of unknown type' error.
Workaround: Only use positive integers for the 'Port number' of Outport blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrete FIR Filter block can generate incorrect results in simulation and code generation
Description: The Discrete FIR Filter block incorrectly allows you to specify slope and bias data types on the block dialog. The block correctly prevents signals with non-zero bias and non-power-of two slope from coming in on the input or from being defined in the 'Fixed point' mode of the Data Type Assistant. However, when you select 'Expression' for the 'Mode' parameter in the Data Type Assistant, the block incorrectly allows you to define a data type with non-zero bias and/or non-power-of-two slope. Doing so gives incorrect results in both simulation and code generation.
Workaround: When you select 'Expression' for the 'Mode' parameter in the Data Type Assistant, do not define a data type with non-zero bias and/or non-power-of-two slope.

Summary: Weighted Sample Time Math: When parameter overflow occurs in this block for operation '1/Ts' and the 'Detect overflow' configuration parameter is set to 'Error', no error message appears
Description: For the Weighted Sample Time Math block, no error message appears when  parameter overflow occurs under these conditions: 'Operation' is set to '1/Ts' in the block dialog box.'Detect overflow' is set to 'Error' in the Data Validity pane of the Configurations Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: Set the 'Detect overflow' configuration parameter to 'Warning' and the warning message is issued.

Summary: Text in a dialog field may become difficult to read if its color is too light
Description: When a value is changed in a Simulink block dialog, the background color of the parameter field changes to antique white to identify that it is dirty. If a theme or color scheme is chosen where the foreground color of text is close to antique white, then the text in a parameter field may become difficult to read when it is changed.
Workaround: Change the theme or color scheme so that foreground text is dark.

Summary: Plot block does not correctly save what compartment a species is in.
Description: If a species with the same name exists in different compartments, the plot block does not save and load that information correctly.
Workaround: No workaround.

Summary: mmreader returns a readTimedOut error when reading Windows Media and MPEG-2 files on Windows systems
Description: On Windows systems, when you attempt to read the last frame in a Windows Media or MPEG-2 video file, mmreader  sometimes returns a readTimedOut  error.For example, the code  obj = mmreader('sample_MPEG2_file.mpeg');  img = obj.read(inf); sometimes returns the error??? Summary: Wrong result of an integral involving dirac
Description: The following commands f:= x*(x + dirac(x)): int(f, x = -1..1) produce a wrong result: 0
Workaround: Expand the integrand before passing it to int:int(expand(f), x = -1..1)2/3

Summary: filterbuilder scale SOS coefficients option does not have any effect on the generated MATLAB file.
Description: In filterbuilder checking the Scale SOS coefficients checkbox for IIR filters does not have any effect on the generated MATLAB file.

Summary: Using DataTypeOverride on a model reference hierarchy with global data stores may cause unpredictable results
Description: In a model reference hierarchy that uses global data stores, setting the DataTypeOverride  parameter inconsistently on the models may cause a simulation to get incorrect results or cause a segmentation violation. You can set the DataTypeOverride  parameter with the Fixed-Point Tool.
Workaround: Set the DataTypeOverride parameter consistently on all models in a model reference hierarchy.

Summary: When verifying Ada code, PolySpace stops during semantic compilation checking with the error message: "expected private type"
Description: When using private type, PolySpace Client/Server for Ada software incorrectly reports a compilation issue. PolySpace stops with following error message: -> Verifier found an error in mypackage.adb:9:28: expected private type "My_Type" defined at otherpackage.ads:5  The following example leads to this incorrect compilation issue: package otherpackage is   type My_Type (A : Integer; B : Integer; C : Integer) is limited private;   type My_access_Type is access all My_Type;   private protected type My_Type (A : Integer;B : Integer; C : Integer) is pragma Interrupt_Priority(System.Interrupt_Priority'LAST);   end My_Type; end otherpackage;
Workaround: The only workaround is to intrusively authorize access to private type in the code. You could achieve this workaround in the previous example as follow:package otherpackage is  protected type My_Type (A : Integer; B : Integer; C : Integer) is pragma Interrupt_Priority(System.Interrupt_Priority'LAST);  end My_Type;  type My_access_Type is access all My_Type; end otherpackage;
