Summary: Unnecessary 'MemberVisibilityType' warning displayed for clear classes command after running model with C++ encapsulation interface
Description: If you issue a clear classes  command after running a model configured with a C++ encapsulation interface, the software might display an unnecessary warning similar to the following: Warning: an error occurred while parsing class Simulink.ERTCPPComponent: The type named 'MemberVisibilityType' already exists.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name: matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@ERTCPPComponent/schema.pDownload the attached file and place it at the same location as the file you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: An mwException generated in a C++ shared library causes a segmentation violation.
Description: If an mwException  generated from MATLAB code, built as a C++ shared library, does not have a stack trace then an application using it causes the application to crash which might invoke the JIT debugger if one is set. The following code compiled as a C++ shared library and called from a driver application reproduces the crash: function foo  a = zeros(10,10);  a[30,1];
Workaround: Wrap the MATLAB code throwing the exception in a try-catch block and rethrow the exception. For example:trya = rand(10,10);a[30,1]; %throw an index out of bounds exception catch ME rethrow(ME); end

Summary: Embedded structures might not be properly aligned when used with CALLLIB.
Description: Given the following C declarations, you cannot use the variable a2  in s_B  with default structure packing. typedef struct {  int one;   int two;  int three;} s_A;  typedef struct {  s_A a1;   s_A a2;} s_B;  The problem occurs when a structure has lower alignment requirements than the size of the structure dictates. In this example the structure s_A  only requres int  alignment in s_B , but MATLAB attempts to give it 8-byte alignment by adding padding between a1  and a2 . This can cause incorrect results or possibly crashes when using embedded structures.
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:Change the packing used for the given structure by adding #pragma pack (1) to your header fileCreating a loader MATLAB file and modify the packing of the affected strutures.The new packing value must be less than or equal to the size of the largest member of the embedded structures.

Summary: When the Multiport Switch block has only one data input that is a scalar, the control input is ignored if it is also a scalar
Description: When the Multiport Switch block's only data input and its control input are both scalars, the block ignores the control input.  The block outputs the input value in all cases, silently ignoring any out-of-range control inputs.
Workaround: Turning off Block reduction in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box will detect and report an out-of-range control input.

Summary: Building large subsystems can fail with message "Input bounds are out of range" or "Cannot connect inport signals to the temporary subsystem"
Description: Building a subsystem might fail if the subsystem: Has a large number of inports or outports. Drives a large number of GoTo blocks. Is driven by a large number of From blocks. Is driven by a Bus Creator block with a large number of inputs.  In the bullets above, "large" means on the order of 1000 or more. The build failure generates various messages, which might include "Input bounds are out of range ", "Cannot connect inport signals to the temporary subsystem ", or "Unknown error ".
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2007a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/private/slbus.pmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/private/ss2mdl.pDownload the attached files slbus_7a.p and ss2mdl_7a.p, rename them to slbus.p and ss2mdl.p, and place them in the same directory as the files you renamed.Also download the attached file sanitizePosition.p and place it in the same directory as the other two.Restart MATLAB.Please contact The MathWorks if you need a patch for a release other than R2007a.

Summary: Function translate fails for a strel that is a single row
Description: The function translate  issues an error message if you try to translate a structuring element, or strel, containing only a single row. For example, this code improperly issues an error message: translate(strel([1 1 1]), [1 1])
Workaround: Copy the file /toolbox/images/images/@strel/translate.m to a backup location. Open translate.m in the Editor and find the following line:idx = find(nhood);Add a new line to the file so that it looks like this:idx = find(nhood);idx = idx(:);Save the file and call:rehash toolbox

Summary: Validation fails for AUTOSAR server model with port names containing non-alphabetic characters
Description: If an AUTOSAR server operation model has a port name that contains a non-alphabetic character, for example, feedback.correction , then model validation fails with the following message: Error:Could not extract operation name from op(IN uint8 mode1, OUT uint8 feedback.correction).
Workaround: Remove all non-alphabetic characters from root-level I/O port names of your Simulink model.

Summary: intersect, setdiff, setxor, and union may return incorrect results when comparing a cell array of strings and a character string with trailing spaces.
Description: When comparing between a cell array of strings and a character string, if both inputs contain an identical string with trailing spaces, intersect , setdiff , setxor , and union  incorrectly interpret them as different strings. For example, the following command returns an empty cell array as output, even when both inputs contain an identical string: intersect({'a ', 'b '}, 'a ')
Workaround: To work around this issue, put all input strings into cell arrays before passing into intersect, setdiff, setxor, or union. For example,intersect({'a ', 'b '}, {'a '})

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow matrix or vector data passed to custom code functions can result in unexpected results
Description: Stateflow supports the use of custom code or C library functions in the action language when the user specifies custom code includes in the Simulation Target > Custom Code  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. When a Stateflow matrix or vector data is passed to a custom code function, the user might expect C semantics where the matrix is passed by reference and can be modified within the custom code function. However, sometimes, Stateflow passes a copy of the matrix data to the custom code function, thereby ensuring "call-by-value" semantics. This behavior might result in unexpected results.
Workaround: Always use the syntax custom_code_fcn(&x[0]) to pass a reference to a matrix variable to a custom code function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using sizeof operator in Stateflow action language can result in incorrect or unexpected answers
Description: Stateflow supports the use of custom code or C library functions in the action language when the user specifies custom code includes in the Simulation Target > Custom Code  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. This feature enables users to use the C sizeof  operator in the Stateflow action language. However, when a Stateflow vector or matrix data is passed into the sizeof  operator, it can result in incorrect or unexpected answers. For example, suppose that a user defines a Stateflow local data "x" of type int32  and size "1000". The expected value of sizeof(x)  in this case is 4000. However, this expression results in a value of 4 on 32-bit operating systems and 8 on 64-bit operating systems.
Workaround: Do not pass vector or matrix data directly into the sizeof operator. Instead, use something like 1000*sizeof(x[0]). To avoid hard-coding the size of the local data, you can parameterize the size of the vector or matrix data using a chart parameter and use an expression such as N*sizeof(x[0]).

Summary: Missing labels when boxplot draws into a subplot
Description: When the boxplot  command plots into a subplot that is short, the box labels may be clipped off. For example, the following commands may produce a plot with missing labels: subplot(4,1,1)boxplot(randn(10,2))
Workaround: In many cases the default plot tick labels will be satisfactory box labels. You can turn these labels on with the following command:set(gca,'XTickMode','auto','XTickLabelMode','auto')To insure that labels appear only at box locations, specify the tick mark locations:set(gca,'XTick',1:2)If the clipped labels are text rather than column numbers, specify the tick labels as well:set(gca,'XTick',1:2,'XTickLabel',{'one' 'two'})

Summary: Warnings and incorrect PEV values from models exported to Simulink
Description: When you export Simulink models from the Model-Based Calibration Toolbox, the connections for any PEV blocks are broken. When you run the Simulink models, the broken connections cause warnings and incorrect values for PEV. The model evaluations in the Simulink models are correct.
Workaround: To avoid this problem: 1. Copy the attached file to:/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@xregmodel2. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: In R2009b, the redesigned Filter Design HDL Coder dialog is not scrollable and is difficult to view on smaller screens.
Description: In R2009b, the redesigned Filter Design HDL Coder dialog is not scrollable. The large size of the dialog makes it difficult to view the entire dialog or move the dialog around on a screen with less resolution.
Workaround: To work around this problem:Download the attached file, attachment_561986_20100720.zip.Unzip it to produce the file getDialogSchema.m file.Replace the following file in your MATLAB installation with the new getDialogSchema.m:MATLABROOT\toolbox\shared\hdlshared\@fdhdlcoderui\@fdhdltooldlg\getDialogSchema.m

Summary: Analog output does not generate data when you use NI USB-6251, 6255, and 6259
Description: Analog output will not generate any data if you are using an NI USB-6251, USB-6255, or USB-6259 device under these conditions: You are using hardware digital triggers The HwDigitalTriggerSource property is set to PFI0 You have connected digital port 0 pin 0 to PFI0 TriggerCondition is left at the default of NegativeEdge  Set up the analogoutput object and queue data: ao = analogoutput('nidaq', 'Dev1');  addchannel(ao,0);  set(ao, 'TriggerType', 'HwDigital');  set(ao, 'HwDigitalTriggerSource', 'PFI0');putdata(ao,zeros(1000,1));  Create the digitalio object and add one line: port 0 line 0. dio = digitalio('nidaq', 'Dev1'); addline(dio, 0, 0, 'out', 'TrigLine');  Initialize the digital line to a high value. putvalue(dio.Line(1), 1);  Start the analogoutput object and send the trigger value to the digitalio object: start(ao); putvalue(dio.Line(1), 0);  Examine the analogoutput object and notice that no samples have been output because it is waiting for the trigger signal: aoDisplay Summary of Analog Output (AO) Object Using 'USB-6255'.       Output Parameters:  1000 samples per second on each channel.      Trigger Parameters:  1 'HwDigital' trigger.           Engine status:  Waiting for TRIGGER.                           0 total sec. of data currently queued per trigger.                           1000  samples currently queued by PUTDATA.                           0 samples sent to output device since START.AO object contains channel(s):   Index:  ChannelName:  HwChannel:  OutputRange:  UnitsRange:  Units:      1       ''            0           [-10 10]      [-10 10]     'Volts'   ? ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, pause one tenth of a second immediately after you call start and before you send the trigger value to the digital I/O object: start(ao)pause(0.01)putvalue(dio.Line(1), 0);

Summary: If an S-Function Builder block with no inputs propogates a bus to its outputs ports, then the S-Function Builder errors out during compilation
Description: If an S-Function Builder block has no inputs and attempts to propogate a bus to its output ports, then during compilation, the S-Function Builder returns the following error: error C2143: syntax error : missing ')' before ',' error C2198: 'test_Outputs_wrapper' : too few arguments for call error C2059: syntax error : ')'

Summary: Values copied from a MATLAB uitable cell and pasted into Excel 2007 are rounded
Description: When I create a uitable  object using the following command: uitable('Data', 1234.56, 'ColumnFormat', {'bank'}); and copy the cell contents by pressing Ctrl +C, the value in the clipboard is rounded to 1234.6. If I paste this value in an Excel 2007 worksheet cell by pressing Ctrl +V, the value that is pasted is 1234.6.
Workaround: No workaround is available

Summary: Calling bndspread with vector bond inputs causes an error
Description: Calling bndspread  with vector variables for the bond inputs causes an error. For example: SpotCurve = [datenum('13-Jul-2009') .03;datenum('13-Jul-2010') .04;datenum('13-Jul-2011') .05];   Price = 98;   Coupon = .04;   Settle = '13-Jul-2009';   Maturity = {'15-Feb-2010';'15-Sep-2010'};   Spread = bndspread(SpotCurve, Price,Coupon, Settle,Maturity)   ??? Error using ==> fzero at 334Function value at starting guess must be finite and real.   Error in ==> bndspread at 266[X(bondidx),Fval,EXITFLAG(bondidx)]= fzero(objfun, x0(bondidx), options1);   ?
Workaround: To workaround this problem, use a loop around the calls to bndspread. For example:SpotCurve = [datenum('13-Jul-2009') .03;datenum('13-Jul-2010') .04;datenum('13-Jul-2011') .05];Price = 98;Coupon = .04;Settle = '13-Jul-2009';Maturity = {'15-Feb-2010';'15-Sep-2010'};for bondidx=1:2Spread(bondidx) = bndspread(SpotCurve, Price,Coupon,Settle,Maturity(bondidx));endType Spread.Spread =391.9334 161.4349

Summary: Compiling and running the NaN checking function "rt_nonfinite.c" with full compiler optimization is turned on may produce the wrong results
Description: The NaN checking function in "rt_nonfinite.c" produced by Real-Time Workshop works when compiler optimization is turned off. However, with full compiler optimization (Code Composer studio, target TMS320C6747), the compiler optimizes out the NaN comparison: "if (value != value)...". The compiler replaces "value != value" with "false/0". TI's website reports this issue in the C6000 Tools bug list. It states that the optimizer for c6x assumes any floating point value, including NaN, is equal to itself. For more information, see http://www.ti.com/sc/docs/dsps/hotline/techbits/c6_ccsbug.htm#SDSsq33513 .
Workaround: Set the "optimization" for rt_nonfinite.c to none:Expand the generated project in Code Composer Studio.Right-click rt_nonfinite.c.Choose File Specific Options...In the Basic category, set Opt Level to None.

Summary: Simulating MPC block with input signals representing real-time constraints crashes MATLAB?
Description: MATLAB? crashes when you simulate a model containing an MPC block with input signals that represent real-time constraints on one or more of the inports for the lower and upper bounds on the maniupulated variables. These inports are umin  and umax , respectively. Any binary code you generate using Real Time Workshop? also crashes during execution.
Workaround: Start MATLAB. Set the current folder to toolbox\mpc\mpcutils\src\ relative to the installed MATLAB? path.Open mpc_common.c in the MATLAB editor. Ensure that you have the writing permission for toolbox\mpc\mpcutils, including subfolders and files.In the c-file, find the following code segment (for example, by searching for the string of "bounds on inputs from Simulink diagram"):if (!no_umax) /* Get upper bounds on inputs from Simulink diagram */for (i=0; i<degrees; i++)  for (j=0; j<nu; j++) { rhsc[h] = rhsc[h] + umax[j]; h++;}if (!no_umin) /* Get lower bounds on inputs from Simulink diagram */for (i=0; i<degrees; i++) for (j=0; j<nu; j++) { rhsc[h] = rhsc[h] - umin[j]; h++;}In this code, replace degrees with degrees/nu in both places.In command window, type make and execute it. This command re-generates the binary code used by the Model Predictive Control Toolbox?.

Summary: Interactions with Simulink? models slow when certain block dialogs are open
Description: Interactions with Simulink? models, such as simulation, code generation, and linearization, may be slow when block dialogs are open that contain a large number of data type parameters. Examples of affected blocks are PID Controller, PID Controller (2DOF), Lookup Table (n-D), and Discrete Filter. You add data type parameters by selecting DataTypeStr  for Type  in the Mask Editor for Subsystem blocks.
Workaround: Close the block dialogs before interacting with the Simulink? model.

Summary: PolySpace follow progress incorrectly reports 'Verification process failed' when an I/O error occurs in connection
Description: When using 'PolySpace follow progress' from the Queue Manager (Spooler), the Queue Manager and attached PolySpace Server (CPU) can briefly lose communication while displaying the log file of a currently running verification. This causes an 'I/O error occured in connection' message to appear in the log file. 'PolySpace follow progress' then displays a pop-up 'Verification process failed' error even if the verification continues on the server.

Summary: Computation time can be incorrect for the "Follow Progress" feature of the spooler
Description: When you use the Follow Progress feature, the interface can compute the verification time incorrectly. This problem occurs most frequently when the time zone on the Client OS and on the Server OS are not the same.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point reciprocal operation for non-trivial slope returns different results from simulation
Description: For a Math Function block that performs a fixed-point reciprocal operation, if the input or output data types have slopes that are not zero or power of 2, Real-Time Workshop generated code might produce results that are slightly different from simulation results.

Summary: Macintosh? OS X 10.5 or 10.6: Java NullPointerException on MATLAB startup
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.5 with Java Update 4 installed, or on OS X 10.6, the following exception might appear (perhaps intermittently) when starting MATLAB: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException at apple.awt.CGraphicsEnvironment.resetDisplays(CGraphicsEnvironment.java:99)  at apple.awt.CGraphicsEnvironment.displayChanged(CGraphicsEnvironment.java:49)  at apple.awt.CToolkit$4.run(CToolkit.java:1310) at java.awt.event.InvocationEvent.dispatch(InvocationEvent.java:209) at java.awt.EventQueue.dispatchEvent(EventQueue.java:461)  at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpOneEventForHierarchy(EventDispatchThread.java:269) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEventsForHierarchy(EventDispatchThread.java:190) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(EventDispatchThread.java:184) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(EventDispatchThread.java:176) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.run(EventDispatchThread.java:110) The issue does not impact results or numerical accuracy, but it may become an annoyance in some instances. See also Bug Report 410438 .
Workaround: Install Java for Mac OS X 10.5 Update 6 or Java for Mac OS X 10.6 Update 1.

Summary: Non ANSI C compliant code might be generated to externally declare structure
Description: Building a model might generate external declarations of structures that meet C++ requirements but not ANSI C requirements. The result might generate warnings from some compilers, such as Microsoft Visual C/C++. For example, if you select the the ert.tlc  target and the C language for your model and then generate code, the generated model .h  file might include code similar to the following: /* Real-time Model Data Structure */ struct RT_MODEL_modelname { const char_T * volatile errorStatus; }; .../* Real-time Model object */ extern RT_MODEL_modelname *name_M; To be ANSI C compliant, the external declaration should include the struct  keyword: extern struct RT_MODEL_modelname *name_M;
Workaround: To work around this bug, either manually edit the generated code to contain the struct keyword or use appropriate compiler settings to permit the C++ style declaration.

Summary: When verifying Ada code, PolySpace verification stops during the CDFA phase with the error message: "unexpected node #<number>"
Description: Due to an internal problem, verifying code containing a specific Ada construct can lead to the following error message: - analyzing MYPACKAGE.READTYPE.FUNC : #<entry> - analyzing MYPACKAGE.   READTYPE : #<task_type FUNC,MSG1,MSG2> ana : INTERNAL ERROR unexpected node #2380: {comes_from_source = true, sloc = "mypackage.adb" line 111, column 22, parent = 2378,   variant = Attribute_Reference{prefix = 2377, attribute_name = "last", expressions = [], entity = 0,   expr_info = {etype = 30,   flags = {assignment_ok=false,do_range_check=false,   raises_constraint_error=false,is_static_expression=false}}}} exception Match(Match) raised. FATAL ERROR for mypackage.atz   This problem is caused by the use of a attribute name 'last ' on 'Duration ' type, but the cause of the error is really code-specific.For this reason, there is no workaround. If you are experiencing this error, send your code to customer support. We will investigate the problem and provide a workaround, if possible. ?

Summary: Function input('Prompt:','s') in a compiled application fails to return an empty string data type when the user's input is only <ENTER>.
Description: When the optional second parameter to the input  function is given as 's'  , the function is specified to return a string data type. However, in a compiled application, in the case where the user enters only <ENTER >, the function does not properly convert the empty return value to a string type. As a result, passing the returned value to other MATLAB functions which expect string data as input, e.g. strtrim , may in the case of empty input generate an error such as: ??? Error using ==> strtrim  Input should be a string or a cell array of strings.   The incorrect behavior is dependent on both compiled code and empty user input. When used in MATLAB, input  with the 's' option does correctly convert all user input, including empty input, to a string type. When used in compiled code, input  with the 's' option does correctly convert all non-empty user input to a string type.

Summary: MATAB can enter an infinite loop when setting or getting the value of a dynamic property.
Description: If the property set or get method of an dynamic property is a class method, setting or getting the property value can cause MATLAB to enter an infinite loop. Suppose you have a class called testObj : % File: testObj.m classdef testObj < dynamicprops methods  function obj = testObj()   ph = obj.addprop('myProp');   ph.GetMethod = @getMyProp;   ph.SetMethod = @setMyProp;  end  function out=getMyProp(obj)   out = obj.myProp;  end  function setMyProp(obj,val)   obj.myProp = val;  end endend  The following step can cause this bug: >> a = testObj;   >> a.myProp = 101;   a.myProp = 101??? Maximum recursion limit of 500 reached. Use set(0,'RecursionLimit',N)to change the limit. Be aware that exceeding your available stack space cancrash MATLAB and/or your computer.   Error in ==> testObj>testObj.setMyProp   ? ?
Workaround: Use local functions for the property set and get methods. For example, to make the previous sample code work correctly, change it to:% File: testObj.mclassdef testObj < dynamicprops methods function obj = testObj() ph = obj.addprop('myProp'); ph.GetMethod = @getMyProp; ph.SetMethod = @setMyProp; end endendfunction out=getMyProp(obj) out = obj.myProp;endfunction setMyProp(obj,val) obj.myProp = val;end??

Summary: Empty values for CommentStyle in calls to textscan cause MATLAB to hang.
Description: For example, given a file named myfile.txt , running the following code hangs MATLAB: f = fopen('myfile.txt','r');textscan(f,'%s\n','CommentStyle','') ;
Workaround: Remove the CommentStyle parameter and empty value from the call to textscan.

Summary: Memory leak when destroying libpointer objects of type stringPtrPtr.
Description: Destroying libpointer  objects of type stringPtrPtr  in the MATLAB shared library interface can cause memory leaks. For example, executing the following code leaks 4096 bytes (8192 on 64-bit machines) of memory. c=cell(1024,1)for n=1:length(c),c{n}='';endp=libpointer('stringPtrPtr',c); % this will leak 1024*4 bytes when cleared
Workaround: Minimize the number of stringPtrPtr objects created by reusing previously created objects if possible.

Summary: mcc doesn't pick up static methods which have an external implementation and are named like a built-in
Description: mcc  has difficulty compiling a class that has a static method when the method is implemented in a MATLAB language file outside the class definition file.  For example, compiling a class with a loadobj  method in file loadobj.m  will not include the loadobj  MATLAB file  in the compiled application. The mcc  command line flag -C  can be used to verify the presence or absence of a particular MATLAB language file.
Workaround: Move the implementation of the static method into the class definition file.

Summary: mdscale using strain criterion with weights gives wrong results or generates an error.
Description: Using the mdscale  function with the strain criterion and weights, for example, D = pdist(rand(5,3));W = rand(1,10);mdscale(D,2,'Criterion','strain','Weights',W); generates incorrect results. Using the mdscale function with the strain criterion, weights, and more than one random starting point, for example, D = pdist(rand(5,3));W = rand(1,10);mdscale(D,2,'Start','random','Criterion','strain','Weights',W,'Replicates',2); generates the following error: ??? Index exceeds matrix dimensions.Error in ==> mdscale at 359 sigsq = mean(dissimilarities(weights>0).^2)./(2.*p);
Workaround: Copy and rename one of the files in the attached zip file to MATLABROOT/toolbox/stats/mdscale.m, where MATLABROOT is the directory in which MATLAB(R) is installed. For R2009b, use the file mdscale_2009b.m. For R2006a through R2009a, use the file mdscale_R2006a_to_R2009a.m.

Summary: Depending on the content of model, a warning about a shadowed model file name might appear when you build a model
Description: Depending on the complexity of the model, you might receive a warning about a model file name shadowed by another file name. This warning might occur if the shadowing file (same name as current file) is higher up the file hierarchy than the current file when you build the target application.
Workaround: As a workaround:Close MATLAB.Download the attached files xpc_watc.tmf, xpcert_vc.tmf, xpc_vc.tmf, and xpcert_watc.tmf and save them to: matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpcFrom your build folder, delete _xpc_rtw.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during 'Software Safety Integration Analysis Level 0' with the message: '@FFT: bad array type for 65536(0)'
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on a C code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: **** Software Safety Integration Analysis Level 0 - 43 (P_SP) ... **** Software Safety Integration Analysis Level 0 - 44 (P_SULV) ---------------------------------------------------------------------- @FFT: bad array type for 65536(0) |An internal consistency problem has been detected. |In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information, |the computation has been halted. |Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support site |https://www.mathworks.com/support. |Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report. ----------------------------------------------------------------------  This problem can occur on a cast from a size 1 array to an unknow size array: typedef unsigned short unknown_array[]; typedef unsigned short known_array[1];... unknown_array *p1; known_array p2; p1 = (unknown_array *)p2;

Summary: The dec2bin function errors when the input is a matrix.
Description: When the input to the dec2bin function is matrix, the function errors as follows: dec2bin ([2 3;4 5]) ??? Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values. Error in ==> dec2bin at 31if any(d < 0) || any(~isfinite(d))  ?
Workaround: Convert the input to a vector before passing to the function.

Summary: The quadprog 'medium-scale' algorithm can incorrectly return a positive exitflag when the result is infeasible.
Description: The quadprog 'medium-scale'  algorithm can produce infeasible iterates, and not restore feasibility for the result. In these cases, the exitflag can be positive, incorrectly indicating a feasible solution.
Workaround: Check the feasibility of the result by evaluating your constraints.

Summary: Set distributed pipeline insertion (DistributedPipelining) 'off' in models that have states
Description: Note that the DistributedPipelining property applies only to Embedded MATLAB Function blocks or Stateflow charts. When a Stateflow chart contains any state or local variable, or an Embedded MATLAB block defines states through persistent variables, and DistributedPipelining is turned 'on', the coder should generate pipeline registers at the output(s), as defined by the 'OutputPipeline" property. In the above case, however, an internal bug can either prevent code generation or cause generation of incorrect code.
Workaround: In cases where your model has states, set DistributedPipelining 'off'.

Summary: Using haver/nextinfo on a cell-array generated haver object causes MATLAB? to crash
Description: If you instantiate a Haver Analytics database connection object by inputting the database name in a cell array, the haver  constructor returns what appears to be a valid connection object. If you then call nextinfo  on this object MATLAB? will seg fault.

Summary: New Task and New Project menu items in Control and Estimation Tools Manager when the Workspace node is selected causes errors
Description: When you select File > New Task  or New Project  in the Control and Estimation Tools Manager when the Workspace node is selected without any of these products installed: Simulink? Control Design? Simulink? Design Optimization? Model Predictive Control Toolbox?  You receive this error: %%%BEGIN ERROR%%% ??? Error using ==> javaArray All dimension arguments must be greater than zero
Workaround: To create a new SISO Design Task, you can either:Type sisotool and the MATLAB command prompt.Launch from the MATLAB Start menu by selecting Toolboxes > Control System > SISO Design Task.

Summary: The ga solver sometimes returns a positive exitflag (indicating a feasible solution) with an infeasible solution.
Description: ga  is a feasible step solver with respect to linear and bound constraints. This means the entire population remains feasible if all the individuals in the initial population are feasible. However, the built-in ga  creation function has a bug that sometimes causes it to generate some infeasible initial individuals when there are linear and bound constraints. This can lead to an infeasible solution. And, since ga  is a feasible step solver, it does not check feasibility when returning the final solution.
Workaround: Copy the attached customGAcreationFcn.m file to a directory on your MATLAB path. Use it as your creation function:options = gaoptimset('CreationFcn',@customGAcreationFcn);

Summary: If you pass ktrlink a KNITRO options text file, ktrlink allocates a full Hessian matrix. This can use excessive memory.
Description: When you give ktrlink  options in a KNITRO text-file, ktrlink  allocates an n-by-n full matrix (where n is the number of variables in the optimization problem). This includes cases where the KNITRO hessopt  option is set to exact , product , bfgs , lbfgs , and sr1 . However, when HessPattern  is defined in the options  structure, ktrlink  uses HessPattern  instead of an n-by-n full matrix.
Workaround: To avoid excessive memory allocation, set a sparse HessPattern in the options structure. For example, suppose you are using a Hessian approximation such as BFGS, LBFGS, or SR1, or suppose you have a function that computes a Hessian-vector product (HessMult in the options structure). Set HessPattern to an n-by-n all zero sparse matrix:options = optimset('HessPattern',sparse(numberOfVariables,numberOfVariables));ktrlink(...,options,'ktrOptions.txt');If you provide an exact Hessian to ktrlink (HessFcn in the options structure), set HessPattern to match the sparsity pattern of the exact Hessian. If you don't know the exact sparsity pattern, do not pass HessPattern.

Summary: stepcpncfamounts generates cash flow amounts with two principal payments for the same time period
Description: When using stepcpncfamounts  to generate cash flows for stepped coupons, instead of one principal payment, there are two payments. For example: Settle=datenum('04/30/2009');Maturity = datenum('04-May-2014');ConvDates = Settle:90:Maturity-30;CouponRates = linspace(.04,.06,22);[CFlows, CDates, CTimes] = stepcpncfamounts('04/30/2009','04-Jul-2014',ConvDates, CouponRates,4);cfplot(CDates,CFlows)title('Cash Flows')
Workaround: Attached is the fix for the problem -- note that this fix will only work in versions including and after 2009B.To workaround this issue, replace the version of bndspread.m function in your current installation with the updated version attached.1. Exit MATLAB?.Make a backup copy of the following file.matlabroot\toolbox\finfixed\finfixed\stepcpncfamounts.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, stepcnpcfamounts.m.OLD.2. Download the attached file and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.3. Restart MATLAB.4. Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Referenced models configured for single-instance mode ignore memory sections
Description: If you generate code for a reference model for which the Total number of instances allowed per top model  parameter on the Model Referencing pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog is set to one  (single ),  the code generator ignores memory sections even though the memory sections are used to generate code for the top-level model.

Summary: toquarterly fails on certain combinations of start- and end-dates in a FTS object
Description: toquarterly  fails on certain date ranges. If you use toquarterly  to set up a date range with just start and end dates, and the start or end date is either a non-business day or does not fall on a valid end-of-quarter date, toquarterly  fails. The following example demonstrates this problem. >> mydates = [datenum(2000,12,31) ; datenum(2009,6,2)]; >> myfints = fints(mydates,[1;2]); >> newfints = toquarterly(myfints) ??? Error using ==> fints.toquarterly at 186 Index exceeds matrix dimensions.
Workaround: If you are setting up a date range with toannual, tomonthly, toquarterly, tosemi, and toweekly by providing just a start and end date, use valid start and end dates at the desired periodicity. For example, use lbusdate to obtain valid business dates for your date range:myfints = fints([lbusdate(2000,12); lbusdate(2009,6)],[1; 2]); myfints = toquarterly(myfints)myfints =  desc: TOQUARTERLY: myfints freq: Quarterly (4)'dates: (35)' 'series1: (35)' '29-Dec-2000' [ 1] '30-Mar-2001' [ NaN] '29-Jun-2001' [ NaN] '28-Sep-2001' [ NaN] '31-Dec-2001' [ NaN] '28-Mar-2002' [ NaN] '28-Jun-2002' [ NaN] '30-Sep-2002' [ NaN] '31-Dec-2002' [ NaN] '31-Mar-2003' [ NaN] '30-Jun-2003' [ NaN] '30-Sep-2003' [ NaN] '31-Dec-2003' [ NaN] '31-Mar-2004' [ NaN] '30-Jun-2004' [ NaN] '30-Sep-2004' [ NaN] '31-Dec-2004' [ NaN] '31-Mar-2005' [ NaN] '30-Jun-2005' [ NaN] '30-Sep-2005' [ NaN] '30-Dec-2005' [ NaN] '31-Mar-2006' [ NaN] '30-Jun-2006' [ NaN] '29-Sep-2006' [ NaN] '29-Dec-2006' [ NaN] '30-Mar-2007' [ NaN] '29-Jun-2007' [ NaN] '28-Sep-2007' [ NaN] '31-Dec-2007' [ NaN] '31-Mar-2008' [ NaN] '30-Jun-2008' [ NaN] '30-Sep-2008' [ NaN] '31-Dec-2008' [ NaN] '31-Mar-2009' [ NaN] '30-Jun-2009' [ 2]

Summary: tcdf isn't monotonically increasing for some extreme values
Description: The cumulative distribution function is always monotonically non-decreasing, regardless of the probability distribution. However, for Student's t distribution, the tcdf  function may return a result of zero for some extreme inputs: >> tcdf(-9, 500) ans =      0 but a small non-zero value for more extreme inputs: >> tcdf(-30, 500) ans =     3.607144444214177e-114

Summary: When adding your own HTML files to the Help browser Contents pane, an XML-file validation error can appear if you specify an icon using <help_contents_icon>.
Description: When you provide your own HTML files for the Help browser, if you specify the type of icon that appears in the Help browser Contents  pane by using the <help_contents_icon>  tag in the info.xm l file, the icon does not display and MATLAB generates an XML-file validation error.
Workaround: To avoid the XML-file validation error, do not include the help_contents_icon tag in the info.xml file. Then, the Help Browser Contents pane shows the default icon for the <type> you specified.

Summary: uistack layers uipanels incorrectly in MATLAB R2009a and R2009b
Description: The uistack  function does not stack uipanels correctly in MATLAB 7.8 (R2009a) and 7.9 (R2009b). The following code illustrates the issue: figure;axes('units','pixels','position',[50 50 200 200]);u=uipanel('units','pixels','position',[100 100 200 200]);uistack(u,'bottom') Notice that uipanel does not stack beneath the axes, as the call to uistack  should have caused it to do. ?

Summary: Custom library cannot use Event-Based Random Number block or Event-Based Sequence block
Description: If you create a library containing any of the following blocks and copy the block from your library to a model, then the block issues an error message upon model compilation: Event-Based Random Number Event-Based Sequence A subsystem that contains the Event-Based Random Number or Event-Based Sequence block
Workaround: Rename the following files by appending .old to the file name: $MATLAB\toolbox\des\desmasks\des_rngsfunmask.m$MATLAB\toolbox\des\desmasks\des_seqgen_sfunmask.m$MATLAB is the MATLAB root folder on your machine, as returned by entering this command in the MATLAB Command Window: matlabrootDownload the attached file, ebsource_maskfix.zip, to the $MATLAB\toolbox\des\desmasks folder.In the MATLAB Command Window, enter: cd(fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox','des','desmasks'));unzip('ebsource_maskfix.zip');rehash toolboxYou can now create libraries or use your library blocks as usual.

Summary: Using stepcpnyield and stepcpnprice in succession fails to recover the original bond price
Description: When computing the bond price for stepped coupons using stepcpnprice , the inputs to stepcpnprice  are found using stepcpnyield  from the bond price. The bond price computed by stepcpnprice  is different than the starting bond price. The following example demonstrates the problem when calling stepcpnyield  and stepcpnprice  in succession: Settle = datenum('02-Aug-1992'); ConvDates = [   datenum('15-Jun-1997'), datenum('15-Jun-2001'),...   datenum('15-Jun-2005')]; Maturity = datenum('15-Jun-2010');  CouponRates = [0.075 0.08875 0.0925 0.1]; Basis = 1; Period = 12; EndMonthRule = 1; Face = 100;  Price2=50 Yield2 = stepcpnyield(Price2, Settle, Maturity, ConvDates, ... CouponRates, Period, Basis, EndMonthRule, Face);  Yield = Yield2; Price = ... stepcpnprice(Yield, Settle, Maturity, ConvDates, CouponRates, ... Period, Basis, EndMonthRule, Face)
Workaround: Attached is the fix for the problem -- note that this fix will only work in versions including and after 2009B.To workaround this issue, replace the versions of stepcpncfamounts.m, stepcpnprice.m, and stepcpnyield.m functions in your current installation with the updated version attached.1. Exit MATLAB?.Make a backup copy of the following files:matlabroot\toolbox\finfixed\finfixed\stepcpncfamounts.mmatlabroot\toolbox\finfixed\finfixed\stepcpnprice.mmatlabroot\toolbox\finfixed\finfixed\stepcpnyield.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing files, adding "old" to the file extension, stepcnpcfamounts.m.OLD, stepcpnprice.m.OLD, and stepcpnyield.m.OLD.2. Download the attached files and store them in the directory specified in the previous step.3. Restart MATLAB.4. Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Multiple warnings that "additional knots occur already to full multiplicity" when using fnmin.
Description: With some splines, fnmin  displays endless repetitions of the following warning: Warning: All additional knots occur already to full multiplicity.> In sprfn>sprfn1 at 84  In sprfn at 50  In fnrfn at 54  In fnzeros at 126  In fnmin at 58 Typically, this occurs when the spline has a discontinuous derivative.

Summary: Simulink does not function properly if the user does not have write permission to the temp directory.
Description: When the user does not have permission to write to the temp folder, simulink cannot function properly.  For example, calling 'open_system' results in a crash.  However, Simulink does not inform the user if it cannot write to the temp folder.

Summary: MWException "??? No appropriate method, property, or field ..." thrown from MATLAB code when using MWJavaObjectRef in conjunction with multiple component instances.
Description: This failure occurs when trying to pass Java objects by reference to deployed MATLAB code using MATLAB Builder JA and MWJavaObjectRef . For example, the following code fails: try {  Calculator c = new Calculator();  Map<String,String> m = new HashMap<String,String>();  m.put("key","value");  c.a_matlab_function(new MWJavaObjectRef(m)); // exception thrown here with message: no appropriate method, property or field  } catch (MWException e) {  e.printStackTrace();}
Workaround: To workaround this issue, place a suppressed call in the body of your function to the methods command on the input HashMap parameter via evalc:evalc('methods(input_hashmap)');This line of code should be inserted before the keySet property of the HashMap is accessed.

Summary: The Import Wizard does not import empty variables in MAT-files
Description: The Import Wizard does not recognize empty variables in MAT-files when you create the file without specifying the file extension. For example, if you run the following code, the Import Wizard does not detect the variable emptyVar : emptyVar=[]; save MyFile emptyVar -matuiimport('MyFile')
Workaround: Include the extension when saving the file. For example:emptyVar=[];save MyFile.mat emptyVaruiimport('MyFile.mat')

Summary: Using nontunable structure parameters with registered bus objects may cause assertion in Embedded MATLAB Function blocks
Description: You may get an assertion when you use nontunable structure parameters with registered bus objects in Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink. The assertion looks something like this: failed assertion in .\cdr\cdr_utils.cpp at line 2179: numEle > 0 Stack Trace:  [0] sf.mexw32:void __cdecl assertion_failed(char const *,char const *,int,char const *)(0x6b47b850 "numEle > 0", 0x6b47b5a4 ".\cdr\cdr_utils.cpp", 2179, 0) + 58 bytes  [1] sf.mexw32:class CG::TypeBuilder * __cdecl cdr_struct_type_builder(class CG_Ctx *,unsigned int,struct MemPool_struct *,struct SimStruct_tag *,int,class CG_Scope *)(0x3510a878, 106, 0x354e1078, 0x1ee7d8b0) + 110 bytes  [2] sf.mexw32:class CG_Type * __cdecl cdr_struct_create_from_sl_type(class CG_Ctx *,unsigned int,struct SimStruct_tag *,int,class CG_Scope *)(0x3510a878, 106, 0x1ee7d8b0, 14) + 51 bytes
Workaround: Set the type of the nontunable structure parameter to Inherit:Same as Simulink in the Ports and Data Manager of the Embedded MATLAB Function block.

Summary: The automatic stubber writes through a constant pointer when the pointer type is a structure.?
Description: If a function takes a const pointer as an argument, and the pointer type is a structure in which some fields are arrays, then the automatic stubber will ignore the const qualifier. Example: %%%CODE%%% struct _s2 {int a;int b;int c[8];};  typedef struct _s1 t_s1;typedef struct _s2 t_s2;  void f2(const t_s2 *p); // to be stubbed automatically  %%%CODE%%% The function f2 is pure, but the log says: * Function f2 may write to its arguments and may return random. Does not model pointer effects. Moreover, the const qualifier will also be ignored if the structure fields are written via a const pointer. Example: %%%CODE%%% struct _s1 {int a;int b;};  stru ct _s2 {int a;int b;int c[8];};  typedef struct _s1 t_s1;typedef struct _s2 t_s2;  t_s1 s1;t_s2 s2;  void f(void) {  const t_s1 *p1 = &s1;const t_s2 *p2 = &s2;  p1->a = 10; // PolySpace doesn't raise any error, while gcc says: test.c: error: assignment of read-only member `a'p2->c[1] = 20; // PolySpace doesn't raise any error, while gcc says: test.c: error: assignment of read-only location }  %%%CODE%%% ?

Summary: In PolySpace Viewer, back to model links fail to open Stateflow? models with the message: "Chart#-SP-#With#-SP-#Trailing#-SP-#Space#-SP-# command line cannot be executed"
Description: In PolySpace Viewer, back to model links fail with a pop-up message displaying many "#" characters.  This occurs most often with Stateflow? models.  In every case, the object in the model has a trailing space. ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a PolySpace Client:Browse to the folder: %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\ViewerCreate backup copies of the 11 jar files located in the .\bin sub-folderDownload the attached file: 550054_Viewer.zip.Extract the 11 jar files from the zip archive, and copy them to the folder:%POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Viewer\bin

Summary: Generated HDL code for Upsample block does not match Simulink results when Upsample factor is set to 2 and Sample offset is set to 1
Description: HDL code generated from a model that includes the Upsample block may not produce results identical to Simulink when the Upsample block has the following parameters settings: Upsample factor : 2 Sample offset : 1  You can observe the discrepancy by running the generated HDL testbench in any HDL simulator.
Workaround: To work around this problem, change the Sample offset parameter of the Upsample block from 1 to 0, and insert a Unit Delay block after the Upsample block.?

Summary: MATLAB? Coverage Report: 0% coverage erroneously reported for MATLAB class files
Description: Regardless of how much of a class MATLAB file ran when it was profiled, the Coverage Report indicates 0% of the file ran.
Workaround: Use the Profiler to see the coverage for an individual class file, as follows:Open the Profiler by entering the following in the Command Window: profile('viewer').Click the link for class file of interest.Scroll down to the section entitled Coverage results.

Summary: Cannot import exported AUTOSAR XML files using DaVinci tool suite
Description: If you generate XML files for models with arrays and try to import these files using the  DaVinci tool suite, you get the following error: #VImExARDataObjComSpec::ResolveBindings: :reference </rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/Ground/rt_Array_Double_10InitValue/rt_Array_Double_10InitValue> could not be resolved # VImExARDataObjComSpec::ResolveBindings: :reference </rtwdemo_autosar_counter_pkg/rtwdemo_autosar_counter_dt/Ground/rt_Array_Double_10InitValue/rt_Array_Double_10InitValue> could not be resolved
Workaround: Either, change the CONSTANT-SPECIFICATION in the generated <model>_datatype.arxml file from<CONSTANT-SPECIFICATION> <SHORT-NAME>rt_Array_Double_10InitValue</SHORT-NAME> <VALUE> <ARRAY-SPECIFICATION>  <SHORT-NAME>rt_Array_Double_10InitValue</SHORT-NAME>to<CONSTANT-SPECIFICATION> <SHORT-NAME>rt_Array_Double_10InitValue</SHORT-NAME> <VALUE> <ARRAY-SPECIFICATION> <SHORT-NAME>DoubleInitValue</SHORT-NAME>or, do the following:Download the attached file.Use this file to replace: matlab/toolbox/rtw/targets/AUTOSAR/AUTOSAR/@arxml/@exporter/private/p_export_component.pBuild your model to regenerate the data type XML file.

Summary: Starting MuPAD from MATLAB may fail to validate the Symbolic Math Toolbox license.
Description: When you start MuPAD using the MATLAB menu Start  > Toolboxes > Symbolic Math > MuPAD ,  you may see the following error even though a Symbolic Math Toolbox license is available: "There was an error validating your license in MUPAD. Please start MATLAB to activate your license."
Workaround: To work around the issue on win32 in R2009a and R2009b run the attached MATLAB function using the MATLAB that requires patching. The script will prompt for confirmation and then patch the required files. Restarting MATLAB should solve the issue.

Summary: Issuing the report command displays the generated report, regardless of the View report after generation setting
Description: When you use the report  command from the MATLAB command line, the report displays, even if you disable the View report after generation  option in the Report Format and Stylesheet dialog box.
Workaround: To ensure that the report does not display when you use the report command, use the '-noview' flag:report('myReport.rpt','-noview');

Summary: Verification of a model stops with a Java exception
Description: Verification of a model ends with the following Java exception: Language is not definedjava.lang.NullPointerExceptionat com.polyspace.iabc.ihml.IHML_Context.getKernelFile(Unknown Source)at com.polyspace.iabc.ihml.Root.loadSilent(Un  For example, after generating ert code from a Simulink model and launching PolySpace verification, the following error appears: ### PolySpace Technologies MATLAB integration### Version MBD-5.3.10 (R2009a)### Configuring Project ### PolySpace Technologies MATLAB integration### Version MBD-5.3.10 (R2009a)### Configuring Project ### PolySpace Technologies RTW Embedded Coder integration### Version MBD-5.3.10 (R2009a)### Preparing code verification ### PolySpace Technologies MATLAB integration### Version MBD-5.3.10 (R2009a)### Configuring Project### Creating results directory ### Analysing subsystem: my_subsystem### Locating generated source files: C:\file1.c ok C:\file2.c ok### Generating DRS table glob_var min max init ### Computing code verification optionsLanguage is not defined java.lang.NullPointerException  at com.polyspace.iabc.ihml.IHML_Context.getKernelFile(Unknown Source)  at com.polyspace.iabc.ihml.Root.loadSilent(Unknown Source)  at com.polyspace.iabc.ihml.Root.initSilent(Unknown Source)  at com.polyspace ? This error occurs due to a missing line in the configuration file (.cfg) used to launch the PolySpace verification. -lang=C
Workaround: To workaround this issue:Edit the configuration file (<model_name>.cfg)Add the following line at the beginning of the file:-lang=C

Summary: Error when generating code from model with SimMechanics blocks whose name begins with invalid character followed by number
Description: If a SimMechanics block name begins with an invalid identifier character followed by a number, then invalid code will be generated. The generated code fails to compile. An example of a block name that would cause this error is '+1Body'.
Workaround: Rename the SimMechanics block to have a name that begins with a valid identifier character.

Summary: MATLAB produces an error when an overloaded rand function is called.
Description: If a function rand.m is defined on the path, overriding MATLAB's built-in definition, calls of the form rand(1) may incorrectly produce an error.
Workaround: Execute the following command before calling the overloaded rand function:feature accel off;

Summary: Unnecessary name mangling of local variables that represent block outputs
Description: Prior to 2009b, local variables representing block outputs were given mangled names, even though the mangle suffix was not necessary to distinguish between two similarly named variables. For example, in rtwdemo_counter , the local variable, rtb_equal_to_count , derived from a block by the same name, does not need a mangle suffix. If a block by the same name was contained in an inner, inlined subsystem, then a mangle suffix would be needed.

Summary: Some scope blocks display signals incorrectly on 64-bit Mac? platforms
Description: In R2009b, there are conditions under which some Communications Blockset? and Signal Processing Blockset? scopes display signals incorrectly. The incorrect signal display only occurs when there is exactly one scope block in your Simulink? model and you are working on a 64-bit Mac platform. The following five blocks are affected: Discrete-Time Eye Diagram Scope - Communications Blockset Discrete-Time Scatter Plot Scope - Communications Blockset Discrete-Time Signal Trajectory Scope - Communications Blockset Spectrum Scope - Signal Processing Blockset Vector Scope - Signal Processing Blockset  When you simulate a model containing exactly one of the affected scopes, the scope figure displays a few frames and then stops updating. The scope updates when you move the mouse, but as soon as you stop moving the mouse, the scope stops updating.
Workaround: To workaround this issue in R2009b, you have the following options:Click on the scope figure window while the simulation is running. The scope then displays the correct signal for the remainder of the simulation.Add a second scope to your model. Both scopes will now behave as expected.?

Summary: cdfinfo errors when it encounters EPOCH16 datatype
Description: The CDF EPOCH16 datatype was introduced in CDF library release 3.0. The cdfinfo  function does not support this datatype and issues an error when trying to read a file containing an EPOCH16 variable.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, download these updated versions of MATLAB CDF functions and MEX-files. Note that the updated cdfinfo function will return information about a file containing an EPOCH16 variable without erroring out. The updated cdfinfo skips EPOCH16 values rather than issuing an error.Type matlabroot and mexext at the MATLAB prompt and take note of your MATLAB root installation folder and the MEX file extension on your platform.Quit MATLAB.Download the attached files appropriate for your platform into a temporary folder.Change folders into $matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/imagesci, where $matlabroot is the name returned by your call to the matlabroot function.Backup cdfinfo.m to cdfinfo.m.bak.Backup cdfread.m to cdfread.m.bak.Copy cdfinfo.m and cdfread.m from the temporary folder into the current folder.Change directories into private.Backup cdfinfoc.mex$mexext to cdfinfoc.mex$mexext.bak, where $mexext is the MEX file extension for your platform.Backup cdfreadc.mex$mexext to cdfreadc.mex$mext.bak.Copy cdfinfoc.mex$mexext and cdfreadc.mex$mexext from the temporary folder's private folder into the current folder.Restart MATLAB.Type rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB prompt.?

Summary: Simulink crashes when used interactively on HP computers using ProtectTools
Description: There are three known scenarios that can cause this crash: Scenario 1:  Double-click a Goto block in either the Library Browser or a Simulink editor window. Scenario 2:  1. Select Tools > Real-Time Workshop > Options  (or use Ctrl+H and browse to RTW Options). 2. Click the "Browse" button under Target Selection. 3. Select a target different from the one currently selected, then click OK . Scenario 3:   Open a model in Model Explorer, then select Configuration(Active)  in the tree view under the model node. Note:  There may be other scenarios that can cause this problem that have not yet been identified.
Workaround: To workaround this issue:Open the HP ProtectTools application. Browse to Credential Manager Options, then select the Single Sign On tab. Disable the check box "Enable Single Sign On functionality."

Summary: (Windows 32-bit Version platforms) Displaying more than 16 blocks in the Library Browser causes Windows timers to fail silently.
Description: The Library Browser sets up a timer for each block displayed in the browser window. Displaying more than 16 blocks causes calls to Windows timer functions (such as timeSetEvent ) to fail silently.
Workaround: Display fewer than 16 blocks at a time in the Library Browser.Note that closing the Library Browser does not prevent this problem.

Summary: imread produces unexpected results with floating-point LAB TIFF images
Description: imread  produces unexpected results when given a CIE L*a*b* image with an invalid BitsPerSample  value. imread  converts CIE L*a*b* data into ICC L*a*b* by applying a shift of 128. The TIFF specification only allows for 8-bit and 16-bit CIE L*a*b* images. Any other value for BitsPerSample  is invalid.

Summary: Maci64 platform does not support rubberband box cursor action for selecting multiple items in Model Explorer list view
Description: On Macintosh 64 (Maci64) platforms, you cannot use a rubberband box to select multiple items in Model Explorer list view.
Workaround: Use Shift+click or Control+click to select multiple items in the list view.

Summary: Corrupt polygon topology in usastatelo.shp
Description: When certain polygons in the usastatelo.shp  shapefile data appear near the edges of a map as displayed with geoshow , some areas that should be filled appear unfilled, and vice versa. This is due to subtle defects in the topology of the polygons for certain states.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the data files usastatelo.shp and usastatelo.shx in your current installation with the updated versions in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make backup copies of the following files:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdemos/usastatelo.shpmatlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdemos/usastatelo.shxwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing version of usastatelo.shp, adding "old" to the file extension: usastatelo.shp.oldCopy the versions of usastatelo.shp and usastatelo.shx from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.As of June 4, 2010, the versions of usastatelo.shp and usastatelo.shx in the attachment have been updated to correct the problem with the Maryland polygon described in Bug Report 637196.

Summary: When using a Parallel RLC Branch or Series RLC Branch block with the Capacitance and Inductance parameters set to zero, the Print report still indicates both a capacitance of 1e-6 and and inductance of 1e-3
Description: If you create a model with a Series RLC branch or Parallel RLC branch block set to be only a resistor, choose File ->Print Details , select the Entire Model  radio button then click Print , the resulting report will show nonzero capacitance and inductance associated with that RLC element. Note that these values are not actually used by the model, they are just shown in the report.

Summary: PolySpace installation hangs during the "Refresh environment" phase
Description: When installing PolySpace software, the installer hangs during the phase "Refresh environment" (beginning of the installation). There are two potential causes for this problem: Instability of the environment after file installation. The use of UAC settings under Windows VISTA prevent elevation by programs that are not in 'Program Files' (The default folder for PolySpace is C:\PolySpace ).
Workaround: For the first cause, rebooting your machine may solve the problem.For the second cause, a reboot does not help. The only solution is to update your UAC settings to allow installation in the desired folder.

Summary: Stateflow does not always produce an error for invalid data type strings
Description: When a chart data type string is invalid, Stateflow might silently resolve the string to the wrong type, which results in incorrect generated code.

Summary: Model with Bit Clear block fails to update after looking under mask
Description: Using the Bit Clear block with a vector value for the Index of bit  parameter leads to a model that fails to update or simulate after looking under the mask of the Bit Clear block.

Summary: When it closes, uigetdir can change focus to a previously clicked window
Description: When function uigetdir  (a GUI for selecting directories) returns, the active window can lose focus and a window clicked previously can obtain focus. You can observe this issue in Simulink when a model callback calls uigetdir . When the user closes the directory chooser, some other window that was previously active can obtain focus. That window can be a window unrelated to MATLAB and Simulink, such as a browser.

Summary: Data with nonprinting characters in row or column headers causes Import Wizard to throw Java exceptions and garble output
Description: When importing data from text file that starts with a nonprinting character causes the Import Wizard to throws Java exceptions and produce unreadable output. This issue can potentially arise when a nonprinting character occurs anywhere in text data that the Import Wizard processes.
Workaround: Open the input text file in a text editor, delete nonprinting characters, save as a text file, and import the saved file. Some text editors do such filtering automatically.

Summary: Segv error when using MS Visual C++ compilers to generate C code for the MPC Controller block
Description: When you use Microsoft Visual C++ compilers, such as 2003, 2005 and 2007 in RTW, to generate C code for the MPC Controller block, segv error occurs if the Inline parameters  checkbox in the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is selected. When the Inline parameters  checkbox is selected, S-function parameters are treated as read-only variables but the MPC Controller block tries to write to it which results in a segv error.
Workaround: Uncheck the Inline parameters checkbox in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: The Model Explorer does not preserve the order of columns displayed between MATLAB sessions
Description: Model Explorer column ordering is not saved. When you start Model Explorer in a new MATLAB session, the column display order may differ from what it was in the previous session.

Summary: When creating many figures (> 40), MATLAB can generate a Segmentation Violation error. This error requires the user to exit and restart.
Description: When creating many figures, MATLAB runs low on memory and encounters a software bug that can cause a Segmentation Violation.

Summary: Meridian label may be incorrectly rotated by 180 degrees in map axes
Description: In certain map axes configurations, it's possible for a meridian (or parallel) label near the edge of the map frame to be incorrectly rotated through an angle of 180 degrees.
Workaround: In many cases you can work around this problem by turning off the LabelRotation property in your map axes. For example, if the current axes is a map axes, use the command: setm(gca,'LabelRotation','off').Alternatively, you can replace the rotatetext.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/rotatetext.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: rotatetext.m.oldCopy the version of rotatetext.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: MATLAB scripts started via the -r command line option cannot be interrupted by pressing CTRL-C.
Description: When running a MATLAB script from the MATLAB command line: matlab -r script_name you cannot terminate the script by pressing CTRL-C from the keyboard. ?
Workaround: In order to terminate the script, you need to terminate MATLAB by selecting the Exit MATLAB command from the File menu.

Summary: Why do I get an error when I run optimization solvers in R2008a with a problem structure that was exported from the Optimization Tool GUI and saved to a MAT file in R2008b?
Description: If any field in the Optimization Tool GUI contains an anonymous function, then the Optimization Tool fails to create a valid problem structure when you export using the File > Export to Workspace  command. MATLAB errors if you try to use the exported structure. For example, if your objective funtion is the anonymous function @(x)testobj(x) , then the exported problem structure is, in part, optimproblem =  objective: @(x)testobj(x)  x0: [1 2] ...  Running fmincon(optimproblem),  for example, gives the following error: ??? Undefined function handle. Error in ==> fmincon at 517 initVals.f = funfcn{3}(X,varargin{:}); Caused by: Failure in initial user-supplied objective function evaluation. FMINCON cannot continue.
Workaround: After exporting the problem structure to MATLAB workspace in R2008b, manually recreate all anonymous functions before saving the problem structure to a MAT file. In the example above, you manually modify the field 'objective' at the command line as follows:optimproblem.objective = @(x)testobj(x)optimproblem =objective: @(x)testobj(x)x0: [1 2]...Alternatively, you can use the File > Generate M-File command to export your work to MATLAB, instead of File > Export to Workspace.

Summary: Why do I get an error when I run optimization solvers with a problem structure that was exported from the Optimization Tool GUI and saved to a MAT file?
Description: If any field in the Optimization Tool GUI contains an anonymous function, the Optimization Tool fails to create a valid problem structure when you export using the File > Export to Workspace  command. MATLAB errors if you try to use the exported structure. For example, if your objective funtion is the anonymous function @(x)testobj(x) , then the exported problem structure is, in part, optimproblem =  objective: @(x)testobj(x)  x0: [1 2] ...  Running fmincon(optimproblem),  for example, gives the following error: ??? Undefined function handle. Error in ==> fmincon at 517 initVals.f = funfcn{3}(X,varargin{:}); Caused by: Failure in initial user-supplied objective function evaluation. FMINCON cannot continue.

Summary: Third-party libraries that depend on network proxies are unable to access network-hosted artifacts
Description: Third-party libraries can depend on network hosted artifacts. For example, Xerces and WSDL4j depend on network artifacts for their schema and WSDL-related definitions. Changes in R2009a that allow Axis-based Web services to use proxy settings in the Web preferences panel cause the default proxy settings to be incompatible with third-party libraries.
Workaround: To work around this issue in most cases, set system properties following this guide: http://java.sun.com/javase/6/docs/technotes/guides/net/proxies.html.However, note that this might disable MATLAB internal capabilities for certain functionality, including Help > Check for Updates. Therefore, set these system properties only when the required third-party library is needed and the MATLAB functionality is not needed (that is, when they are mutually exclusive).

Summary: svd with one output argument has performance regression.
Description: The performance of svd  with one output argument is reverted back to R2006b level. The performance of R2007a and R2007b is noticeably faster but can fail to converge on rare occasions. The performance regression is particularly noticeable for matrices of dimensions less than 200-by-200.

Summary: The function isequal with a cell array input may return an incorrect answer if the cell array contains an object that has an overloaded isequal method.
Description: If objects A  and B   have overloaded isequal  methods such that isequal(A,B)  returns true , isequal({A},{B})  may return false .

Summary: Under certain conditions, existing breakpoints fail to display in MATLAB Editor.
Description: If you create a breakpoint in the MATLAB Editor, but clear and restore it programatically, the breakpoint icon is not restored.
Workaround: Close and re-open the editor to display breakpoints correctly.

Summary: Linux? platform: Printing text to Postscript? printer produces blank pages
Description: On Linux? platforms, printing text from the Editor or Command Window to a Postscript? printer may produce blank pages. This is due to a bug in how Linux software converts Postscript? data into PCL (Printer Control Language).
Workaround: There are two workarounds. One is to use a PostScript printer so that there is no translation of the Postscript data. If that is not possible, follow these steps to print the content:Select File > Print > Print to File, and then click Print to create a file.Open the newly created file in a text editor.Search for the string DeferredMediaSelection, and then delete the line that contains it. For example, delete the entire line that appears similar to: <</DeferredMediaSelection true /PageSize [612.0 792.0] /ImagingBBox null /ManualFeed false /NumCopies 1 >> setpagedevice.Save the file, and then print it using a Linux? printing program (for example, KGhostView if you're using KDE).

Summary: Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms: Command+Tab does not switch to MATLAB? window correctly
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms, Expos¨¦ features such as Command +Tab  do not switch to the MATLAB? window correctly; the window does not become active.
Workaround: To make the MATLAB window active, click inside the window or on its title bar.

Summary: for-loops using a fi object counter variable produce wrong answers
Description: When a fi  object is used as the counter variable in a for -loop, the loop does not execute correctly. The output of the for -loop is invalid, but no warning or error message appears. For example, when you run the following code, you would expect MATLAB? to iterate through the loop 3 times and return k=1 , k=2 , k=3 :   counter = fi(1:3);  for k = counter     k  end Instead, MATLAB executes the loop only once, and incorrectly returns the full fi  object vector that you defined in the variable counter : k =     1     2     3          DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scaling                Signed: true            WordLength: 16        FractionLength: 13
Workaround: To work around this issue, cast the for-loop counter variable from a fi object to a built-in double:  counter = double(fi(1:3));  for k = counter     k  endWhen you run this code, MATLAB correctly returns the following output:k =     1k =     2k =     3

Summary: Cannot restore default font on some machines
Description: In the Simulink Preferences you can specify a font name for blocks, lines and annotations in new models. On some machines the default "Helvetica" font is not listed in the Simulink Preferences, so you cannot restore the default behavior after changing it. There are two lists of fonts in Simulink. The first is in the Simulink Preferences, where you set the font for new  blocks, lines and annotations in new  block diagrams. The second is in the Simulink Editor (accessed via the "Font..." item on the Simulink "Format" menu or context menus) where you can set the font for an existing block, line or annotation. On some machines, these lists are not exactly the same. In particular, on some Windows and Linux machines, the "Helvetica" font is available in the Simulink Editor but not in the Preferences. This makes it impossible to restore the default font after changing it.
Workaround: If the required font is not listed in the Simulink Preferences, you can assign it from the MATLAB command-line as shown in the following examples.To change the default font which will be used for new blocks in new block diagrams:%%%BEGIN CODE%%%set_param( 0, 'DefaultBlockFontName', 'Helvetica');set_param( 0, 'DefaultBlockFontSize', 10);set_param( 0, 'DefaultBlockFontWeight', 'normal');set_param( 0, 'DefaultBlockFontAngle', 'normal');%%%END CODE%%%For lines and annotations, replace "Block" with "Line" or "Annotation" in the above commands, e.g.%%%BEGIN CODE%%%set_param( 0, 'DefaultLineFontName', 'Helvetica');set_param( 0, 'DefaultAnnotationFontName', 'Helvetica');%%%END CODE%%%To change the font which is used for new blocks/lines/annotations in an existing block diagram, specify the name of that block diagram instead of "0" in the above commands, e.g.%%%BEGIN CODE%%%set_param( 'mymodel', 'DefaultBlockFontName', 'Helvetica');%%%END CODE%%%

Summary: Printing fails when IPP printers using CUPS are present on Linux or Macintosh? systems
Description: In a Linux or Apple? Macintosh configuration where IPP (Internet Printing Protocol) printers using CUPS are present, printing from the Editor, Command Window, or Figure window results in the following exception: java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException at ice.util.io.LazyCachedInputStream.read(OEAB) at ice.util.io.MarkerInputStream.read(OEAB) at java.io.DataInputStream.read(Unknown Source) at sun.print.IPPPrintService.readIPPResponse(Unknown Source) at sun.print.IPPPrintService.opGetAttributes(Unknown Source) at sun.print.IPPPrintService.initAttributes(Unknown Source) at sun.print.IPPPrintService.getSupportedDocFlavors(Unknown Source) at sun.print.IPPPrintService.isDocFlavorSupported(Unknown Source) at sun.print.RasterPrinterJob.getPrintService(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.widgets.text.print.PrintSettings.getLastPrintRequestAttributes(PrintSettings.java:259) at com.mathworks.widgets.SyntaxTextPaneBase.printDocument(SyntaxTextPaneB ase.java:2017) This is due to a known bug in Sun Microsystems? Java? software. Sun Microsystems has issued a report on this bug (6227867).
Workaround: This problem has been fixed for:Linux platforms for MATLAB 7.5 (R2007b) as part of the new Sun Microsystems JVM? versionThe Apple? Macintosh Java Runtime Environment (JRE) 1.6To get Apple? Macintosh JRE 1.6, do one of the following:If you are running 32-bit MATLAB Version 7.8 (R2009a) or later, upgrade your Apple operating system to Mac OS X 10.6 Snow Leopard.If you are running 64-bit MATLAB Version 7.9 (R2009b), download the latest Java update for Mac OS X Leopard from the Apple Web site.In addition, a?customer reported that the following workaround addresses the problem:Get?the IP address and?add an LPD (Line Printer Daemon) printer using the?IP.

Summary: Background of printed or exported figures is black instead of transparent with OpenGL in Windows XP or Vista.
Description: In Windows XP or Vista, printed or exported figures that are rendered with OpenGL can have a black background, even though you request a transparent background. Background color is controlled by the "Figure background color" preference setting on the Copy Options  panel of the Figure Copy Template  preferences, but sometimes the Transparent background  option is not respected. ?
Workaround: One workaround is to try adjusting the Hardware Acceleration display setting. If you are running Windows Vista:Open the Display Settings dialog by right-clicking a blank area of the desktop; select Personalize, and then click on Display Settings.Open the Monitor tab and click the Advanced Settings buttonOpen the Troubleshooting tab and set Hardware Acceleration to Basic.Click the OK button and then close the Display Settings dialogRestart the computer if requested to do so.To adjust the Hardware Acceleration display setting in Windows XP:Open the Display Settings dialog by right-clicking a blank area of the desktop; select Properties.Click on the Settings tab and click the Advanced buttonOpen the Troubleshooting tab and adjust the Hardware Acceleration.Click the OK button and then close the Display Settings dialogRestart the computer if requested to do so.The following procedure might also work in some cases. Instead of using the Aero color scheme, use a different one, such as Windows Vista Basic:Click the Start button and open the Control Panel.Click PersonalizationClick Window Color and Appearance. You may need to click Open classic appearance properties at the bottom of the page if the Appearance Settings dialog box does not appear.Choose another color scheme, such as Windows Vista Basic in the Color scheme list.Click OK.

Summary: Function geoshow improperly projects vectors onto a 'globe' map axes
Description: For a map axes having a MapProjection  property of 'globe' , function geoshow  does not work properly with vector input ('globe'  is a rotatable 3-D display, not a true map projection). When given points, lines, or polygons, either as pairs of latitude-longitude vectors or within a geostruct array, geoshow  projects all the vertices onto the plane of the Equator, so that they fall within the Earth's interior instead of on its surface.
Workaround: For point or line latitude-longitude vectors, you can use plotm or linem. For polygon vectors, you can try using patchm or patchesm, although any holes in polygons (outlined by inner, counter-clockwise rings) will be filled with color rather than empty.

Summary: Computer Modern fonts are incorrect on Solaris
Description: The following integral symbol appears as an "R" on solaris:  text('string','$\int$','interp','latex');
Workaround: If the Computer Modern fonts do not load on Solaris, you must manually install the fonts using the Solaris Font Administrator.? Do the following as root on Solaris:Right Click on Desktop->Applications->Application Manager->System Admin->Font Administrator.In the Solaris Font Administrator, select the Fonts|Install menu item.Choose the "List Fonts From" directory as matlabroot/sys/fonts/type1/cm. This is where MATLAB stores the Computer Modern font familyChoose the "Install To" directory as /usr/openwin/lib/X11/fonts/Type1.Perform the installation.
